source.subject: Final security handoff & timeline — SOC 2, SIG/CAIQ, DPA retention windows
messages:
From: Derek Shaw <derek.shaw@clearnet.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 8 Nov 2026 10:12:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Security scope and timelines — next steps

Hi Irene,

Thanks again for the demo last week. Procurement (cc'd Nicole) asked me to consolidate the security handoff so we can map to our internal SSO/contract timeline. Two quick asks from our side: 

1) Can you confirm the expected SOC 2 evidence window for an initial private deployment? (we're targeting Dec 1 go-live)
2) Where does Redwood stand on SIG/CAIQ completeness vs the CAIQ spreadsheet — do you need more time to populate?

I've attached our procurement_sheet_clearnet.xlsx with requested SLAs, retention thresholds, and the preferred DPA template. If you can share a short mutual action plan (MAP) that ties the security gates to milestones (contract, DPA sign, evidence delivery), that will help speed internal approvals.

Appreciate it — happy to hop on a quick call if easier.

Best,
Derek
Head of Procurement, ClearNet

Attachments: procurement_sheet_clearnet.xlsx (Excel)

From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>
To: Derek Shaw <derek.shaw@clearnet.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 8 Nov 2026 12:05:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Security scope and timelines — next steps

Hi Derek —

Thanks for the procurement sheet; that's helpful. We can absolutely put together a concise MAP that maps the compliance gates to dates and deliverables. To answer your two bullets up front: 

- SOC 2: we can provide SOC 2 Type II summary artifacts and our audit report excerpt within 7–10 business days, and full evidence bundles (control narratives, screenshots) on a 3-week cadence thereafter. For a Dec 1 private deployment we recommend an acceptance window that allows one week after contract sign to validate controls in staging.
- SIG/CAIQ: we have a CAIQ draft that covers core controls; Marcus will own the final pass. We typically deliver a CAIQ XLSX with numbered responses and references to our SOC 2 docs. Expect a completed draft within 10 business days.

Marcus / Priya / Laura — can you confirm the realistic dates below so I can fold into a MAP for Derek? I suggest: 

- DPA redline received: Nov 9
- DPA final (DocuSign): Nov 18
- SOC2 summary + CAIQ draft: Nov 18
- Evidence handoff (staging): Nov 25–Nov 30
- Production enablement target: Dec 1 (contingent on evidence verification)

Derek — we'll attach the MAP + evidence checklist in my next message (PDF + Drive link). If ClearNet needs a longer retention carve-out in the DPA we can escalate to legal during redlines.

Thanks,
Irene
Manager, Customer Security Programs
Redwood Inference

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>, Derek Shaw <derek.shaw@clearnet.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 9 Nov 2026 09:18:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Security scope and timelines — SOC 2 / CAIQ plan

Thanks Irene — quick updates from the controls/evidence side: 

- SOC 2: I attached SOC2_summary_v1.pdf with a short list of the control families we will surface for evidence (Access, Change Mgmt, Monitoring, Data Protection). That file lists the snapshot artifacts (logs, runbooks, policy excerpts).
- CAIQ: I've started the CAIQ_responses_draft.xlsx and marked all 'TBD' rows for items that require legal or engineering notes. Expect a full pass by Nov 18.

A couple of clarifications for ClearNet so we align acceptance criteria in the MAP: 

1) Evidence format: we deliver evidence as links to a secured Drive folder plus exported PDFs for sensitive artifacts.
2) Data retention: by default Redwood retains logs for 90 days; application-level customer data retention is configurable per deployment. If ClearNet requires a longer retention period (e.g., 1 year for audit), we need to capture that as a DPA addendum.

Action items for ClearNet: please confirm if 90 days log retention is acceptable or if you require a retention appendix.

Thanks — Marcus
Sr. Security Engineer, Redwood Inference

Attachments: SOC2_summary_v1.pdf (PDF), CAIQ_responses_draft.xlsx (Excel)

From: Derek Shaw <derek.shaw@clearnet.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 9 Nov 2026 11:02:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Security scope and timelines — SOC 2 / CAIQ plan

On Mon, Nov 9, 2026 at 09:18, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com> wrote:
> SOC 2: I attached SOC2_summary_v1.pdf with a short list of the control families we will surface for evidence (Access, Change Mgmt, Monitoring, Data Protection). That file lists the snapshot artifacts (logs, runbooks, policy excerpts).
> 
Thanks Marcus — this is helpful. For the retention question: ClearNet needs logs retained for 12 months for our internal audit. We can accept shorter application-level retention if the DPA enumerates export and retrieval guarantees.

Two follow-ups: 
- Can you confirm whether the SOC 2 evidence bundle will include sample audit logs or only aggregated metrics? We need at least sample logs for a subset of controls to validate monitoring.
- For CAIQ, we prefer that you populate the CAIQ responses with cross-references to SOC 2 sections where applicable (helps our audit team).

I'll plan to finalize internal sign-off on Nov 17 if we have the DPA redline this week.

Best,
Derek

Attachments: procurement_sheet_clearnet.xlsx (Excel)

From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>
To: Derek Shaw <derek.shaw@clearnet.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Nov 2026 16:03:00 -08:00
Subject: Final MAP & milestone commitments — SOC2 / CAIQ / DPA retention appendix

Derek — attached is the MAP (also shared to the Drive link above) and a short evidence checklist. Highlights and agreed commitments: 

- DPA redline receipt: Nov 9 (received) — Redwood legal to respond with redlines by Nov 12.
- DPA final (DocuSign): target Nov 18. Link: https://app.docusign.com/link/ABC12345
- SOC 2 summary + CAIQ completed draft: Nov 18. (We will include sample audit logs for monitoring and alerting controls; see SOC2_summary_v1.pdf section 3.)
- Evidence handoff to ClearNet staging: Nov 25. Evidence bundle will be delivered as: exported PDFs + limited-time Drive links.
- Retention appendix: Redwood default logs = 90 days. ClearNet requested 12 months; Redwood will add a retention appendix to the DPA to support 12-month log retention for audit exports only (no change to platform default without a paid retention addon).

Mutual Action Plan (brief):
- Nov 9: DPA redline received (ClearNet) — COMPLETED
- Nov 12: Redwood legal redline returned — OWNER: Laura Bennett
- Nov 17: ClearNet internal sign-off (procurement & security) — OWNER: Derek Shaw
- Nov 18: DocuSign complete; SOC2 summary + CAIQ draft delivered — OWNER: Irene Choi / Marcus Lin
- Nov 25: Evidence handoff to staging — OWNER: Marcus Lin
- Dec 1: Production enablement (contingent on evidence verification) — OWNER: Product Ops / ClearNet runbook approval

Next steps: Laura will circulate the DPA redline by EOD Nov 12. Marcus will finish the CAIQ with cross-references by Nov 18 and include sample logs in the evidence bundle. Priya, please prepare the short runbook snippets we'll need for staging verification.

If this all looks good I will create a single shared MAP PDF and upload it to the Drive folder for signatures and tracking. If ClearNet needs expanded retention in-platform (not just export for audits) we should discuss pricing and capacity planning — happy to include that as an addendum.

Thanks everyone —
Irene Choi
Manager, Customer Security Programs
Redwood Inference

Attachments: MAP_clearNet_v1.pdf (PDF), evidence_checklist_clearNet.txt (TXT)

source.subject: CloudSmith pilot: subsidy clearance & activation plan
messages:
From: Alyssa Moore <alyssa@cloudsmith.io>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>
Cc: CloudSmith Partnerships <partnership@cloudsmith.io>
Date: Mon, 9 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Partner-sourced credits for CloudSmith x LaunchPad pilot

Hi Connor,

Thanks again for the demo last week — the team at CloudSmith is excited about the integration with Redwood. We're targeting an SI-led pilot with LaunchPad (their cohort starts in early December) and we'd like to cover promotional credits on our side to accelerate adoption.

High-level ask:
- $10k in partner-sourced credits (to be distributed as $5k test + $5k extension) for a 6-week pilot
- Public-facing co-marketing mention and one intro to LaunchPad ops team

Can you share the steps Redwoods needs to clear these credits? We can sign an MSP referral addendum (attached) and route the DocuSign. Time is tight — LaunchPad wants kickoff the week of Dec 7.

Attachments: CloudSmith_MSP_Referral_Agreement.pdf (pdf)

Thanks,
Alyssa Moore
Partnerships Manager | CloudSmith Inc
alyssa@cloudsmith.io | +1 415-555-0198
From: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>
To: Alyssa Moore <alyssa@cloudsmith.io>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 9 Nov 2026 11:02:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Partner-sourced credits for CloudSmith x LaunchPad pilot

Alyssa —

Appreciate the clear ask. We can usually approve partner-sourced pilot credits under our Marketplace Partner subsidy program but need a couple things before I can finalize:

1) Official SOW or pilot scope (who's running infra, expected token usage profile)
2) Signed referral/addendum (your PDF is fine)
3) Proposed activation window and canary rules (how you want the credits consumed — e.g., pinned to pilot project id, expiration date)

I've attached a short pilot-credit-proposal template to capture the token burn assumptions — fill that and return and I'll route for approval. Vanessa / Priya I'm looping you for approvals and any billing carve-outs or escrow you recommend.

Connor O'Brien
Enterprise Partnerships | Redwood Inference
connor@redwood.com | +1 650-555-0102

Attachments: pilot-credit-proposal.xlsx (xlsx)
From: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa@redwood.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Cc: Mina Haddad <mina@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 9 Nov 2026 17:30:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Partner-sourced credits for CloudSmith x LaunchPad pilot

Connor / Priya —

Quick ops note after reviewing Alyssa's agreement: we can approve the $10k subsidy but will require the following gating items for release:

- Executed MSP referral addendum (legal will countersign)
- Pilot project created and tagged in our billing console (we will allocate credits to project ID)
- Expiration: credits must be used within 90 days of activation
- Escrow/rollback: if usage exceeds profile by >50% without prior sign-off, billing will default to customer and we will notify partner for reconciliation

Operational plan (recommended):
- Day 0: DocuSign executed (partner sends envelope)
- Day 1–2: Redwood creates pilot project + issues a numbered credit token pool (CS-4512)
- Week 1–6: Monitor token spend; automatic alert at 50% and 85% thresholds
- End of Week 6: review and decide on extension (pullback or additional $5k)

I can approve the subsidy once Legal confirms the countersign. Priya — can you queue the legal review and confirm the billing tag process with Finance? Mina — please add the monitoring rules to the activation checklist and confirm alerts.

Vanessa Ortiz
Head of Channel Programs | Redwood Inference
vanessa@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0115

Attachments: CloudSmith_MSP_Referral_Agreement.pdf (pdf)
From: Alyssa Moore <alyssa@cloudsmith.io>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>
Cc: CloudSmith Partnerships <partnership@cloudsmith.io>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Nov 2026 15:45:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Partner-sourced credits for CloudSmith x LaunchPad pilot

Thanks — this is great. We routed the agreement through DocuSign and you should receive envelope CS-ENV-8892; Raj (LaunchPad ops) confirmed the cohort start window of Dec 7–13 and is happy with a 6-week pilot.

preview:
From: Alyssa Moore <alyssa@cloudsmith.io>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>
Cc: CloudSmith Partnerships <partnership@cloudsmith.io>
Date: Mon, 9 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Partner-sourced credits for CloudSmith x LaunchPad pilot

Hi Connor,

Thanks again for the demo last week — the team at CloudSmith is excited about the integration with Redwood. We're targeting an SI-led pilot with LaunchPad (their cohort starts in early December) and we'd like to cover promotional credits on our side to accelerate adoption.

High-level ask:
- $10k in partner-sourced credits (to be distributed as $5k test + $5k extension) for a 6-week pilot
- Public-facing co-marketing mention and one intro to LaunchPad ops team

Can you share the steps Redwoods needs to clear these credits? We can sign an MSP referral addendum (attached) and route the DocuSign. Time is tight — LaunchPad wants kickoff the week of Dec 7.

Attachments: CloudSmith_MSP_Referral_Agreement.pdf (pdf)

Thanks,
Alyssa Moore
Partnerships Manager | CloudSmith Inc
alyssa@cloudsmith.io | +1 415-555-0198


From: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>
To: Alyssa Moore <alyssa@cloudsmith.io>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 9 Nov 2026 11:02:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Partner-sourced credits for CloudSmith x LaunchPad pilot

Alyssa —

Appreciate the clear ask. We can usually approve partner-sourced pilot credits under our Marketplace Partner subsidy program but need a couple things before I can finalize:

1) Official SOW or pilot scope (who's running infra, expected token usage profile)
2) Signed referral/addendum (your PDF is fine)
3) Proposed activation window and canary rules (how you want the credits consumed — e.g., pinned to pilot project id, expiration date)

I've attached a short pilot-credit-proposal template to capture the token burn assumptions — fill that and return and I'll route for approval. Vanessa / Priya I'm looping you for approvals and any billing carve-outs or escrow you recommend.

Connor O'Brien
Enterprise Partnerships | Redwood Inference
connor@redwood.com | +1 650-555-0102

Attachments: pilot-credit-proposal.xlsx (xlsx)


From: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa@redwood.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Cc: Mina Haddad <mina@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 9 Nov 2026 17:30:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Partner-sourced credits for CloudSmith x LaunchPad pilot

Connor / Priya —

Quick ops note after reviewing Alyssa's agreement: we can approve the $10k subsidy but will require the following gating items for release:

- Executed MSP referral addendum (legal will countersign)
- Pilot project created and tagged in our billing console (we will allocate credits to project ID)
- Expiration: credits must be used within 90 days of activation
- Escrow/rollback: if usage exceeds profile by >50% without prior sign-off, billing will default to customer and we will notify partner for reconciliation

Operational plan (recommended):
- Day 0: DocuSign executed (partner sends envelope)
- Day 1–2: Redwood creates pilot project + issues a numbered credit token pool (CS-4512)
- Week 1–6: Monitor token spend; automatic alert at 50% and 85% thresholds
- End of Week 6: review and decide on extension (pullback or additional $5k)

I can approve the subsidy once Legal confirms the countersign. Priya — can you queue the legal review and confirm the billing tag process with Finance? Mina — please add the monitoring rules to the activation checklist and confirm alerts.

Vanessa Ortiz
Head of Channel Programs | Redwood Inference
vanessa@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0115

Attachments: CloudSmith_MSP_Referral_Agreement.pdf (pdf)

source.subject: Trial ownership playbook & infra coordination - next steps
messages:
From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah_schmitt@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@atlasretail.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-08T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: Trial ownership playbook & infra coordination - next steps

Thanks for a productive discovery earlier, Elena — good to meet the Atlas team. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we have a clear path into the trial: 

Recap / Success criteria (draft)
- Integrations: Redwood SDK ingest + embedding endpoint validated on a representative workload (<= 120ms median token latency).
- Quality: top-3 business intent accuracy >= 88% on the provided eval set (we'll share the prompt suite).
- Cost: Production TCO estimate within the band we discussed (target: < $0.06/token S1 equivalent).

Proposed owners (we need confirmation or alternate contacts):
- Infra (cluster, region, network): Monica Patel
- App / SDK + validation: Priya Natarajan
- Security & compliance liaison: TBD (Atlas to confirm security contact)
- Procurement / PO & contracting: Atlas procurement contact (CC'd if available)

Timeline (proposal)
- Week 0 (Nov 15): Kickoff + environment checklist
- Week 1-2: Integration, smoke tests, data wiring
- Week 3: Eval run and quality readout
- Week 4: PO/contract finalization and production plan

Attachments: trial_success_criteria_v1.xlsx, infra_responsibility_matrix.csv (first-pass).

Asks
1) Atlas: confirm security contact and data residency requirement (US / EU).
2) Internal: Karthik / Priya / Monica — please review the infra matrix and comment by EOD Monday so we can lock owners.
3) Everyone: confirm availability for a 60-minute kickoff on Nov 15 (proposed 9am PT).

Thanks — Hannah

--
Hannah Schmitt
Enterprise Sales 
Redwood Inference
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah_schmitt@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.ai>, Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@atlasretail.com>
Date: 2026-11-08T10:05:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Trial ownership playbook & infra coordination - next steps

Thanks for the clear recap, Hannah. I made a quick pass on the infra matrix and sketched a lightweight RACI so we have something to sign off on before the kickoff. Attached: proposed_raci.csv

My notes (short):
- Infra: Monica is best placed to own cluster provisioning, network/VPC peering, and monitoring hooks. Monica -> Owner, Karthik -> Support (runbook + SLO targets).
- App: Priya will own SDK integration, test corpus, and the quality evaluation harness. Priya -> Owner, Hannah -> Account Exec / stakeholder comms.
- Security: Recommend a split. Priya handles app-level controls (secrets, token handling). Monica + our security engineering (cc'd on follow-up) handle infra-level audits (audit logs, KMS).
- Procurement: Monica to coordinate internal cost estimates; Hannah to coordinate with Atlas procurement for PO timelines.

Proposed short checklist pre-kickoff:
- Monica: VPC/subnet plan + access method (bastion or SSO).
- Priya: sample payloads + eval prompts (we'll use these for the quality gate).
- Hannah: DocuSign PO template + list of procurement contacts.

If this looks good I can drop these into the Drive trial folder and flag the kickoff invite.

On Mon, Nov 8, 2026 at 09:12 AM Hannah Schmitt <hannah_schmitt@redwood.ai> wrote:
> [previous message omitted]

Thanks,
Karthik
From: Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@atlasretail.com>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah_schmitt@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-09T08:30:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Trial ownership playbook & infra coordination - next steps

Appreciate the recap, Hannah. Confirming Atlas folks and a couple notes: 
- Security point of contact: Marco Bianchi (copied on this thread) will manage our side of the security and data handling assessment.
- Data residency: primary dataset must remain in US region for this pilot — we cannot use EU at this stage.
- Procurement: I will introduce you to our procurement lead (PO expected within 3 weeks after kickoff).

We agree with the suggested owners. One request: can Redwood provide an example audit log snippet and KMS integration checklist prior to the kickoff so Marco's team can pre-validate?

Thanks,
Elena
Atlas Retail
From: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.ai>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah_schmitt@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.ai>, Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@atlasretail.com>
Date: 2026-11-09T11:15:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Trial ownership playbook & infra coordination - next steps

Thanks, Elena — welcome Marco. Quick clarifications from my side: 
- App-level security (token lifecycle, input sanitization, SDK secrets) = owned by Priya (Redwood).
- Infra-level security (KMS, network ACLs, audit logs) = Monica (Redwood) + Atlas security (Marco) for verification.
- Data residency confirmed: US region only. We'll configure the trial cluster in us-west-2 unless Atlas requests another zone.

I've attached a short playbook that outlines data handling expectations: data_handling_playbook.pdf.

Next steps from me:
- Share sample payloads and the prompt/eval suite by EOD Nov 10.
- Block 60m on Nov 15 for kickoff (Hannah to confirm time).

On Mon, Nov 8, 2026 at 10:05 AM Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai> wrote:
> [previous message omitted]

Best,
Priya
From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah_schmitt@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@atlasretail.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-10T15:42:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Trial ownership playbook & infra coordination - next steps

All — thanks for the quick turn. Final confirmation and action items before kickoff: 

Confirmed owners
- Infra (provisioning/monitoring): Monica Patel
- App + eval harness: Priya Natarajan
- Security liaison (Redwood): Priya & Monica (split responsibilities)
- Security liaison (Atlas): Marco Bianchi
- Procurement contact (Atlas): Elena to introduce PO contact during kickoff

Action items (owners & due dates)
- Monica: VPC diagram + KMS checklist (attach audit sample) — due Nov 12 EOD — attachment: infra_responsibility_matrix.csv
- Priya: prompt/eval suite + sample payloads — due Nov 10 EOD (I see the file in Drive, thanks)
- Karthik: add RACI to Drive and tag stakeholders — due Nov 11
- Hannah: send final kickoff invite (Nov 15 9am PT) + DocuSign PO template link — due Nov 11

Please reply-all with any objections by Nov 11 EOD, otherwise we consider this the baseline for the kickoff. If Atlas procurement needs a different PO cadence, flag that in your reply and we'll adjust.

Kickoff invite: placeholder calendar invite attached (calendar.ics) — I'll follow with the actual calendar event once I have confirmations. DocuSign link: https://www.docusign.com/placeholder/ATL-PO-2026

Thanks — Hannah

--
Hannah Schmitt
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference

text:
From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah_schmitt@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@atlasretail.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-08T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: Trial ownership playbook & infra coordination - next steps

Thanks for a productive discovery earlier, Elena — good to meet the Atlas team. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we have a clear path into the trial: 

Recap / Success criteria (draft)
- Integrations: Redwood SDK ingest + embedding endpoint validated on a representative workload (<= 120ms median token latency).
- Quality: top-3 business intent accuracy >= 88% on the provided eval set (we'll share the prompt suite).
- Cost: Production TCO estimate within the band we discussed (target: < $0.06/token S1 equivalent).

Proposed owners (we need confirmation or alternate contacts):
- Infra (cluster, region, network): Monica Patel
- App / SDK + validation: Priya Natarajan
- Security & compliance liaison: TBD (Atlas to confirm security contact)
- Procurement / PO & contracting: Atlas procurement contact (CC'd if available)

Timeline (proposal)
- Week 0 (Nov 15): Kickoff + environment checklist
- Week 1-2: Integration, smoke tests, data wiring
- Week 3: Eval run and quality readout
- Week 4: PO/contract finalization and production plan

Attachments: trial_success_criteria_v1.xlsx, infra_responsibility_matrix.csv (first-pass).

Asks
1) Atlas: confirm security contact and data residency requirement (US / EU).
2) Internal: Karthik / Priya / Monica — please review the infra matrix and comment by EOD Monday so we can lock owners.
3) Everyone: confirm availability for a 60-minute kickoff on Nov 15 (proposed 9am PT).

Thanks — Hannah

--
Hannah Schmitt
Enterprise Sales 
Redwood Inference


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah_schmitt@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.ai>, Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@atlasretail.com>
Date: 2026-11-08T10:05:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Trial ownership playbook & infra coordination - next steps

Thanks for the clear recap, Hannah. I made a quick pass on the infra matrix and sketched a lightweight RACI so we have something to sign off on before the kickoff. Attached: proposed_raci.csv

My notes (short):
- Infra: Monica is best placed to own cluster provisioning, network/VPC peering, and monitoring hooks. Monica -> Owner, Karthik -> Support (runbook + SLO targets).
- App: Priya will own SDK integration, test corpus, and the quality evaluation harness. Priya -> Owner, Hannah -> Account Exec / stakeholder comms.
- Security: Recommend a split. Priya handles app-level controls (secrets, token handling). Monica + our security engineering (cc'd on follow-up) handle infra-level audits (audit logs, KMS).
- Procurement: Monica to coordinate internal cost estimates; Hannah to coordinate with Atlas procurement for PO timelines.

Proposed short checklist pre-kickoff:
- Monica: VPC/subnet plan + access method (bastion or SSO).
- Priya: sample payloads + eval prompts (we'll use these for the quality gate).
- Hannah: DocuSign PO template + list of procurement contacts.

If this looks good I can drop these into the Drive trial folder and flag the kickoff invite.

On Mon, Nov 8, 2026 at 09:12 AM Hannah Schmitt <hannah_schmitt@redwood.ai> wrote:
> [previous message omitted]

Thanks,
Karthik


From: Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@atlasretail.com>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah_schmitt@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-09T08:30:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Trial ownership playbook & infra coordination - next steps

Appreciate the recap, Hannah. Confirming Atlas folks and a couple notes: 
- Security point of contact: Marco Bianchi (copied on this thread) will manage our side of the security and data handling assessment.
- Data residency: primary dataset must remain in US region for this pilot — we cannot use EU at this stage.
- Procurement: I will introduce you to our procurement lead (PO expected within 3 weeks after kickoff).

We agree with the suggested owners. One request: can Redwood provide an example audit log snippet and KMS integration checklist prior to the kickoff so Marco's team can pre-validate?

Thanks,
Elena
Atlas Retail


From: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.ai>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah_schmitt@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.ai>, Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@atlasretail.com>
Date: 2026-11-09T11:15:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Trial ownership playbook & infra coordination - next steps

Thanks, Elena — welcome Marco. Quick clarifications from my side: 
- App-level security (token lifecycle, input sanitization, SDK secrets) = owned by Priya (Redwood).
- Infra-level security (KMS, network ACLs, audit logs) = Monica (Redwood) + Atlas security (Marco) for verification.
- Data residency confirmed: US region only. We'll configure the trial cluster in us-west-2 unless Atlas requests another zone.

I've attached a short playbook that outlines data handling expectations: data_handling_playbook.pdf.

Next steps from me:
- Share sample payloads and the prompt/eval suite by EOD Nov 10.
- Block 60m on Nov 15 for kickoff (Hannah to confirm time).

On Mon, Nov 8, 2026 at 10:05 AM Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai> wrote:
> [previous message omitted]

Best,
Priya


From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah_schmitt@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@atlasretail.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-10T15:42:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Trial ownership playbook & infra coordination - next steps

All — thanks for the quick turn. Final confirmation and action items before kickoff: 

Confirmed owners
- Infra (provisioning/monitoring): Monica Patel
- App + eval harness: Priya Natarajan
- Security liaison (Redwood): Priya & Monica (split responsibilities)
- Security liaison (Atlas): Marco Bianchi
- Procurement contact (Atlas): Elena to introduce PO contact during kickoff

Action items (owners & due dates)
- Monica: VPC diagram + KMS checklist (attach audit sample) — due Nov 12 EOD — attachment: infra_responsibility_matrix.csv
- Priya: prompt/eval suite + sample payloads — due Nov 10 EOD (I see the file in Drive, thanks)
- Karthik: add RACI to Drive and tag stakeholders — due Nov 11
- Hannah: send final kickoff invite (Nov 15 9am PT) + DocuSign PO template link — due Nov 11

Please reply-all with any objections by Nov 11 EOD, otherwise we consider this the baseline for the kickoff. If Atlas procurement needs a different PO cadence, flag that in your reply and we'll adjust.

Kickoff invite: placeholder calendar invite attached (calendar.ics) — I'll follow with the actual calendar event once I have confirmations. DocuSign link: https://www.docusign.com/placeholder/ATL-PO-2026

Subject: Sales → CS: OncologyHealth onboarding - handoff & kickoff checklist

Team — handing off OncologyHealth (PHI-enabled pilot) for kickoff planning. Quick summary up top, details + attachments below.

Deal context:
- ACV: $420k committed (pilot converts to annual licensing pending acceptance)
- Committed go-live window: Nov 30 - Dec 3 (pilot sampling & smoke tests)
- Primary contact: Sara Kim (Ops) / Ben Alvarez (IT) — CC'd
- Special reqs: VPC peering option; data residency in us-west; SOC2 evidence requested during onboarding.

What I’m asking from CS/Delivery:
1) Validate SOW schedule and confirm primary delivery owner for onboarding sprint
2) Confirm intake items we need from OncologyHealth by 11/13 (data access, test dataset, admin contact)
3) Kickoff meeting cadence: propose Tue 11/09 10am PT for initial alignment

Attachments: OncologyHealth_SOW_v3.pdf (pdf), onboarding_checklist.xlsx (xlsx), network_requirements.pdf (pdf).

Internal notes (sensitive): budget includes one-time setup credit of $30k; Sales agreed to 2-week staging window for integration. Please do not share internal discount language externally.

Next steps: Monica — can you pick an owner and confirm Tue 11/09 10am PT works? Vivek — any immediate red flags on peering/residency? Karthik — will hand off the signed DocuSign after closing.

Date: Mon, 09 Nov 2026 11:22:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Sales → CS: OncologyHealth onboarding - handoff & kickoff checklist

Thanks Priya / Monica — quick technical notes from Delivery: 
- VPC peering and data residency are fine for us; will need OncologyHealth network team to provision a peering CIDR and open the ports listed in network_requirements.pdf. I copied their IT alias.
- EHR connectors: if they want live FHIR integration we estimate +2 weeks of engineering work beyond the pilot SOW. If pilot uses CSV drop we can keep timeline.
- Staging capacity: we recommend a dedicated small GPU instance for the pilot to preserve latency targets. Budget note: this may affect the one-time credit allowance.

Action items I’ll take before kickoff:
- Draft a short runbook for peering and firewall rules (deliverable by 11/12)

Date: Tue, 10 Nov 2026 09:30:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Sales → CS: OncologyHealth onboarding - handoff & kickoff checklist

Monica / Team — quick clarification from Sales / Procurement: OncologyHealth signed SOW includes CSV-based pilot only. They requested FHIR as a follow-on option but did not include it in the pilot scope. Priya negotiated 2-week staging buffer to support any small integration work.

On credits: the $30k setup credit is already applied in the ledger; finance will not require additional approvals for the staging instance if we keep configurations under the committed credit. If Delivery identifies overages we’ll need to review before provisioning.

Sara/Ben — confirming CSVs are the expected data format for pilot. If you need FHIR, let's capture that as a follow-up and schedule a separate technical scoping session.

—Karthik

Date: Tue, 10 Nov 2026 09:30:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Sales → CS: OncologyHealth onboarding - handoff & kickoff checklist

Monica / Team — quick clarification from Sales / Procurement: OncologyHealth signed SOW includes CSV-based pilot only. They requested FHIR as a follow-on option but did not include it in the pilot scope. Priya negotiated 2-week staging buffer to support any small integration work.

On credits: the $30k setup credit is already applied in the ledger; finance will not require additional approvals for the staging instance if we keep configurations under the committed credit. If Delivery identifies overages we’ll need to review before provisioning.

Sara/Ben — confirming CSVs are the expected data format for pilot. If you need FHIR, let's capture that as a follow-up and schedule a separate technical scoping session.

—Karthik
Head of Sales, Redwood Inference
karthik.iyer@redwood.ai

On Mon, Nov 9, 2026 at 11:22 Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Thanks Priya / Monica — quick technical notes from Delivery...

From: Sara Kim <sara.kim@oncologyhealth.com>
To: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Ben Alvarez <ben.alvarez@oncologyhealth.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Nov 2026 10:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Sales → CS: OncologyHealth onboarding - handoff & kickoff checklist

Monica / Vivek — thanks for the clarity. Confirming CSVs only for the pilot — we can provide a sanitized sample dataset by 11/12 and our network team will share peering CIDR + contact by 11/11.

For SOC2, procurement expects the program-level report and evidence of encryption-at-rest. I can share the exact questionnaire items in the kickoff meeting.

I’ll join Tue 11/09 10am PT (I see Monica sent the invite). Looking forward to getting started.

—Sara
Senior Ops Lead, OncologyHealth
sara.kim@oncologyhealth.com

messages:
From: Sven Thomsen <sven.thomsen@eutransit.co>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 05 Nov 2026 08:12:00 +0000
Subject: Vendor onboarding for EU Transit - invoicing & PO requirements

Hi Jasmine,

Hope you're well. We're starting the vendor onboarding for the EU Transit project and need to get Redwood set up in our AP system so we can process the first PO and payments. Can you confirm the following details and share any onboarding packet you have?

- Legal company name as it should appear on invoices
- Billing address and VAT/Tax registration (for EU VAT reverse-charge where applicable)
- DUNS / supplier ID (if available)
- Bank details (IBAN/BIC) or SEPA form for payments
- Who to contact for invoice disputes (name + email + phone)
- Whether you accept invoices via our Coupa portal or only by email

We also need a sample invoice that shows VAT/Tax line items (or a note if reverse charge applies). Our AP team prefers to have the vendor registered in Coupa before we issue the PO.

Can you get this over by end of week? If you prefer, we can provisionally issue a PO subject to receiving the documents.

Thanks,

Sven Thomsen
Head of Procurement, EU Transit Logistics
ap@eutransit.co

Attachments: none

From: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwood.com>
To: Sven Thomsen <sven.thomsen@eutransit.co>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 05 Nov 2026 12:45:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Vendor onboarding for EU Transit - invoicing & PO requirements

Hi Sven — thanks for the clear checklist. I've pulled together the documents you asked for and attached our AP packet. High level answers below; see the packet for files and a sample invoice PDF.

- Legal name: Redwood Inference, Inc.
- Billing address (for invoicing): Redwood Inference, Inc. – 1201 Market St, Suite 400, San Francisco, CA 94103, USA
- EU VAT registration (for EU entity): Redwood Inference EU SARL, VAT FR123456789 (please note: invoices from the EU entity will carry EU VAT when applicable; otherwise, we indicate reverse-charge)
- DUNS: 55-123-9876
- Bank (SEPA/IBAN): see attached redwood_bank_details_sepa.pdf — IBAN: FR76 3000 6000 0112 3456 7890 123
- Invoice disputes / billing contact: Marcus Lin, Finance Operations Manager — marcus.lin@redwood.com, +1-415-555-0147
- Invoice delivery: we can accept invoices via Coupa (preferred for automated PO matching) or by email to invoices@redwood.com. If you upload to Coupa, please set the remit-to to the EU entity listed in the AP packet.
- PO requirements: please ensure PO includes the PO number on page 1, remit-to entity (exact legal name), GL code field (our sample prefers GL 5102–Cloud Services), and a short description of service periods (e.g., Nov 1–30, 2026).

I've attached: redwood-ap-packet.pdf (includes our sample_invoice_20261109.pdf), redwood_vat_certificate.pdf, and the SEPA bank details. Happy to run through the Coupa setup with your AP team if helpful.

One note on terms: standard Redwood terms are Net 30. For the first invoice we can be flexible to Net 45 to accommodate your internal cycles; Marcus is copied for signoff on anything beyond Net 30.

Best,

Jasmine Liu
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
jasmine.liu@redwood.com | +1-415-555-0199

Attachments: redwood-ap-packet.pdf (PDF, 420KB)

From: Accounts Payable <ap@eutransit.co>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwood.com>
Cc: Sven Thomsen <sven.thomsen@eutransit.co>, Lena Fischer <lena.fischer@eutransit.co>
Date: Fri, 06 Nov 2026 09:30:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Vendor onboarding for EU Transit - invoicing & PO requirements

Hi Jasmine,

Thanks — packet looks good. A few clarifying points from AP before we open the vendor profile in Coupa:

1) We require a signed bank details confirmation (SEPA) with authorized signature. The DocuSign link you provided in the packet is fine; alternatively you can send the signed PDF.
2) For EU payments we need the IBAN and BIC on file — we have that from your SEPA doc but will need a copy to store in Coupa.
3) Please confirm the remit-to legal entity for the PO we will issue (we have two Redwood entities on file). For our record please indicate which entity will issue the invoice for the attached PO.
4) Confirm whether Redwood will carve out usage-based line items per model (you provided the sample invoice — thank you). We need line-level descriptions to attach to the PO.
5) Payment term confirmation: Coupa defaults to Net 60 for our region; we will set Net 30 if you explicitly request. Do you confirm Net 30?

If you can upload the signed bank form and confirm the remit-to entity, we'll proceed to create the vendor and issue the PO.

Regards,

Accounts Payable Team
EU Transit Logistics
ap@eutransit.co

Attachments: vendor_registration_instructions.pdf

From: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwood.com>
To: Accounts Payable <ap@eutransit.co>
Cc: Sven Thomsen <sven.thomsen@eutransit.co>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 09 Nov 2026 15:22:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Vendor onboarding for EU Transit - invoicing & PO requirements

Hi — quick status update and uploads.

- Remit-to legal entity: Redwood Inference EU SARL (use VAT FR123456789). This is the entity that will issue the invoice for the EU Transit PO.
- Bank form: signed SEPA bank details uploaded (redwood_bank_details_sepa.pdf). DocuSign link provided for your records: https://www.docusign.com/drive/signer/abc123-redwood-bankform
- VAT certificate: uploaded (redwood_vat_certificate.pdf).
- DUNS: 55-123-9876 (included in AP packet).
- Sample invoice: attached sample_invoice_20261109.pdf — includes per-line model usage, unit price, quantity, and VAT/reverse-charge note.

We have also started the Coupa vendor registration at your portal: https://app.coupaportal.eutransit.co/vendors/new?ref=redwood-20261109 — Priya (cc'ed) uploaded the documents this morning.

Terms: we confirm standard Net 30. For the workload kick-off we can accept Net 45 for the initial invoice if needed; Marcus will approve any exception.

Let me know if AP needs anything else to move the vendor to "Active" in Coupa and issue the PO. Happy to hop on a 15-minute call if there's anything that needs to be matched live.

Thanks,

Jasmine Liu
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
jasmine.liu@redwood.com | +1-415-555-0199

Attachments: redwood_vat_certificate.pdf (PDF, 120KB); redwood_bank_details_sepa.pdf (PDF, 80KB); sample_invoice_20261109.pdf (PDF, 65KB)

source.subject: Rebook request: Borealis demo pending onboarding gates and travel
messages:
From: Emma Jones <emma.jones@borealis.com>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.ai>
Cc: procurement@borealis.com, dave.patel@borealis.com
Date: 2026-11-01T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: Scheduled demo on Nov 10 — request to rebook / security questionnaire attached

Hi Jonas,

Thanks again for the walkthrough plan. We originally penciled a demo with your team for Nov 10 at 10:00 PT, but a couple of gating issues on our side mean we need to push. Our CISO (Dave) is traveling that week and procurement has asked us to complete an initial vendor security questionnaire and get an NDA signed before the product walkthrough.

I've attached our security questionnaire (Borealis_Security_Questionnaire.xlsx) — our security team asks for that to be returned filled in so they can triage. Procurement also wants a brief vendor onboarding form. Apologies for the short notice.

Could we look at the week of Nov 16 instead? Would prefer a remote session only.

Emma
Emma Jones
Senior Product Manager, Borealis Systems
emma.jones@borealis.com
+1 415-555-0182

Attachment: Borealis_Security_Questionnaire.xlsx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet)
From: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.ai>
To: Emma Jones <emma.jones@borealis.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-01T11:03:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Scheduled demo on Nov 10 — request to rebook / security questionnaire attached

Hi Emma — thanks for the heads up and for the questionnaire. Totally reasonable to gate the demo behind your security intake.

Looping in Priya (security) and Karthik (solutions engineer) to help speed the review. Priya — could your team confirm the minimum artifacts needed to move to a live demo? Karthik and I can prepare a reduced-scope remote demo if your intake team is happy with the answers.

Emma — we can hold Nov 16–20 as potential windows. Do you have preferred days/times that week? Also we can send a short DocuSign NDA to speed procurement if that helps. I’ll attach the draft demo agenda once we lock a day.

Best,
Jonas Weber
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference
jonas@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0123

--
On Nov 1, 2026, at 9:12 AM, Emma Jones <emma.jones@borealis.com> wrote:
> Hi Jonas,
> 
> Thanks again for the walkthrough plan. We originally penciled a demo with your team for Nov 10 at 10:00 PT...
From: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.ai>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.ai>, Emma Jones <emma.jones@borealis.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-02T08:45:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Scheduled demo on Nov 10 — request to rebook / security questionnaire attached

Hi all — thanks for looping me in. Quick checklist from our side to set expectations: 

- We need the completed questionnaire you attached (Borealis_Security_Questionnaire.xlsx).
- SOC 2 type II (or current security attestation) and a contact for technical intake (SAML metadata if you plan to test SSO).
- If you prefer, we can accept a partial run-through focused on features that don’t require data connectivity while the formal review completes.

Practically, our intake team generally needs ~3 business days to review a completed questionnaire + artifacts before we sign off for a live demo that includes integrations. If Borealis can return the questionnaire by EOD Nov 12 then we should be able to review by Nov 17. If your CISO is out the week of Nov 9–13, that would push a live demo to the week of Nov 16/23 depending on when the paperwork lands.

If procurement needs a PO or vendor contract before the demo, please flag the timeline — we can surface a draft SOW/contract and expedite a DocuSign link.

Priya Natarajan
Head of Security & Trust, Redwood Inference
priya@redwood.ai

--
On Nov 1, 2026, at 11:03 AM, Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Hi Emma — thanks for the heads up and for the questionnaire...
From: Emma Jones <emma.jones@borealis.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.ai>
Cc: procurement@borealis.com, dave.patel@borealis.com, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-04T14:20:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Scheduled demo on Nov 10 — request to rebook / security questionnaire attached

Thanks Priya — that timeline makes sense. Apologies, a couple of clarifications from our side: 

- Our CISO Dave will be out the week of Nov 9–13 (travel). He can review the completed questionnaire the week of Nov 16.
- Procurement has asked us to return a short vendor onboarding form at the same time (attached partial). We’ll aim to get both documents back by Nov 12 but can confirm earlier if possible.
- Preferred demo slots: Nov 18 (anytime PT) or Nov 19 (morning PT). Remote only is fine.

Attaching the vendor onboarding form we started in case any fields from your side would help (Vendor_Onboarding_Form.pdf). We’ll send the completed security questionnaire and PO status by Nov 12.

Best,

source.subject: Pre-kickoff: Carousel Health MAP & stakeholder rolesync
messages:
From: Diego Fernandez <diego@redwood.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 3 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Draft MAP attached — Carousel Health pre-kickoff

Team,

I put together an initial mutual action plan for Carousel Health (attached) ahead of tomorrow's internal pre-kickoff. Goal is to lock roles and the evaluation plan so we can present a clean timeline to their procurement and security teams next week. Can you take a pass on the draft and call out any gaps by EOD today? Specific asks below: 

- Marcus: confirm the technical evaluation criteria (latency targets, SLOs, test prompts) and add any infra constraints we should note.
- Grace: confirm the post-trial success metrics and CS handoff milestones (training, onboarding playbook).
- Priya: flag any security evidence we need to surface during kickoff (SOC2 artifacts, data retention agreement).
- Neha: note any contract/term items likely to require legal review for procurement (data residency, audit rights).

I also sketched a proposed RACI at the top of the sheet — take a look and adjust.

Kickoff objective for internal sync: produce the version we'll share with the prospect on Friday, and agree on a 6–8 week evaluation cadence.

Attachment: Carousel_MAP_v1.xlsx (spreadsheet)

Thanks — Diego

-- 
Diego Fernandez
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Mobile: +1 (415) 555-0219
From: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
To: Diego Fernandez <diego@redwood.com>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 3 Nov 2026 11:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Draft MAP attached — Carousel Health pre-kickoff

Diego —

Quick read, this looks like a good start. A few technical notes (inline):

- Evaluation criteria: add a section for prompt latency P95 (target < 400ms) and a per-day token throughput target. The prospect emphasized throughput for nightly batch jobs.
- Infrastructure constraints: Carousel runs in a private VPC, so we need to confirm whether the demo can run against our hosted API or whether we must use a dedicated/Private deployment for the PoC.
- Test prompts: I added 6 core vertical prompts in the sheet under tab 'eval_prompts'.

I've attached a lightly annotated copy (Carousel_MAP_v1-markup.xlsx). Also, happy to take lead on the technical runbook for the kickoff demo.

On Tue, Nov 3, 2026 at 09:12 AM Diego Fernandez <diego@redwood.com> wrote:
> Team,
> 
> I put together an initial mutual action plan for Carousel Health (attached) ahead of tomorrow's internal pre-kickoff. Goal is to lock roles and the evaluation plan so we can present a clean timeline to their procurement and security teams next week. Can you take a pass on the draft and call out any gaps by EOD today? Specific asks below: 
> 
> - Marcus: confirm the technical evaluation criteria (latency targets, SLOs, test prompts) and add any infra constraints we should note.
> - Grace: confirm the post-trial success metrics and CS handoff milestones (training, onboarding playbook).
> - Priya: flag any security evidence we need to surface during kickoff (SOC2 artifacts, data retention agreement).
> - Neha: note any contract/term items likely to require legal review for procurement (data residency, audit rights).
> 
> I also sketched a proposed RACI at the top of the sheet — take a look and adjust.
> 
> Kickoff objective for internal sync: produce the version we'll share with the prospect on Friday, and agree on a 6–8 week evaluation cadence.
> 
> Attachment: Carousel_MAP_v1.xlsx (spreadsheet)
> 
> Thanks — Diego
> 
> -- 
> Diego Fernandez
> Account Executive | Redwood Inference
> 

-- 
Marcus Lin
Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
From: Grace O'Connor <grace@redwood.com>
To: Diego Fernandez <diego@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 4 Nov 2026 08:23:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Draft MAP attached — Carousel Health pre-kickoff

Thanks for the draft, Diego — good coverage. I made several suggestions in the comments on milestones; main points summarized here: 

1) Stakeholder roster: we should call out names/roles explicitly in the MAP so the prospect can immediately see each owner's scope. My proposed list: 
   - Product (trial owner): Mira Patel — will own evaluation sign-off
   - Security: Aaron Cho — will request SOC2 & data-flow diagrams
   - Procurement: Tom Wu — commercial negotiation lead
   - Engineering: SRE lead (TBD) — technical POC

2) RACI refinement: propose the following: Responsible = Marcus (demo + test harness), Accountable = Diego (deal owner), Consulted = Priya & Neha (security/legal), Informed = CS delivery leads (I'll add names once prospect confirms).

3) Agenda for internal pre-kickoff (30 min):
   - 5m: quick deal context and success criteria
   - 10m: technical constraints / demo plan (Marcus)
   - 10m: security/compliance ask list (Priya)
   - 5m: next steps + assignment of owners (Diego)

4) Proposed internal kickoff times: Thu 11/5 10:00–10:30am PT or Thu 11/5 3:00–3:30pm PT. I prefer the morning.

Please confirm availability and I'll draft the calendar invite with an agenda.

On Tue, Nov 3, 2026 at 11:05 AM Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com> wrote:
> Diego —
> 
> Quick read, this looks like a good start. A few technical notes (inline):
> 
> - Evaluation criteria: add a section for prompt latency P95 (target < 400ms) and a per-day token throughput target. The prospect emphasized throughput for nightly batch jobs.
> - Infrastructure constraints: Carousel runs in a private VPC, so we need to confirm whether the demo can run against our hosted API or whether we must use a dedicated/Private deployment for the PoC.
> - Test prompts: I added 6 core vertical prompts in the sheet under tab 'eval_prompts'.
> 
> I've attached a lightly annotated copy (Carousel_MAP_v1-markup.xlsx). Also, happy to take lead on the technical runbook for the kickoff demo.
> 
> -- 
> Marcus Lin
> Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
From: Diego Fernandez <diego@redwood.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 5 Nov 2026 07:45:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Draft MAP attached — Carousel Health pre-kickoff

Appreciate the quick turn, everyone — this is helpful. Decisions / action items from the thread: 

- Marcus to own technical evaluation doc and the demo runbook (deliverable: tech-runbook.pdf) — due 11/10.
- Priya to compile SOC2 + network/connectivity checklist for Private deployment (deliverable: compliance-evidence.zip) — due 11/9.
- Neha to list any contract clauses we should expect to negotiate (deliverable: redline_guidance.docx) — due 11/12.
- Grace to own the customer success success criteria and onboarding milestones (deliverable: cs-success-metrics.md) — due 11/10.
- Diego to consolidate a shareable MAP and present it at external kickoff on Fri 11/6.

Internal pre-kickoff scheduled: Thu 11/5 10:00–10:30am PT (confirmed). Calendar invite with agenda and Zoom link: https://zoom.us/j/9876543210?pwd=mapkickoff

I'll upload the next MAP version to the shared drive and forward the HubSpot record once complete: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3c-carousel-map

If anything blocks the deliverable dates above, flag here ASAP.

Thanks — Diego

-- 
Diego Fernandez
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Mobile: +1 (415) 555-0219

source.subject: PO exception request — pilot onboarding and thresholds
messages:
From: Sasha Turner <sasha.turner@finovate.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@finovate.com
Date: Mon, 2 Nov 2026 08:47:12 -0700
Subject: Pilot onboarding — PO flexibility request

Hi Aditya,

Thanks again for the demo last week — excited to move the pilot forward. We have a short internal procurement note: our standard AP team prefers issuing a PO for all vendor spend, but for short pilot engagements we can do invoice-only if the vendor accepts an exception.

Pilot details: 3 months, expected spend ~$13,200 (monthly invoices). Attached is the SOW (finovate_sow_v1.pdf). Can Redwood accept an invoice-only path for this pilot while we complete vendor setup? If not, what is the quickest route to get a PO issued from our side?

We can provide the vendor intake form, SOC2 report, and a contact at our procurement team for any questions. Hoping to start billing in early December.

Best,
Sasha Turner
Director, Partner Programs
Finovate Analytics

----
Attachment: finovate_sow_v1.pdf (PDF, 92 KB)

From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
To: Sasha Turner <sasha.turner@finovate.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 2 Nov 2026 10:12:03 -07:00
Subject: Re: Pilot onboarding — PO flexibility request

Thanks for the SOW and the clear ask, Sasha — excited to run this with your team. Quick summary of Redwood's default practice and the exception path: 

- Default: PO required for new vendor onboarding and recurring billing; this ensures invoice matching and finance controls.
- Exceptions: Small-dollar pilots can be approved on an invoice-only basis if preapproved by Procurement and Finance, with evidence (SOC2, W9, signed SOW) and a completed PO-exception form. The standard temporary threshold we approve without executive signoff is $10k total.

Given your SOW estimate (~$13.2k), we can do one of two pragmatic paths:

Option A — expedited exception (fastest): you submit the vendor intake + evidence and we route a PO-exception form to Procurement. With Priya's approval and Finance signoff we can approve a temporary invoice-only cap up to $12k while contracts are finalized.

Option B — full approval: Procurement and Finance approvals + Director (or above) signoff for a one-off increase to $15k. This takes longer but aligns with your full pilot estimate.

Next steps I suggest:
- Sasha: confirm whether you can reduce the first-month invoice (or accept a 50% prepayment structure) to keep initial billing under $12k to speed start.
- Priya: please confirm what additional evidence you need and an estimated SLA for review.

I'll loop procurement and AM to move this.

Thanks,
Aditya Rao
Head of Solutions, Redwood Inference

----
Quoted message below
On Mon, Nov 2, 2026 at 8:47 AM Sasha Turner <sasha.turner@finovate.com> wrote:
> (original message and attachment referenced)

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 3 Nov 2026 09:05:44 -07:00
Subject: RE: Pilot onboarding — PO flexibility request

Thanks Aditya — capturing the evidence checklist and our recommended path below. I ran this past Finance; approximate turnaround times included.

Required evidence for an exception (please upload to shared drive):
- Signed SOW (we have finovate_sow_v1.pdf)
- Vendor intake form / W9 / company registration
- SOC 2 Type II or equivalent security attestation
- Point of contact for billing and for escalation
- Completed PO-exception form (attached stub: redwood_po-exception-form.pdf)

Temporary approval lanes and SLA: 
- Procurement (Priya) can approve up to $12,000 temporary invoice-only carveout with Finance reviewer signoff (24–48 business hours after docs submitted).
- Any request above $12,000 requires Director-level approval (2–5 business days) and additional risk review.

Attachments: redwood_po-exception-form.pdf (fill and return), vendor-risk-worksheet.xlsx (for internal risk scoring).

If Sasha can shave the pilot’s first invoice below $12k (either by shifting month 1 deliverables or by a 50% prepay), we can enable billing and start the pilot while we finish the full approval track. Otherwise we'll route for Director approval — we can fast-track but it will add a few days.

Priya Natarajan
Procurement Manager, Redwood Inference

----
Attachment: redwood_po-exception-form.pdf (PDF stub)
Attachment: vendor-risk-worksheet.xlsx (Excel stub)

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Sasha Turner <sasha.turner@finovate.com>
Cc: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 4 Nov 2026 16:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Pilot onboarding — PO flexibility request

Sasha — quick operational update and next steps. We can approve a temporary invoice-only path capped at $12,000 while we complete the director review for a higher cap. This gets you billing ability immediately for the first invoices.

What we need from you (action items):
1) Complete and return the attached redwood_po-exception-form.pdf (scan or DocuSign)
2) Finalize and sign the SOW (we can send a DocuSign request if helpful) — link to SOW and Drive: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3c-sow-finovate
3) Provide SOC2 or equivalent security attestation and vendor intake fields in the intake form

Operational notes:
- Send invoices to billing@redwood.com and add the Finovate vendor code FIN-5542 in the invoice reference.
- The temporary approval is valid for 90 days; if your pilot extends beyond that we will need to either issue a PO or re-evaluate the threshold.

Marissa will follow up with a DocuSign link for the SOW and a HubSpot task will be created on the deal (HUB-23457).

Best,
Marissa Cole
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

----
Forwarded message included above
Attachments forwarded: redwood_po-exception-form.pdf, vendor-risk-worksheet.xlsx

- Send invoices to billing@redwood.com and add the Finovate vendor code FIN-5542 in the invoice reference.

Best,
Marissa Cole

From: Elaine Park <elaine.park@novaworks.com>
To: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Cc: Nora Singh <nora.singh@novaworks.com>
Date: Tue, 3 Nov 2026 08:12:00 -0500
Subject: Exec briefing — CIO + VP presence (travel day)

Grace — thanks for the quick turnaround on this. CIO (Martin Hale) and VP Product (Aisha Patel) are both traveling the week of Nov 15 and have very tight on-site itineraries. They asked if we can compress the executive briefing into a 45-minute window on Nov 16 while they are in our region. A couple of constraints:

- Preferred time window is 15:00–16:00 local (CIO needs the earlier end to catch a flight).
- Both will be arriving on-site; they would like a short escort/transport plan (15–20 minute buffer) so the meeting start can accommodate travel from the terminal.
- If we need to move to Nov 17 AM, that's possible but less preferred.

Can you confirm whether Redwood can hold 45 minutes (30 min briefing + 15 min Q&A) and provide an escort/arrival buffer? Also if you can send a short run-of-show and SOW stub ahead of time, that would help us escalate internally.

Appreciate it — Elaine

Attachments: itinerary_stub.pdf (referenced)

From: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
To: Elaine Park <elaine.park@novaworks.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 3 Nov 2026 10:00:00 -05:00
Subject: Re: Exec briefing — CIO + VP presence (travel day)

Hi Elaine — thanks, and absolutely we can run that. Quick notes and proposed plan: 

- Yes: 45 minute slot (30 min product/demo, 15 min exec Q&A).
- Proposed time: Nov 16, 15:00–15:45 ET. We'll schedule the room and AV check at 14:30 to allow device warm-up and remote execs to join if needed.
- Escort / commute buffer: we recommend a 20-minute buffer from arrival point to meeting room. Redwood will provide an in-house escort (Ben Carter) who will meet the execs at the reception and confirm they are seated by 14:50.
- Run-of-show: attached is a 1-page agenda (novaworks_exec_briefing_agenda_v2.pdf). It includes a 5-min intro, 25-min demo focused on latency/unit-cost controls for customer scenarios, then 15-min Q&A + next steps.
- SOW & travel note: attaching a draft SOW (draft_sow_novaworks.docx). We can mark travel/escort as an addendum; if NovaWorks prefers Redwood to coordinate on-site logistics (parking voucher, security pass), we can handle that.

Small ask: can you confirm the arrival airport/terminal and whether the execs will have internal security clearances on arrival (helps our escort meet them without delay)?

I'll put Priya on the thread to lock calendar and Marcus to cover any escalations.

Best —
Grace O'Connor
Director, Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference

Attachments: novaworks_exec_briefing_agenda_v2.pdf, draft_sow_novaworks.docx, redwood-escort-guidelines.pdf

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 3 Nov 2026 15:20:00 -05:00
Subject: RE: Exec briefing — CIO + VP presence (travel day)

Looping in the logistics we use for short-notice exec briefings. A couple operational notes: 

- AV dry-run: we should confirm bandwidth and streaming fallback at 14:45 (15 min prior). Priya, please schedule a 14:45 quick test with our webcast host.
- Backup coverage: if either CIO or VP runs late, Ben will serve as the on-site liaison and can start the briefing with a 3–5 minute overview while we wait the 10 minutes grace period. If both are >10 minutes late, propose we shift to Nov 17 AM.
- Security/POC: have them share an on-site POC (name & mobile). That avoids reception bottlenecks.

I'll prepare a short contingency note to include in the meeting invite.

— Marcus

From: Elaine Park <elaine.park@novaworks.com>
To: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Cc: Nora Singh <nora.singh@novaworks.com>

Subject: Re: Exec briefing — CIO + VP presence (travel day)

Grace / team — thanks for the fast work and for the escort plan. Confirmed: 15:00–15:45 ET on Nov 16 works for both Martin and Aisha.

Details:
- Arrival: Martin Hale (CIO) lands at 14:10 ET, Terminal B, will clear security quickly but prefers a 20-minute buffer.
- Aisha Patel (VP) arrives by car at ~14:20 and will be escorted from the building entrance.
- On-site POC: Nora Singh, +1 (617) 555-0192 — she'll meet the escort at reception.

Please go ahead and send the calendar invite with the attached run-of-show and SOW stub. Also confirm whether Redwood needs the execs' badges pre-printed (we can share name/affiliations if that helps).

Elaine

source.subject: Third-party flow-down: DPA & subcontracting approval plan
messages:
From: Olivia Chen <olivia.chen@acmecloud.com>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Tom Evans <tom.evans@mirrorco.com>
Date: Fri, 31 Oct 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Notice of vendor flow-down changes / DPA impact

Hi Hannah,

Attached is Acme's formal change notice for our vendor flow-down terms — we updated the DPA and added a short subcontracting approval clause that affects downstream infra vendors. Key items:

- DPA: added new audit access and retention language (Section 4).
- Subcontracting: flow-down required for any subprocessor that stores or processes customer PII; Acme also requests 15 days' notice for new subprocessors.
- Liability: minor cap language tweaks to align with Acme procurement.

We know this may trigger your internal review. Could you confirm next steps on Redwood's side and who we should work with for legal + procurement? I included Tom from procurement for visibility.

Attachments: ChangeNotice_v2.pdf

Thanks,
Olivia Chen
Senior Contracts Manager
Acme Cloud

From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwoodinference.com>
To: Olivia Chen <olivia.chen@acmecloud.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 31 Oct 2026 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Notice of vendor flow-down changes / DPA impact

Hi Olivia — thanks for sending.

Quick ack from my side and a proposed path forward:

1) Legal (Marcus) to review the DPA redlines and flag any non-starters.
2) RevOps/Procurement (Laura) to confirm how this impacts PO/ordering timelines and whether the 15-day notice meets our SLA commitments.
3) Priya to check whether this triggers any dedicated infra exceptions for customers on Redwood Dedicated or Private.

Can you also drop a clean redline of the DPA (PDF or Word) and a short summary of the exact subcontractor flow-down language you need? That will speed the review.

We will aim to get an internal redline and a mutual action plan by COB Monday. If there's urgency on your side, tell us which items are blocking go-live.

Best,
Hannah Schmitt
Senior Director, Customer Success
Redwood Inference

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwoodinference.com>, Olivia Chen <olivia.chen@acmecloud.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 01 Nov 2026 08:48:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Notice of vendor flow-down changes / DPA impact

Thanks Hannah / Olivia. Looping procurement ops context:

- If Acme requires a 15-day review for new subprocessors, that should be fine operationally but it will need to be reflected in the onboarding SLA and possibly the PO acceptance window.
- For Dedicated/Private customers we may need an explicit exception path (e.g., longer notice or approval committee). Priya, can you confirm the gating today?
- For commercial hosted customers, we can accept notice + email consent for infra providers that are standard cloud providers, but anything custom will require a security questionnaire + legal sign-off.

Proposed immediate asks for Acme:
- Provide the Word redline (so we can iterate).
- Flag which subprocessors are considered "in-scope" (names + roles).
- If possible, confirm whether Acme can accept Redwood's standard DPA with the proposed flow-down language or if they need their version signed.

I can own the PO/ops timeline once legal signals green.

- Laura
Director, RevOps
Redwood Inference

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Olivia Chen <olivia.chen@acmecloud.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 01 Nov 2026 13:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Notice of vendor flow-down changes / DPA impact

Hi all — initial legal read attached (redline_summary.xlsx). High-level notes:

- Scope: We are comfortable with flow-down language that requires subprocessors to abide by equivalent security and data-processing obligations, but we will limit the scope to subprocessors that actually process Customer Data. We will push to avoid an overly-broad "all subcontractors" formulation.
- Notice period: 15 days is reasonable but ask for an explicit right to review and object within that window. If Acme requires automatic approval after 15 days, that's a concern.
- Audit & access: We can accept audit cooperation clauses but not unfettered audit rights. Suggest a vendor-managed attestation (SOC2 + supporting evidence) rather than onsite audits.
- Liability: push to keep caps and carve-outs consistent with Redwood's standard commercial terms.
- Emergency subprocessors: retain emergency substitution language (notify within 48 hours and follow-up with approval process).

I attached a short redline workbook that maps the clauses and recommends fallback language. Next steps: Marcus to propose language and Olivia/Acme to propose alternate text if needed.

Attachments: redline_summary.xlsx

Regards,
Marcus Lin
Senior Counsel, Contracts & Privacy
Redwood Inference

From: Acme Cloud Legal <legal@acmecloud.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Olivia Chen <olivia.chen@acmecloud.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 02 Nov 2026 11:35:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Notice of vendor flow-down changes / DPA impact

Marcus / Hannah — thanks for the quick review. Please find Acme's counter-redline attached (Acme_DPA_redlines_v3.pdf). Summary of our position:

- We can narrow the flow-down to subprocessors that process PII, but we need mandatory flow-down for any subprocessor involved in storage or processing of log data.
- 15-day notice should convert to automatic approval unless a written objection is received. We are willing to accept vendor-hosted attestations (SOC2 Type II) but need audit cooperation language for high-risk subprocessors.
- Emergency substitution language is acceptable with a 72-hour notice and post-facto confirmation.

We are open to a short mutual action plan to get this across the finish line next week. If Redwood can accept the above carve-outs and provide a signable DPA, Acme can issue a PO immediately after signature.

Attachments: Acme_DPA_redlines_v3.pdf

Regards,
Acme Cloud Legal
legal@acmecloud.com

From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwoodinference.com>
To: Acme Cloud Legal <legal@acmecloud.com>, Olivia Chen <olivia.chen@acmecloud.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 02 Nov 2026 16:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Notice of vendor flow-down changes / DPA impact

Thanks everyone — good progress. Proposed mutual action plan (MAP):

1) Redwood legal (Marcus) will provide a clean DPA with our recommended flow-down text by EOD Wednesday (2026-11-03).
2) Acme legal to review and either accept or provide at most one counter-redline by Friday (2026-11-05).
3) Operational checkpoints: Laura to confirm PO timeline; Priya to confirm Dedicated/Private gating.
4) Upon agreement, Acme to send DocuSign link and issue PO.

Interim operational decisions we can accept today:
- Redwood will agree to a 15-day review window with written objection rights (no automatic approval).
- We will accept vendor-hosted SOC2 attestations in lieu of on-site audits for standard infra providers.
- Emergency substitution: 48-hour notice with follow-up confirmation.

Olivia — please confirm if the MAP and interim decisions are acceptable to Acme. If so, I'll ask Marcus to post the clean DPA and include a short summary sheet that maps open items.

Best,
Hannah Schmitt
Senior Director, Customer Success
Redwood Inference

- Notice period: 15 days is reasonable but ask for an explicit right to review and object within that window. If Acme requires automatic approval after 15 days, that's a concern.
- Audit & access: We can accept audit cooperation clauses but not unfettered audit rights. Suggest a vendor-managed attestation (SOC2 + supporting evidence) rather than onsite audits.
- Liability: push to keep caps and carve-outs consistent with Redwood's standard commercial terms.
- Emergency subprocessors: retain emergency substitution language (notify within 48 hours and follow-up with approval process).

I attached a short redline workbook that maps the clauses and recommends fallback language. Next steps: Marcus to propose language and Olivia/Acme to propose alternate text if needed.

Attachments: redline_summary.xlsx

Regards,
Marcus Lin
Senior Counsel, Contracts & Privacy
Redwood Inference



From: Acme Cloud Legal <legal@acmecloud.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Olivia Chen <olivia.chen@acmecloud.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 02 Nov 2026 11:35:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Notice of vendor flow-down changes / DPA impact

Marcus / Hannah — thanks for the quick review. Please find Acme's counter-redline attached (Acme_DPA_redlines_v3.pdf). Summary of our position:

- We can narrow the flow-down to subprocessors that process PII, but we need mandatory flow-down for any subprocessor involved in storage or processing of log data.
- 15-day notice should convert to automatic approval unless a written objection is received. We are willing to accept vendor-hosted attestations (SOC2 Type II) but need audit cooperation language for high-risk subprocessors.
- Emergency substitution language is acceptable with a 72-hour notice and post-facto confirmation.

We are open to a short mutual action plan to get this across the finish line next week. If Redwood can accept the above carve-outs and provide a signable DPA, Acme can issue a PO immediately after signature.

Attachments: Acme_DPA_redlines_v3.pdf

Regards,
Acme Cloud Legal
legal@acmecloud.com



From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwoodinference.com>
To: Acme Cloud Legal <legal@acmecloud.com>, Olivia Chen <olivia.chen@acmecloud.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 02 Nov 2026 16:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Notice of vendor flow-down changes / DPA impact

Thanks everyone — good progress. Proposed mutual action plan (MAP):

1) Redwood legal (Marcus) will provide a clean DPA with our recommended flow-down text by EOD Wednesday (2026-11-03).
2) Acme legal to review and either accept or provide at most one counter-redline by Friday (2026-11-05).
3) Operational checkpoints: Laura to confirm PO timeline; Priya to confirm Dedicated/Private gating.
4) Upon agreement, Acme to send DocuSign link and issue PO.

Interim operational decisions we can accept today:
- Redwood will agree to a 15-day review window with written objection rights (no automatic approval).
- We will accept vendor-hosted SOC2 attestations in lieu of on-site audits for standard infra providers.
- Emergency substitution: 48-hour notice with follow-up confirmation.

Olivia — please confirm if the MAP and interim decisions are acceptable to Acme. If so, I'll ask Marcus to post the clean DPA and include a short summary sheet that maps open items.

Best,
Hannah Schmitt
Senior Director, Customer Success
Redwood Inference

Cc: Tom Evans <tom.evans@mirrorco.com>
Date: Fri, 31 Oct 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Notice of vendor flow-down changes / DPA impact

Hi Hannah,

Attached is Acme's formal change notice for our vendor flow-down terms — we updated the DPA and added a short subcontracting approval clause that affects downstream infra vendors. Key items:

- DPA: added new audit access and retention language (Section 4).

Best,
