source.subject: Cohort-tiling swapflow — plan selection and rollback options
messages:
From: David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alicia Ward <alicia.ward@crestlinehealth.com>, Procurement <procurement@crestlinehealth.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, 25 Oct 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Cohort-tiling swapflow — plan selection and rollback options

Hi Alicia / Procurement team,

Thanks again for taking the time last week. Per our conversation, I put together a short set of packaging options that map to the traffic patterns you described (multi-cohort usage with seasonal spikes and strict rollback windows). High-level options attached (Crestline_SOW_v0.1.pdf).

Quick summary — three paths we usually present:
- Cohort Tiling (reserved per-cohort pools): reserve throughput buckets per clinical cohort, predictable unit economics for each cohort, supports hard isolation and targeted QoS.
- Swapflow (soft-swap + rollback guards): blue/green style rollouts per cohort with automated fallback if latency or quality regressions cross thresholds.
- Hybrid (reserved + on-demand overflow): reserved capacity for baseline with metered overflow for spikes — lower up-front than full reservation.

For the upcoming workshop it would help to have:
- a 7-day sample of token/hour per cohort (CSV)
- target latency SLO per cohort (p50/p95)
- any compliance constraints for private deployment (if you want VPC/on-prem).

I'll propose concrete pricing scenarios once we have the sample traffic — I attached a starter SOW to capture scope and deliverables. Can we schedule 45 minutes the week of Oct 26 to walk the scenarios?

Attachments: [Attachment: Crestline_SOW_v0.1.pdf (pdf, 120KB)]

Best,
David Kim
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
[d] +1 415-555-0162
redwoodinference.com
From: Alicia Ward <alicia.ward@crestlinehealth.com>
To: David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 27 Oct 2026 14:08:13 -0700
Subject: Re: Cohort-tiling swapflow — plan selection and rollback options

Hi David —

Thanks for the SOW. A few quick clarifying questions before the workshop: 
1) Can you define “cohort tiling” in terms of billing unit? Is it per-reserved-GPU-hour, per-token for the cohort, or a combination?
2) For swapflow rollbacks — what triggers an automatic rollback and what are the SLA credit implications if rollback isn’t executed in time?
3) We have three operational cohorts (Intake, Triage, Follow‑up). Can you provide pricing examples for Small (Intake), Medium (Triage), Large (Follow‑up) profiles — e.g., baseline + 30% spikes per month?

Attaching a small sample of hourly token counts for Intake/Triage/Follow-up for last two weeks.

Thanks — happy to do the 45m workshop next Tue (Oct 28) or Thu (Oct 30).

Best,
Alicia Ward
Director, Clinical AI — Crestline Health
[procurement@crestlinehealth.com]
Attachments: [Attachment: crestline_sample_traffic.csv (csv, 14KB)]
From: David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alicia Ward <alicia.ward@crestlinehealth.com>, Procurement <procurement@crestlinehealth.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 29 Oct 2026 11:23:02 -0700
Subject: Re: Cohort-tiling swapflow — plan selection and rollback options

On Tue, Oct 27, 2026 at 2:08 PM Alicia Ward <alicia.ward@crestlinehealth.com> wrote:
> Thanks for the SOW. A few quick clarifying questions before the workshop: 
> 1) Can you define “cohort tiling” in terms of billing unit? Is it per-reserved-GPU-hour, per-token for the cohort, or a combination?
> 2) For swapflow rollbacks — what triggers an automatic rollback and what are the SLA credit implications if rollback isn’t executed in time?
> 3) We have three operational cohorts (Intake, Triage, Follow‑up). Can you provide pricing examples for Small (Intake), Medium (Triage), Large (Follow‑up) profiles — e.g., baseline + 30% spikes per month?

Thanks for the sample traffic — useful. Short answers here and I'll bring detailed numbers to the workshop (pricing_scenarios.xlsx attached):

1) Billing model for Cohort Tiling:
   - Baseline: reserved throughput priced as committed GPU-seconds translated into a per-token effective rate for the reserved window.
   - Overflow: metered per-token above baseline at a defined overflow rate.
   - Net billing is combination (reserved baseline + metered overflow) so customers see predictable baseline spends and controlled spike costs.

2) Swapflow rollback triggers & SLA implications:
   - Triggers: regression on p95 latency > threshold OR quality regression against baseline eval prompts > configured delta OR error rate spike (5xx).
   - Automatic rollback: our routing layer will switch traffic back to the last known good variant within 60–120s if thresholds are crossed during a canary.
   - SLA credits: if rollback fails due to Redwood platform error (not due to customer model code), we offer service credits tiered by downtime/impact. We can standardize exact credit % in the SOW.

3) Pricing examples (summary):
   - Small (Intake): baseline reserve equivalent to 1 rGPU*month, expected overflow ~= 20% — projected monthly: $9,500 (metered overflow billed at $0.00035/token).
   - Medium (Triage): baseline 2 rGPU*month, overflow 30% — projected monthly: $18,000.
   - Large (Follow‑up): baseline 4 rGPU*month, overflow 30–40% — projected monthly: $34,000.

I'll walk through the attached spreadsheet during the workshop and show a hybrid example where we reserve per-cohort for Intake/Triage and leave Follow‑up largely metered to save on committed cost.

Attachments: [Attachment: pricing_scenarios.xlsx (xlsx, 85KB)]

Proposed workshop times: Thu Oct 30, 10:00–10:45 PT or Fri Oct 31, 9:00–9:45 PT. Which works?

Best,
David
From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 30 Oct 2026 16:40:21 -0700
Subject: RE: Cohort-tiling swapflow — concession ask for Crestline (internal)

David — quick internal note before we finalize the pricing proposal:

- Discounting guidance: standard allowable AE concession is 15% off list for onboarding pilots. For a competitive close here (Crestline is strategic regional healthcare), we can stretch to 20% net if they sign a 12‑month commit with a minimum committed spend of $10M tokens (or equivalent Dedicated capacity).
- SLA/rollback: we must limit automatic rollback obligation to production releases initiated through Redwood-managed rollout; customer-managed variants should be subject to a 24‑hour manual remediation window before credit applies.
- Legal: ask Legal to review SOW_v0.1 for the rollback clause and indemnities. Recommend we attach the SOW and a redline doc for the customer to review.

If David wants to offer the 20% scenario, Karthik please confirm we can approve the concession for this deal and provide any required approval matrix/discount code.

Thanks,
Priya Natarajan
Head of Revenue Operations | Redwood Inference
From: David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alicia Ward <alicia.ward@crestlinehealth.com>, Procurement <procurement@crestlinehealth.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 01 Nov 2026 15:03:00 -0700
Subject: Cohort-tiling swapflow — final pricing proposal and next steps

Alicia / Procurement team —

Thanks for the workshop today — appreciated the clarity on cohort profiles. Attached is the formal pricing proposal (Crestline_Pricing_Proposal_v3.pdf) and an SOW prepared for signature (SOW_Crestline_v1_for_signature.docx). Summary of the two options we discussed: 

Option A — Hybrid Cohort Pack (recommended pilot):
- Reserve: Intake (1 rGPU equivalent), Triage (2 rGPU equivalent)
- Follow‑up: metered overflow only
- Intro discount: 15% off list for the first 6 months; 12‑month commit available at 20% if a minimum $10M token commit is accepted.
- Rollback SLA: automatic rollback for Redwood-managed canaries (60–120s); credits applied only for platform failures per SOW.

Option B — Dedicated Cohort Reservation (higher predictability):
- Reserve baseline for all three cohorts (4 rGPU total)
- 18% discount with 12‑month commit; 22% available with 24‑month commit and upfront invoice.

Next steps:
1) Please review the attached SOW and indicate any redlines by EOD Nov 5.
2) If the terms look good, we will send the DocuSign package (link below) for signature and provision a 2‑week onboarding window after signature.
3) Proposed kickoff: Nov 8, 10:00 PT (internal onboarding + technical handoff).

DocuSign preview: https://www.docusign.com/crestsignevent/abc123

Attachments: [Attachment: Crestline_Pricing_Proposal_v3.pdf (pdf, 95KB), Attachment: SOW_Crestline_v1_for_signature.docx (docx, 210KB)]

Let me know if you want me to schedule a short call with Legal to walk the rollback wording.

Best regards,
David Kim
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
[d] +1 415-555-0162
On Mon, 01 Nov 2026 at 15:03:00 -0700 David Kim wrote: See attachments.

- Follow‑up: metered overflow only
- Intro discount: 15% off list for the first 6 months; 12‑month commit available at 20% if a minimum $10M token commit is accepted.
- Rollback SLA: automatic rollback for Redwood-managed canaries (60–120s); credits applied only for platform failures per SOW.

Option B — Dedicated Cohort Reservation (higher predictability):
- Reserve baseline for all three cohorts (4 rGPU total)
- 18% discount with 12‑month commit; 22% available with 24‑month commit and upfront invoice.

Next steps:
1) Please review the attached SOW and indicate any redlines by EOD Nov 5.
2) If the terms look good, we will send the DocuSign package (link below) for signature and provision a 2‑week onboarding window after signature.
3) Proposed kickoff: Nov 8, 10:00 PT (internal onboarding + technical handoff).

DocuSign preview: https://www.docusign.com/crestsignevent/abc123

Attachments: [Attachment: Crestline_Pricing_Proposal_v3.pdf (pdf, 95KB), Attachment: SOW_Crestline_v1_for_signature.docx (docx, 210KB)]

Let me know if you want me to schedule a short call with Legal to walk the rollback wording.

Best regards,
David Kim

text:
From: David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alicia Ward <alicia.ward@crestlinehealth.com>, Procurement <procurement@crestlinehealth.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, 25 Oct 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Cohort-tiling swapflow — plan selection and rollback options

Hi Alicia / Procurement team,

Thanks again for taking the time last week. Per our conversation, I put together a short set of packaging options that map to the traffic patterns you described (multi-cohort usage with seasonal spikes and strict rollback windows). High-level options attached (Crestline_SOW_v0.1.pdf).

Quick summary — three paths we usually present:
- Cohort Tiling (reserved per-cohort pools): reserve throughput buckets per clinical cohort, predictable unit economics for each cohort, supports hard isolation and targeted QoS.
- Swapflow (soft-swap + rollback guards): blue/green style rollouts per cohort with automated fallback if latency or quality regressions cross thresholds.
- Hybrid (reserved + on-demand overflow): reserved capacity for baseline with metered overflow for spikes — lower up-front than full reservation.

For the upcoming workshop it would help to have:
- a 7-day sample of token/hour per cohort (CSV)
- target latency SLO per cohort (p50/p95)
- any compliance constraints for private deployment (if you want VPC/on-prem).

I'll propose concrete pricing scenarios once we have the sample traffic — I attached a starter SOW to capture scope and deliverables. Can we schedule 45 minutes the week of Oct 26 to walk the scenarios?

Attachments: [Attachment: Crestline_SOW_v0.1.pdf (pdf, 120KB)]

Best,
David Kim
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
[d] +1 415-555-0162
redwoodinference.com


From: Alicia Ward <alicia.ward@crestlinehealth.com>
To: David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 27 Oct 2026 14:08:13 -0700
Subject: Re: Cohort-tiling swapflow — plan selection and rollback options

Hi David —

Thanks for the SOW. A few quick clarifying questions before the workshop: 
1) Can you define “cohort tiling” in terms of billing unit? Is it per-reserved-GPU-hour, per-token for the cohort, or a combination?
2) For swapflow rollbacks — what triggers an automatic rollback and what are the SLA credit implications if rollback isn’t executed in time?
3) We have three operational cohorts (Intake, Triage, Follow‑up). Can you provide pricing examples for Small (Intake), Medium (Triage), Large (Follow‑up) profiles — e.g., baseline + 30% spikes per month?

Attaching a small sample of hourly token counts for Intake/Triage/Follow-up for last two weeks.

Thanks — happy to do the 45m workshop next Tue (Oct 28) or Thu (Oct 30).

Best,
Alicia Ward
Director, Clinical AI — Crestline Health
[procurement@crestlinehealth.com]
Attachments: [Attachment: crestline_sample_traffic.csv (csv, 14KB)]


From: David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alicia Ward <alicia.ward@crestlinehealth.com>, Procurement <procurement@crestlinehealth.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 29 Oct 2026 11:23:02 -0700
Subject: Re: Cohort-tiling swapflow — plan selection and rollback options

On Tue, Oct 27, 2026 at 2:08 PM Alicia Ward <alicia.ward@crestlinehealth.com> wrote:
> Thanks for the SOW. A few quick clarifying questions before the workshop: 
> 1) Can you define “cohort tiling” in terms of billing unit? Is it per-reserved-GPU-hour, per-token for the cohort, or a combination?
> 2) For swapflow rollbacks — what triggers an automatic rollback and what are the SLA credit implications if rollback isn’t executed in time?
> 3) We have three operational cohorts (Intake, Triage, Follow‑up). Can you provide pricing examples for Small (Intake), Medium (Triage), Large (Follow‑up) profiles — e.g., baseline + 30% spikes per month?

Thanks for the sample traffic — useful. Short answers here and I'll bring detailed numbers to the workshop (pricing_scenarios.xlsx attached):

1) Billing model for Cohort Tiling:
   - Baseline: reserved throughput priced as committed GPU-seconds translated into a per-token effective rate for the reserved window.
   - Overflow: metered per-token above baseline at a defined overflow rate.
   - Net billing is combination (reserved baseline + metered overflow) so customers see predictable baseline spends and controlled spike costs.

2) Swapflow rollback triggers & SLA implications:
   - Triggers: regression on p95 latency > threshold OR quality regression against baseline eval prompts > configured delta OR error rate spike (5xx).
   - Automatic rollback: our routing layer will switch traffic back to the last known good variant within 60–120s if thresholds are crossed during a canary.
   - SLA credits: if rollback fails due to Redwood platform error (not due to customer model code), we offer service credits tiered by downtime/impact. We can standardize exact credit % in the SOW.

3) Pricing examples (summary):
   - Small (Intake): baseline reserve equivalent to 1 rGPU*month, expected overflow ~= 20% — projected monthly: $9,500 (metered overflow billed at $0.00035/token).
   - Medium (Triage): baseline 2 rGPU*month, overflow 30% — projected monthly: $18,000.
   - Large (Follow‑up): baseline 4 rGPU*month, overflow 30–40% — projected monthly: $34,000.

I'll walk through the attached spreadsheet during the workshop and show a hybrid example where we reserve per-cohort for Intake/Triage and leave Follow‑up largely metered to save on committed cost.

Attachments: [Attachment: pricing_scenarios.xlsx (xlsx, 85KB)]

Proposed workshop times: Thu Oct 30, 10:00–10:45 PT or Fri Oct 31, 9:00–9:45 PT. Which works?

Best,
David


From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 30 Oct 2026 16:40:21 -0700

For a competitive close here (Crestline is strategic regional healthcare), we can stretch to 20% net if they sign a 12‑month commit with a minimum committed spend of $10M tokens (or equivalent Dedicated capacity).
- SLA/rollback: we must limit automatic rollback obligation to production releases initiated through Redwood-managed rollout; customer-managed variants should be subject to a 24‑hour manual remediation window before credit applies.
- Legal: ask Legal to review SOW_v0.1 for the rollback clause and indemnities. Recommend we attach the SOW and a redline doc for the customer to review.

If David wants to offer the 20% scenario, Karthik please confirm we can approve the concession for this deal and provide any required approval matrix/discount code.

source.subject: Vendor onboarding: invoice routing preference and invoice template details
messages:
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
To: Sophie Liu <sophie.liu@ozdata.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 12 Oct 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Vendor onboarding: invoice routing preference and invoice template details

Hi Sophie —

Welcome and thanks for getting set up with Redwood. We're looping in Priya (Vendor Ops) and Marissa (AP) so we can confirm the quickest path for OzData invoices. Two quick questions to align on process before we finalize the vendor record: 

1) Preferred invoice submission method: our finance team prefers vendors use the Vendor Portal for automated PO matching and faster payment turnarounds (portal invoices create an automatic AP ticket tied to the PO). If you cannot access the portal, we can accept invoices by email to ap@redwood.ai, but that requires manual matching and can add a week to payment.

2) Invoice template fields: to avoid delays we require Invoice#, Invoice Date, Redwood PO#, Remit To (address + ACH info or payment email), Itemized line items with unit price/qty, and Tax ID/withholding info if applicable. I've attached a sample invoice template for reference.

If you want the portal invite now I can trigger that and send step-by-step onboarding (only takes a couple minutes). Otherwise, please confirm that your AP team will send invoices to ap@redwood.ai and that all invoices will include the PO number listed on purchase orders.

Thanks — happy to jump on a quick call if helpful.

—Jordan
Jordan Blake
Strategic Partnerships | Redwood Inference
jordan.blake@redwood.ai
Attach: ozdata_sample_invoice.xlsx (xlsx, 48KB)
From: Sophie Liu <sophie.liu@ozdata.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Cc: OzData AP <ap@ozdata.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Oct 2026 12:03:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Vendor onboarding: invoice routing preference and invoice template details

Hi Jordan —

Thanks for the overview. OzData can do either portal or email, but our AP team currently handles invoices via email by default. A couple of clarifying items before we proceed: 

- Remit/ACH details: where should we put the bank info on the invoice? Is there a preferred remit-to email or address you want listed?
- PO workflow: will the PO number always appear on the Redwood PO? Some of our billing runs are generated by our ERP and don't include the PO unless we map the PO to the billing batch.

If the portal is preferred and will speed payment, we'd like an invite. Otherwise we will email invoices to ap@redwood.ai and include the PO field when present. Attaching our AP team contact for visibility.

Best,
Sophie Liu
Finance Operations Manager
Sophie.Liu@ozdata.com
OzData Analytics
Attach: (none)
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 13 Oct 2026 08:22:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Vendor onboarding: invoice routing preference and invoice template details

Priya / Marissa —

Forwarding Sophie’s reply. Can you do two things: 

1) Priya: please send the portal invite for OzData and include a note that PO matching is required. Use the onboarding URL below so the vendor sees the correct remit and payment instructions when they accept.

2) Marissa: if for any reason OzData sends invoices by email, can AP confirm the exact remit-to text they should put on the invoice so Sophie can include it? Also confirm whether we can accept invoices without PO for amounts under $1,000 (that would help their ERP edge cases).

I think portal is the path of least friction — but we should be explicit about the PO requirement.

– Jordan

---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: Sophie Liu <sophie.liu@ozdata.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Oct 2026 12:03:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Vendor onboarding: invoice routing preference and invoice template details
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Cc: OzData AP <ap@ozdata.com>

(see above)
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Sophie Liu <sophie.liu@ozdata.com>, OzData AP <ap@ozdata.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 15 Oct 2026 16:42:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Vendor onboarding: invoice routing preference and invoice template details

Hi Sophie and OzData AP —

Thanks for the quick replies. Portal invite sent (link included in onboarding email); once OzData completes the onboarding form, the portal will display the correct Redwood remit-to details (Remit: Redwood Inference, 2200 Market St, San Francisco, CA 94114; ACH: redwood-payments@bank.example).

A couple of AP notes to avoid delays: 
- Please include Redwood PO# on the invoice whenever present. Invoices without PO# will be routed to exceptions and can add ~7–10 business days.
- For one-off invoices under $1,000 we can accept without a PO if you note the Redwood request number in the invoice body and copy ap@redwood.ai.
- Required fields: Invoice Number, Invoice Date, Line-item descriptions, Total, Tax ID (if applicable), and Remit info.

I've attached our vendor onboarding PDF with the required fields and a sample invoice. Once you complete the portal onboarding we will confirm the first PO mapping.

Marissa Cole
Accounts Payable Lead | Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.ai
Attach: vendor_onboarding_form.pdf (pdf, 220KB)

source.subject: CAIQ: runtime isolation exceptions and attestation schedule
messages:
From: Samira Khan <samira.khan@bluecrest-audit.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>
Cc: Evan Lee <evan.lee@acmeretail.com>
Date: 2026-10-11 09:12 PDT
Subject: CAIQ follow-up: runtime isolation mapping and exceptions request

Ben / Kimberly —

Thanks again for the walkthrough yesterday. We are proceeding with the CAIQ review for Acme Retail and need two items to close the "Runtime Isolation" control family:

1) A compact control map showing which Redwood services run in multi-tenant vs dedicated pools, and which controls are enforced at the orchestration layer vs at the VM/gpu runtime. A single-page diagram or CSV crosswalk is ideal.

2) Any current exceptions or planned mitigations for cross-tenant memory-residency (if applicable). If you have a short attestation from the runtime team on expected remediation schedule, that will help Acme's risk committee.

Can you provide the artifacts by EOD Oct 13 PT? If not, propose the earliest delivery. Also attach any sample access logs that demonstrate tenant separation enforcement for the last 30 days.

Thanks — Samira
Lead Auditor, BlueCrest Audit

Attachment: request-checklist-BC.pdf (pdf)
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Samira Khan <samira.khan@bluecrest-audit.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>, Evan Lee <evan.lee@acmeretail.com>
Date: 2026-10-11 10:03 PDT
Subject: Re: CAIQ follow-up: runtime isolation mapping and exceptions request

Samira —

Acknowledged. Kimberly and Priya are looping in the runtime and compliance owners. We should be able to provide a control crosswalk and the requested sample logs by 2026-10-13 EOD PT. Two quick asks:

- Do you need the crosswalk in spreadsheet (CSV) or whiteboard-style PDF?
- For access logs, can the sample be redacted for tenant IDs? We will include a mapping note describing the redaction approach.

Assigned owners: Kimberly (runtime isolation diagram + control map), Priya (evidence package ingestion + log excerpts), Jonas (attestation draft and remediation schedule).

Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference

Attachments: none
From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>
To: Samira Khan <samira.khan@bluecrest-audit.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>, Evan Lee <evan.lee@acmeretail.com>
Date: 2026-10-12 14:20 PDT
Subject: Re: CAIQ follow-up: runtime isolation mapping and exceptions request

Samira —

Short update from the runtime team: we prepared a compact crosswalk and a single-page diagram that shows the service routing and enforcement points. High level notes below and attached files:

- Format: attached PDF for visual diagram + CSV index (caiq-attachment-index.csv) for control mappings to our internal control IDs.
- Multi-tenant vs dedicated: generation, batching, and KV cache processes are multi-tenant by default; customer-dedicated pools are used for Dedicated and Private deployments. Control enforcement for isolation is split between the orchestration layer (namespace & runtime policy) and the gpu runtime (memory guards and periodic scrubs).
- Exceptions: We have one open exception where a legacy caching path could allow ephemeral cross-tenant cache persistence for sequences > 64k tokens; mitigation is scheduled for 2026-11-05 and is tracked in RFC-7543. Jonas will provide attestation language.

Attached: runtime-isolation-diagram.pdf (pdf), caiq-attachment-index.csv (csv), access-log-excerpt.txt (txt)

Kimberly Park
Security Engineering Manager, Redwood Inference
From: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>
To: Samira Khan <samira.khan@bluecrest-audit.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Evan Lee <evan.lee@acmeretail.com>
Date: 2026-10-13 15:04 PDT
Subject: Re: CAIQ follow-up: runtime isolation mapping and exceptions request

Samira —

Attestation and remediation timeline below. Please see the attached attestation snippet (pasted) and the RFC reference for the change window. If Acme requires a formal signed attestation, we can route through our compliance portal — let me know.

Attestation (draft):
"Redwood Inference maintains logical and physical runtime isolation controls between tenants. We have identified a legacy caching path that, under a specific sequence length and batching pattern, could result in ephemeral cross-tenant cache residue. The engineering team has scheduled a remediation for 2026-11-05; mitigations include an aggressive cache eviction policy and a runtime scrub during cold-starts. During the remediation window, runtime logs and the observability pipeline will be used to detect and alert on anomalous cache hits."

Remediation RFC: RFC-7543 (internal) — fix ETA 2026-11-05
Action items:
- Ben: confirm Acme is OK with the remediation ETA and whether they prefer a signed attestation (DocuSign).
- Priya: push the evidence bundle to the Acme secure Share (Drive link) and mark items 1-3 as complete when uploaded.

Jonas Weber
Senior Software Engineer, Runtime — Redwood Inference

Attachment: attestation-draft.txt (txt)

- For access logs, can the sample be redacted for tenant IDs? We will include a mapping note describing the redaction approach.

Assigned owners: Kimberly (runtime isolation diagram + control map), Priya (evidence package ingestion + log excerpts), Jonas (attestation draft and remediation schedule).

Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference

Attachments: none


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>
To: Samira Khan <samira.khan@bluecrest-audit.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>, Evan Lee <evan.lee@acmeretail.com>
Date: 2026-10-12 14:20 PDT
Subject: Re: CAIQ follow-up: runtime isolation mapping and exceptions request

Samira —

Short update from the runtime team: we prepared a compact crosswalk and a single-page diagram that shows the service routing and enforcement points. High level notes below and attached files:

- Format: attached PDF for visual diagram + CSV index (caiq-attachment-index.csv) for control mappings to our internal control IDs.
- Multi-tenant vs dedicated: generation, batching, and KV cache processes are multi-tenant by default; customer-dedicated pools are used for Dedicated and Private deployments. Control enforcement for isolation is split between the orchestration layer (namespace & runtime policy) and the gpu runtime (memory guards and periodic scrubs).
- Exceptions: We have one open exception where a legacy caching path could allow ephemeral cross-tenant cache persistence for sequences > 64k tokens; mitigation is scheduled for 2026-11-05 and is tracked in RFC-7543. Jonas will provide attestation language.

Attached: runtime-isolation-diagram.pdf (pdf), caiq-attachment-index.csv (csv), access-log-excerpt.txt (txt)

Kimberly Park
Security Engineering Manager, Redwood Inference


From: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>
To: Samira Khan <samira.khan@bluecrest-audit.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Evan Lee <evan.lee@acmeretail.com>
Date: 2026-10-13 15:04 PDT
Subject: Re: CAIQ follow-up: runtime isolation mapping and exceptions request

Samira —

Attestation and remediation timeline below. Please see the attached attestation snippet (pasted) and the RFC reference for the change window. If Acme requires a formal signed attestation, we can route through our compliance portal — let me know.

Attestation (draft):
"Redwood Inference maintains logical and physical runtime isolation controls between tenants. We have identified a legacy caching path that, under a specific sequence length and batching pattern, could result in ephemeral cross-tenant cache residue. The engineering team has scheduled a remediation for 2026-11-05; mitigations include an aggressive cache eviction policy and a runtime scrub during cold-starts. During the remediation window, runtime logs and the observability pipeline will be used to detect and alert on anomalous cache hits."

Remediation RFC: RFC-7543 (internal) — fix ETA 2026-11-05
Action items:
- Ben: confirm Acme is OK with the remediation ETA and whether they prefer a signed attestation (DocuSign).
- Priya: push the evidence bundle to the Acme secure Share (Drive link) and mark items 1-3 as complete when uploaded.

Jonas Weber
Senior Software Engineer, Runtime — Redwood Inference

Attachment: attestation-draft.txt (txt)

messages:
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Maya Rao <maya.rao@brightbank.com>, Evan Cho <evan.cho@brightbank.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 11 Oct 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: MAP: rebaseline and milestone reprioritization (post-demo)

Thanks again for running through the demo this morning — appreciated the candid feedback. Attached is a first-pass MAP (v1.2) based on today's discussion. Key points I captured: 

- Target close date: 2026-11-30 (customer requested; draft).
- High-level milestones (in order): Legal review → Procurement terms → Initial integration (sandbox) → Pilot validation (2 weeks) → Production cutover.
- Internal responsibilities: Redwood PM (Jordan) to own pilot scope, Karthik to lead infra/config, Priya to support security questionnaire artifacts.
- Dependencies: BrightBank legal sign-off on data handling and audit logs, procurement PO issuance, and network whitelisting.

Next steps (assignees & target dates):
- I will circulate an editable MAP (Excel) by EOD today — ACTION: BrightBank legal & procurement to confirm internal review windows.
- Karthik: please confirm estimated effort for integration (sandbox + pilot) so we can update timeline.
- Priya: can you pull the SOC2 evidence package and token retention notes for their security team?

Attaching: MAP_v1.2.xlsx (see summary tab).

Jordan Blake
Enterprise AE | Redwood Inference
[jordan.blake@redwood.com]

Attachment: MAP_v1.2.xlsx (spreadsheet)
From: Maya Rao <maya.rao@brightbank.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Cc: Evan Cho <evan.cho@brightbank.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Oct 2026 10:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MAP: rebaseline and milestone reprioritization (post-demo)

Jordan — thanks for the recap and the MAP. Quick update from our side after a sync with legal/procurement this morning: 

- Procurement will not be able to issue a PO until the contract terms (esp. indemnity & data residency clauses) finish a second round of redlines. Legal has scheduled their follow-up for the week of 10/25.
- Because of that, the earliest realistic close we can commit to is now mid-February — proposing a new target: 2027-02-15.
- Given the procurement shift, we'd like to de-prioritize production cutover work until contract is complete; however, we still want Redwood to progress on sandbox integration and a limited pilot prep (data subset only).

Requests: 
1) Can you rebaseline the MAP to reflect the new target close of 2027-02-15 and move the production cutover milestone after legal sign-off?
2) Please mark which deliverables require a PO before work starts vs. those we can do under a discovery/PO-exempt pilot.
3) We also need a short one-pager on audit/logging controls for legal to review ahead of the 10/25 meeting.

Appreciate you looping Priya on this (she's been helpful).

—Maya
Sr. Program Manager, Cloud Integrations | BrightBank
[maya.rao@brightbank.com]
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Maya Rao <maya.rao@brightbank.com>, Evan Cho <evan.cho@brightbank.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Oct 2026 13:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MAP: rebaseline and milestone reprioritization (post-demo)

Maya — thanks for the clarity. I've attached a rebaseline (MAP_v1.3.xlsx) and summarized the changes below so it's easier to digest ahead of your 10/25 legal sync.

Summary of changes (v1.3):
- New target close date: 2027-02-15 (moved from 2026-11-30).
- Milestone reprioritization: 
  1) Legal review (10/25) — owner: BrightBank legal (Maya) / Redwood (Jordan) to provide artifacts.
  2) Procurement PO issuance — owner: BrightBank procurement (Evan).
  3) Sandbox integration (Redwood infra) — can start immediately; owner: Karthik; estimated 3 weeks for initial hookup using a reduced data set.
  4) Pilot validation (limited scope) — owner: BrightBank product + Redwood; target 2 weeks after sandbox completion.
  5) Production cutover & rollout — moved after PO execution and final security sign-off.
- Deliverables that can proceed pre-PO (discovery/pilot-friendly): sandbox integration, pilot plan, limited data ingest script, and audit/logging one-pager.
- Deliverables requiring PO before work: production deployment, extended SLA/uptime commitments, dedicated capacity reservations.

ACTION REQUESTS: 
- Priya: can you attach the SOC2 + logging controls one-pager by EOD tomorrow so I can share with Maya prior to 10/25? (I've added a quick template in the MAP under docs tab.)
- Karthik: please review the sandbox effort estimate in the updated MAP and confirm whether we should schedule a 2-hr kickoff with BrightBank network ops next week.

Attachment: MAP_v1.3.xlsx (updated schedule and owner column), SOW-draft.pdf (SOW placeholder reflecting changes).

Jordan

On Mon, Oct 12, 2026 at 10:03 AM Maya Rao <maya.rao@brightbank.com> wrote:
> Jordan — thanks for the recap and the MAP. Quick update from our side after a sync with legal/procurement this morning:
> ...
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Cc: Maya Rao <maya.rao@brightbank.com>, Evan Cho <evan.cho@brightbank.com>, Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 13 Oct 2026 08:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MAP: rebaseline and milestone reprioritization (post-demo)

Quick note from infra: we can move forward with sandbox integration with the reduced data set — estimate: 2 devs for 3 weeks (prep + validation) and 1 SRE half-time during pilot. 

Constraints/risks: 
- Network whitelisting and firewall rules need network ops engagement from BrightBank; that often takes 7-10 business days once we have the IP list. Recommend scheduling the 2-hour kickoff early next week to surface those blockers.
- If legal requests changes to logging retention or a specific KMS flow, that will add ~1 week for config & keying depending on what BrightBank requires.

I'll update the owner/estimates cells in MAP_v1.3; Jordan, I've penciled in a sandbox kickoff on 10/19 at 11am PT — please confirm or propose alternate.

Karthik
Director, Infrastructure | Redwood Inference
From: Maya Rao <maya.rao@brightbank.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: Evan Cho <evan.cho@brightbank.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 13 Oct 2026 11:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MAP: rebaseline and milestone reprioritization (post-demo)

Jordan / team — this looks good and helpful. Confirming BrightBank internal plan: legal follow-up 10/25, procurement target is week of 11/08 for PO prep, and we can start sandbox hookup the week of 10/19 pending network kickoff.

We accept the new target close of 2027-02-15. Please share the audit/logging one-pager (Priya) and the updated MAP (v1.3 with Karthik's estimates) before EOD 10/14 so we can circulate to legal ahead of the 10/25 meeting.

Maya

> Maya — thanks for the clarity. I've attached a rebaseline (MAP_v1.3.xlsx) and summarized the changes below so it's easier to digest ahead of your 10/25 legal sync.

source.subject: Pricing options and short extension request for 30-day evaluation
messages:
From: Lena Morales <lena@bluecap.com>
To: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@bluecap.com
Date: Fri, 09 Oct 2026 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: Follow-up: pricing breakdown for our 30-day evaluation

Hi David —

Thanks again for the walkthrough this morning. BlueCap's engineering and procurement teams are aligned on moving the eval forward, but before we finalize internal approvals we need a clear comparison between two packaging approaches for the next phase: a prepaid slab (fixed token/burst bucket) vs an hourly-burst/hourpool model billed on usage. Specifically we need:

- A simple cost projection for our likely profile (20k requests/day, median 350 tokens/request, peak x3).
- Which option preserves lower per-token economics once we turn up concurrency?
- How SLA/latency expectations change (if at all) between the two packages and impact on fallback routing.
- Whether Redwood can offer a 2-week trial extension (we need the extra runway to validate a production integration).

Procurement also asked for: sample invoice timeline, PO instructions, and any required terms for short pilots. I'm attaching our internal procurement form for reference.

Appreciate a concise comparison and any spreadsheet you can share so our finance team can run numbers.

Lena Morales
Head of ML Platform
BlueCap Analytics
[Attachment: bluecap_procurement_form.docx]

From: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>
To: Lena Morales <lena@bluecap.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 09 Oct 2026 17:04:00 -07:00
Subject: RE: Follow-up: pricing breakdown for our 30-day evaluation

Lena — thanks for the note and the procurement form. Short answer: we can do both; the right choice depends on expected burstiness and conversion intent. I've attached a lightweight model that snapshots cost at baseline and a 3x peak. High level: 

Option A — Prepaid slab (fixed token bucket)
- Pros: predictable unit economics, easier to map to budget/PO.
- Cons: higher upfront commitment for the same peak capacity; unused tokens may complicate accounting unless converted to credits at conversion.

Option B — Hourpool / burst credits (usage-first)
- Pros: lower friction to start, you pay for actual burst capacity; simpler for unpredictable workloads.
- Cons: costs can be higher under sustained high concurrency; requires a nominal burst buffer to hit SLA targets.

Operational notes:
- SLA: both options can be scoped to our standard inference SLOs. For the slab, we recommend a dedicated small burst reserve to avoid throttling during peak. For hourpool, we configure a soft cap + emergency fallback plan (automated model fallback to a smaller variant) to protect latency.
- Conversion: we typically convert unused slab tokens to a credit that applies to a committed contract if you opt in within 60 days of pilot close.

Next steps for numbers: can you confirm average tokens/request across the 20k/day (sample distribution) and your expected concurrency targets? I attached the model (pilot-pricing-model.xlsx) — fill tabs 'Profile' and 'Peaks' and we'll produce a clean one-page comparison for procurement.

We can do a 2-week extension on the eval under a one-line amendment (no additional charge) — we just need a written confirmation and a PO for any agreed committed capacity if you take the slab. If procurement wants the DocuSign, we can generate it once you confirm packaging.

Attached: pilot-pricing-model.xlsx (editable).

Cheers,

David Kim
Sales Engineer, Redwood Inference
david.kim@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0148
Redwood Inference

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
To: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Cc: Lena Morales <lena@bluecap.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Oct 2026 08:22:00 -07:00
Subject: internal: convertibility & invoice timing (quick)

David / Jonas — quick internal note to align before we finalize the DocuSign: 

- If BlueCap chooses the slab and later converts to a committed plan, we usually apply any unused token value as a prepayment credit that amortizes over the first 3 billing cycles. That avoids the headache of refunds.
- For hourpool customers who want budget predictability, we offer an optional burst cap with alerts and an overage rate that is 20% higher than committed unit price.
- Invoice timing: pilot amendments (no charge) don't need invoices; if they purchase slab up front we can issue an invoice upon execution and accept PO net-30.

David — when you send the DocuSign, mark the payment terms net-30 and include the conversion-credit clause below in the SOW. Jonas — can you double-check SLA wording for the dedicated burst reserve?

Thanks,
Priya
Priya Natarajan
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
[internal note — legal review required only if slab > $50k]

From: Lena Morales <lena@bluecap.com>
To: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@bluecap.com, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Oct 2026 10:04:00 -07:00
Subject: RE: Follow-up: pricing breakdown for our 30-day evaluation

David / Priya — thanks for the clear options and the model. We reviewed internally and would like to: 

1) Take Option B (hourpool) for the extended 2-week eval with a temporary burst cap set to 3x baseline.
2) If we convert to a committed slab within 45 days of pilot close, request a 30% conversion credit on unused burst spend (conditional on a 6-month commit).
3) Please send DocuSign for the 2-week extension amendment and a sample invoice template for PO processing. Procurement attached our PO template and procurement_form for your team to populate.

I'll loop in procurement directly on signature.

Thanks — Lena
Lena Morales
Head of ML Platform, BlueCap Analytics
[Attachment: BlueCap_PO.pdf]

(see earlier attachment)

[End of thread]

source.subject: Interview flow matrix + candidate touchpoints for Jordan Smith (full loop)
messages:
From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
To: Elliot Price <elliot.price@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-10-01T15:12:00Z
Subject: Draft flow matrix for Jordan Smith - full loop / timing constraints

Hi all,

I'm attaching the latest interview_flow_matrix_v3.xlsx for Jordan Smith (Backend + Infra hire). Candidate can do either 10/12–10/16 window; prefers mornings PT (09:00–12:30 PT) and requests a single-day virtual onsite if possible. They also asked for 30-min buffers between panel blocks for restroom/breaks.

Ask: can you please review the proposed sequencing in the sheet and confirm your availability / whether you'd prefer to compress any panels into 45-min vs 60-min blocks? We have four core areas we want covered: System Design, Backend Coding, Performance/Latency (infra), and Cultural/Leadership. Suggested sequence in the draft is: 1) Cultural (30m), 2) System Design (60m), 3) Coding (60m), 4) Infra deep-dive (45m), 5) 15-min debrief.

Notes:
- Candidate is on PT. Interviewers are in PT and CET across the team (I've flagged timezone-sensitive slots in the sheet).
- We'll need an interviewer to capture feedback and post the debrief doc to the hiring folder immediately after the loop.
- I added a tentative Zoom naming convention and placeholder links in the matrix.

Attachment: interview_flow_matrix_v3.xlsx (spread: proposed times, interviewer assignments, buffer slots)

Thanks — please reply with: CONFIRM / SWAP / ADJUST + any comments on debrief logistics.

Priya
Priya Natarajan
Senior Recruiter, Redwood Inference
priya.natarajan@redwood.ai

From: Elliot Price <elliot.price@redwood.ai>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-10-02T09:05:00Z
Subject: Re: Draft flow matrix for Jordan Smith - full loop / timing constraints

Thanks, Priya — I reviewed the matrix. A few quick asks:

1) Debrief: I'd prefer a 20-minute post-loop sync with the panel immediately after the final interview rather than a 15-min; gives us time to settle scores and call out any follow-ups. Can Marissa take notes for the rubric compilation?

2) Sequence tweak: Move System Design to first (60m) — that will set context for the coding exercise. Put Cultural/Leadership in between coding and infra as a 30m touchpoint.

3) Scoring: let's use the 1–5 rubric we've used for the last two hires. Whoever is scheduled in the System Design slot should capture the primary narrative for the hiring doc.

If everyone agrees on the 20-min debrief and the sequence tweak, Priya please go ahead and lock invites and share Zoom links + the debrief doc template.

Elliot
Elliot Price
Staff Engineering Manager, Redwood Inference
elliot.price@redwood.ai

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Elliot Price <elliot.price@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-10-02T11:20:00Z
Subject: Re: Draft flow matrix for Jordan Smith - full loop / timing constraints

Looping in availability: I can cover System Design but have a calendar conflict on 10/14 morning (previous commitment). If Priya can do 10/13 or 10/15 morning that works. For Infra deep-dive, Vivek should take the lead (he already knows the performance baseline we're evaluating).

Two operational notes:
- I can own the notes/feedback compilation if Elliot is okay with that. I'll post the consolidated feedback to the hiring folder and ping the group.
- For the coding exercise, can we do a 45m focused problem + 15m async time for completion (candidate works in the editor and shares link) rather than a full 60m live pair? It shortens the total loop and is less fatiguing.

On the matrix I adjusted the System Design to start at 09:00 PT and moved Cultural to after Coding. See my annotations in the attachment.

Marissa
Marissa Cole
Engineering Manager, Backend
marissa.cole@redwood.ai

From: Jordan Smith <jordan.smith@gmail.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-10-03T08:43:00Z
Subject: Re: Interview availability + matrix notes

Hi Priya — thanks for the schedule draft. I can confirm availability mornings PT on 10/13 and 10/15. Prefer a single-day onsite on 10/15 if that works for the panel. Two quick asks: 1) I use a screen reader occasionally and would appreciate an advance copy of the coding prompt (or an accessible alternative) so I can prepare my environment; 2) 20–30 minute breaks between panels would be helpful.

Happy to sign any accommodations form — let me know. Looking forward to it.

Best,
Jordan Smith
(jordan.smith@gmail.com)

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
To: Elliot Price <elliot.price@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>, Jordan Smith <jordan.smith@gmail.com>
Date: 2026-10-04T14:32:00Z
Subject: Finalized schedule + invites (tentative) — Jordan Smith, virtual onsite 10/15

Thanks everyone — based on availability and Jordan's preference we locked a single-day virtual onsite on 2026-10-15. High-level schedule (all times PT):

09:00–10:00 System Design (Marissa Cole) — Zoom: zoom.us/j/RED-SYS-123
10:20–11:05 Coding (Marissa + panel) — Zoom: zoom.us/j/RED-CODE-456 (45m live + 15m async)
11:25–11:55 Cultural / Leadership (Elliot Price) — Zoom: zoom.us/j/RED-CULT-789
12:15–13:00 Infra Deep-dive (Vivek Kulkarni) — Zoom: zoom.us/j/RED-INFRA-321
13:15–13:35 Panel debrief (all interviewers) — Zoom: zoom.us/j/RED-DEBR-654

I attached final_schedule.ics (calendar bundle) and have provisionally scheduled 20-minute buffers between blocks to respect Jordan's breaks and accessibility request. Marissa — thanks for offering to compile notes; please use the standard scoring template in the hiring folder (hiring/docs/scoring_template.md).

Jordan — we will provide the coding prompt in an accessible HTML format 24 hours in advance and will confirm accommodations intake by EOD 10/05. Priya will also send calendar invites and the Doc link for the debrief doc.

Action items:
- Priya: send invites + accessible prompt by 10/05 EOD
- Marissa: confirm note-taking ownership and post rubric after debrief
- Elliot: confirm if you want any specific infra metrics preloaded for Vivek (latency targets, p99 baselines)

Thanks — Priya
Priya Natarajan
Senior Recruiter, Redwood Inference
priya.natarajan@redwood.ai

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Elliot Price <elliot.price@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-10-05T17:03:00Z
Subject: Re: Finalized schedule + invites (tentative) — Jordan Smith, virtual onsite 10/15

Confirmed — I'll take notes and compile the feedback using the 1–5 rubric. Quick note on the debrief doc: my template includes sections for "Signal", "Risks", and "Follow-ups"; I'll make sure the infra slot adds the p99/latency bullet points for context.

Also, small ops ask: can someone create an interview recording policy blurb for the candidate folder? We don't record, but it's good to have the statement in the hiring pack. I'll post the compiled feedback by 10/15 14:00 PT latest.

Marissa
Marissa Cole
Engineering Manager, Backend
marissa.cole@redwood.ai

They also asked for 30-min buffers between panel blocks for restroom/breaks.

Ask: can you please review the proposed sequencing in the sheet and confirm your availability / whether you'd prefer to compress any panels into 45-min vs 60-min blocks? We have four core areas we want covered: System Design, Backend Coding, Performance/Latency (infra), and Cultural/Leadership. Suggested sequence in the draft is: 1) Cultural (30m), 2) System Design (60m), 3) Coding (60m), 4) Infra deep-dive (45m), 5) 15-min debrief.

Notes:
- Candidate is on PT. Interviewers are in PT and CET across the team (I've flagged timezone-sensitive slots in the sheet).
- We'll need an interviewer to capture feedback and post the debrief doc to the hiring folder immediately after the loop.
- I added a tentative Zoom naming convention and placeholder links in the matrix.

Attachment: interview_flow_matrix_v3.xlsx (spread: proposed times, interviewer assignments, buffer slots)

Priya

source.subject: Execution memo — benchmarking cadence, MAP deliverables & variant tradeoffs
messages:
From: Sarah Nguyen <sarah.nguyen@atlashealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: John Lee <john.lee@atlashealth.com>
Date: 2026-10-01T09:14:00-07:00
Subject: Execution memo — benchmarking cadence, MAP deliverables & variant tradeoffs

Ben — thanks for taking this on. Attached is a short requirements doc from our infra team (latency targets, concurrency expectations, and a note on cold-start tolerance). High-level asks from Atlas Health: 

- End-to-end P50 token latency <= 200ms for the primary clinical summarization flow (avg prompt length ~120 tokens).
- Support concurrent interactive sessions for ~50 clinicians with occasional spikes to 120.
- TTFT (time to first token) under 220ms for cold requests 95% of the time.
- Deterministic output quality: we will run a 500-example eval set (examples attached in the RFP).

We're trying to fold Redwood's benchmarking and variant comparisons into the MAP so procurement can sign off quickly. Can you propose a concrete cadence (what you will measure, when, and what pass/fail criteria you expect) and a recommended production variant (or shortlist) that balances cost and quality?

Attachments: ATLAS_RFP_requirements.pdf (5 pages)

Thanks,
Sarah Nguyen
Program Manager, Platform Reliability
Atlas Health
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Nguyen <sarah.nguyen@atlashealth.com>
Cc: John Lee <john.lee@atlashealth.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-01T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Execution memo — benchmarking cadence, MAP deliverables & variant tradeoffs

Sarah — appreciate the clarity. Quick reply with a proposed MAP-integrated benchmarking plan and timeline. I'll loop in Priya for the technical bench run and Karthik for commercial constraints. Summary below (full MAP doc attached).

Proposed cadence (2-week sprint view):
- Week 0 (Today): Align on eval set (you mentioned 500 examples) and SLO definitions.
- Week 1: Redwood runs parallel microbenchmarks (latency/TTFT) across 3 candidate variants; collect throughput/concurrency profiles.
- Week 2: Quality eval (500 examples) and combined perf-quality tradeoff analysis + cost model.
- Week 2 end: MAP deliverable: go/no-go recommendation + canary rollout plan.

Pass/fail criteria (suggested):
- Latency: P50 token latency <= 200ms and P95 <= 400ms for interactive flows at target concurrency.
- TTFT: median <= 160ms, 95th <= 220ms.
- Quality: automated metrics (ROUGE/BERTScore or custom metrics) not worse than your baseline by > 2% and human spot-checks acceptable.
- Concurrency: baseline sustain at 50 sessions with <10% latency degradation; spike mode to 120 with degraded but available behavior + fallback variant if exhausted.

Attachments: mutual_action_plan_v3.docx, redwood_variant_summary.pdf

Action items:
1) Priya to confirm benchmark config and test harness.
2) Atlas to share the 500 example eval set and baseline metrics.
3) Karthik to provide estimated unit economics per variant (day 1 and steady state).

Ben Carter
Enterprise AE | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0182
From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, John Lee <john.lee@atlashealth.com>, Sarah Nguyen <sarah.nguyen@atlashealth.com>
Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-01T15:48:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Execution memo — benchmarking cadence, MAP deliverables & variant tradeoffs

Folks — attaching a short results summary after our microbench run (attached CSV has full traces). Key notes from the lab run (all tests run on m60-equivalent infra, synthetic prompt distribution matched to your 120-token median):

Candidate variants tested:
- rw-gpt-7b-q4f (quantized fast path) — optimized for latency
- rw-gpt-13b (mixed precision) — balanced quality/throughput
- rw-gpt-13b-q8_365 (higher fidelity quantized) — higher quality, higher cost

Microbench highlights (single-VM steady concurrency profile):
- rw-gpt-7b-q4f: P50 token latency 85ms; TTFT median 90ms; sustain throughput 320 tok/s; handles 60 concurrent user sessions (interactive) before tail latency +30%.
- rw-gpt-13b: P50 token latency 140ms; TTFT median 150ms; sustain throughput 210 tok/s; handles 50 sessions with <10% degradation.
- rw-gpt-13b-q8_365: P50 token latency 210ms; TTFT median 230ms; sustain throughput 150 tok/s; good quality on our eval set but TTFT exceeds your stated 220ms threshold at cold start for 95th percentile.

Quality spot-check (initial):
- On your 500-example holdout: rw-gpt-13b and rw-gpt-13b-q8_365 outperform rw-gpt-7b-q4f on clinical fidelity metrics; difference between 13b and 13b-q8_365 is smaller (~1.2% on our BERTScore proxy) but non-zero on edge-case factuality checks. Full CSV contains per-example scores.

Recommendations (shortlist):
- If TTFT and latency are hard SLOs: rw-gpt-7b-q4f as primary with rw-gpt-13b as fallback for high-quality batch tasks (or selective routing for complex prompts).
- If quality is prioritized and TTFT can be mitigated with warm caches: rw-gpt-13b (or q8 variant) but plan on pre-warming and a higher cost bucket.

Next steps:
- I will run the 500-example end-to-end eval tomorrow and upload atlas_map_benchmark_results_v1.csv to the Drive link.
- Evelyn: can you advise on canary shard size and rollback SLI?

Priya Natarajan
Platform Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
priya.natarajan@redwood.com
From: Sarah Nguyen <sarah.nguyen@atlashealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, John Lee <john.lee@atlashealth.com>
Date: 2026-10-02T09:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Execution memo — benchmarking cadence, MAP deliverables & variant tradeoffs

Thanks — this is exactly the level of detail we needed. A couple quick clarifications before we lock the MAP: 

- For rw-gpt-7b-q4f, what is the expected cost delta vs rw-gpt-13b per 1M tokens (ballpark)? We need that for procurement.
- On the canary, we want progressive rollout across 3 hospital clusters; can you propose shard percentages and SLI thresholds for auto-rollback?

Also, we care about deterministic behavior for clinical summaries (we will accept some latency increase if it meaningfully reduces hallucinations). If rw-gpt-13b-q8 shows only ~1% quality improvement but doubles cost, we will favor the 7b option. Please loop Karthik for pricing and Evelyn for rollout specifics.

Sarah
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Nguyen <sarah.nguyen@atlashealth.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-03T15:42:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Execution memo — benchmarking cadence, MAP deliverables & variant tradeoffs

Sarah — copying high-level commercial guidance and a proposed close timeline so procurement has clarity.

Pricing (ballpark per 1M tokens, USD):
- rw-gpt-7b-q4f: $220 / 1M tokens (optimized quantized footprint)
- rw-gpt-13b: $480 / 1M tokens
- rw-gpt-13b-q8_365: $900 / 1M tokens (higher infra and memory footprint)

Recommended commercial approach:
- Start with rw-gpt-7b-q4f for production canary + selective routing to rw-gpt-13b for complex prompts. This reduces unit cost while preserving quality where it matters.
- MAP stages:
  * Week A (benchmarks & quality) — current (complete).
  * Week B (canary rollout across cluster-1 at 5% traffic, monitor 72 hours).
  * Week C (expand to cluster-2 at 20% if SLI stable).
  * Week D (full migration across clusters 3+ if green).

Suggested canary shard sizes and SLI thresholds:
- Canary shards: 5% -> 20% -> 50% -> 100% across three cluster groups.
- SLIs for auto-rollback: P50 latency increase > 25% or error rate > 1.5% or clinical-fidelity degradation on sampling > 3% vs baseline.

Proposed close timeline (target):
- 2026-10-10: Final MAP and commercial play accepted by Atlas procurement.
- 2026-10-17: Canary start (cluster-1).
- 2026-10-31: Full production if canary green.

HubSpot reference: HS-987654 (I will update with the final quote + contract redlines).

Karthik Iyer
Director, Customer Solutions | Redwood Inference
karthik.iyer@redwood.com

Cc: John Lee <john.lee@atlashealth.com>
Date: 2026-10-01T09:14:00-07:00
Subject: Execution memo — benchmarking cadence, MAP deliverables & variant tradeoffs

Ben — thanks for taking this on. Attached is a short requirements doc from our infra team (latency targets, concurrency expectations, and a note on cold-start tolerance). High-level asks from Atlas Health: 

- End-to-end P50 token latency <= 200ms for the primary clinical summarization flow (avg prompt length ~120 tokens).
- Support concurrent interactive sessions for ~50 clinicians with occasional spikes to 120.
- TTFT (time to first token) under 220ms for cold requests 95% of the time.
- Deterministic output quality: we will run a 500-example eval set (examples attached in the RFP).

We're trying to fold Redwood's benchmarking and variant comparisons into the MAP so procurement can sign off quickly. Can you propose a concrete cadence (what you will measure, when, and what pass/fail criteria you expect) and a recommended production variant (or shortlist) that balances cost and quality?

Attachments: ATLAS_RFP_requirements.pdf (5 pages)

Thanks,
Sarah Nguyen
Program Manager, Platform Reliability
Atlas Health


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Nguyen <sarah.nguyen@atlashealth.com>
Cc: John Lee <john.lee@atlashealth.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-01T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Execution memo — benchmarking cadence, MAP deliverables & variant tradeoffs

Sarah — appreciate the clarity. Quick reply with a proposed MAP-integrated benchmarking plan and timeline. I'll loop in Priya for the technical bench run and Karthik for commercial constraints. Summary below (full MAP doc attached).

Proposed cadence (2-week sprint view):
- Week 0 (Today): Align on eval set (you mentioned 500 examples) and SLO definitions.
- Week 1: Redwood runs parallel microbenchmarks (latency/TTFT) across 3 candidate variants; collect throughput/concurrency profiles.
- Week 2: Quality eval (500 examples) and combined perf-quality tradeoff analysis + cost model.
- Week 2 end: MAP deliverable: go/no-go recommendation + canary rollout plan.

Pass/fail criteria (suggested):
- Latency: P50 token latency <= 200ms and P95 <= 400ms for interactive flows at target concurrency.
- TTFT: median <= 160ms, 95th <= 220ms.
- Quality: automated metrics (ROUGE/BERTScore or custom metrics) not worse than your baseline by > 2% and human spot-checks acceptable.
- Concurrency: baseline sustain at 50 sessions with <10% latency degradation; spike mode to 120 with degraded but available behavior + fallback variant if exhausted.

Attachments: mutual_action_plan_v3.docx, redwood_variant_summary.pdf

Action items:
1) Priya to confirm benchmark config and test harness.
2) Atlas to share the 500 example eval set and baseline metrics.
3) Karthik to provide estimated unit economics per variant (day 1 and steady state).

Ben Carter
Enterprise AE | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0182


From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, John Lee <john.lee@atlashealth.com>, Sarah Nguyen <sarah.nguyen@atlashealth.com>
Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-01T15:48:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Execution memo — benchmarking cadence, MAP deliverables & variant tradeoffs

Folks — attaching a short results summary after our microbench run (attached CSV has full traces). Key notes from the lab run (all tests run on m60-equivalent infra, synthetic prompt distribution matched to your 120-token median):

Candidate variants tested:
- rw-gpt-7b-q4f (quantized fast path) — optimized for latency
- rw-gpt-13b (mixed precision) — balanced quality/throughput
- rw-gpt-13b-q8_365 (higher fidelity quantized) — higher quality, higher cost

Microbench highlights (single-VM steady concurrency profile):
- rw-gpt-7b-q4f: P50 token latency 85ms; TTFT median 90ms; sustain throughput 320 tok/s; handles 60 concurrent user sessions (interactive) before tail latency +30%.
- rw-gpt-13b: P50 token latency 140ms; TTFT median 150ms; sustain throughput 210 tok/s; handles 50 sessions with <10% degradation.
- rw-gpt-13b-q8_365: P50 token latency 210ms; TTFT median 230ms; sustain throughput 150 tok/s; good quality on our eval set but TTFT exceeds your stated 220ms threshold at cold start for 95th percentile.

Quality spot-check (initial):
- On your 500-example holdout: rw-gpt-13b and rw-gpt-13b-q8_365 outperform rw-gpt-7b-q4f on clinical fidelity metrics; difference between 13b and 13b-q8_365 is smaller (~1.2% on our BERTScore proxy) but non-zero on edge-case factuality checks. Full CSV contains per-example scores.

Recommendations (shortlist):
- If TTFT and latency are hard SLOs: rw-gpt-7b-q4f as primary with rw-gpt-13b as fallback for high-quality batch tasks (or selective routing for complex prompts).
- If quality is prioritized and TTFT can be mitigated with warm caches: rw-gpt-13b (or q8 variant) but plan on pre-warming and a higher cost bucket.

Next steps:
- I will run the 500-example end-to-end eval tomorrow and upload atlas_map_benchmark_results_v1.csv to the Drive link.
- Evelyn: can you advise on canary shard size and rollback SLI?

Priya Natarajan
Platform Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
priya.natarajan@redwood.com


From: Sarah Nguyen <sarah.nguyen@atlashealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, John Lee <john.lee@atlashealth.com>
Date: 2026-10-02T09:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Execution memo — benchmarking cadence, MAP deliverables & variant tradeoffs

Thanks — this is exactly the level of detail we needed. A couple quick clarifications before we lock the MAP: 

- For rw-gpt-7b-q4f, what is the expected cost delta vs rw-gpt-13b per 1M tokens (ballpark)? We need that for procurement.
- On the canary, we want progressive rollout across 3 hospital clusters; can you propose shard percentages and SLI thresholds for auto-rollback?

Also, we care about deterministic behavior for clinical summaries (we will accept some latency increase if it meaningfully reduces hallucinations). If rw-gpt-13b-q8 shows only ~1% quality improvement but doubles cost, we will favor the 7b option. Please loop Karthik for pricing and Evelyn for rollout specifics.

Sarah


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Nguyen <sarah.nguyen@atlashealth.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-03T15:42:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Execution memo — benchmarking cadence, MAP deliverables & variant tradeoffs

Sarah — copying high-level commercial guidance and a proposed close timeline so procurement has clarity.

Pricing (ballpark per 1M tokens, USD):
- rw-gpt-7b-q4f: $220 / 1M tokens (optimized quantized footprint)
- rw-gpt-13b: $480 / 1M tokens
- rw-gpt-13b-q8_365: $900 / 1M tokens (higher infra and memory footprint)

Recommended commercial approach:
- Start with rw-gpt-7b-q4f for production canary + selective routing to rw-gpt-13b for complex prompts. This reduces unit cost while preserving quality where it matters.
- MAP stages:
  * Week A (benchmarks & quality) — current (complete).
  * Week B (canary rollout across cluster-1 at 5% traffic, monitor 72 hours).
  * Week C (expand to cluster-2 at 20% if SLI stable).
  * Week D (full migration across clusters 3+ if green).

Suggested canary shard sizes and SLI thresholds:
- Canary shards: 5% -> 20% -> 50% -> 100% across three cluster groups.
- SLIs for auto-rollback: P50 latency increase > 25% or error rate > 1.5% or clinical-fidelity degradation on sampling > 3% vs baseline.

Proposed close timeline (target):
- 2026-10-10: Final MAP and commercial play accepted by Atlas procurement.
- 2026-10-17: Canary start (cluster-1).
- 2026-10-31: Full production if canary green.

HubSpot reference: HS-987654 (I will update with the final quote + contract redlines).

Karthik Iyer
Director, Customer Solutions | Redwood Inference
karthik.iyer@redwood.com

messages:
From: Prof. Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@unibo.it>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Oak Ridge BioLab <datalab@oakridge.edu>
Date: Thu, 01 Oct 2026 15:12:00 +0200
Subject: Request: provisional compute subsidy while IRB completes - ethics board note

Hello Ava,

I'm Elena Rossi, PI for the Computational Biology lab at the University of Bologna. We're preparing a small pilot (30 participants) using a Redwood-hosted model for de-identified symptom synthesis research. Our internal IRB process has taken longer than expected due to a new data minimization clause; IRB will likely be formally approved in ~6 weeks.

We were wondering whether Redwood can grant a provisional compute subsidy / trial-top up so we can complete onboarding and internal evaluation while we await the final IRB. We're requesting 6,000 inference credits or equivalent for 90 days so our students can finish integration testing and the privacy-preserving pre-processing pipeline.

Attached: IRB_provisional_notice_unibo.pdf (signed provisional note from the ethics office) and project_scope_and_datasets.xlsx (high level - all data will be synthetic or fully anonymized).

Happy to provide any additional documentation (data handling SOPs, K-anonymity summary). Our timeline is tight: kickoff workshop on Oct 26 — if you can support provisioning before then, that would be ideal.

Best,
Prof. Elena Rossi
Department of Computational Biology
University of Bologna

Attachment: IRB_provisional_notice_unibo.pdf (pdf)
Attachment: project_scope_and_datasets.xlsx (xlsx)

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Prof. Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@unibo.it>
Cc: Michael Grant <michael.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 01 Oct 2026 17:05:00 +00:00
Subject: Re: Request: provisional compute subsidy while IRB completes - ethics board note

Hi Elena — thanks for the clear note and for attaching the provisional IRB acknowledgement. We're excited about the use case and wanting to help you hit your Oct 26 workshop.

I looped in Michael (accounts) and Priya (solutions) to confirm what we can provisionally offer while the formal IRB is pending, and to confirm any compliance/usage guardrails we should add. Michael/Priya — quick ask: can Redwood provision a 6,000-credit pilot valid for 90 days under a provisional grant while we hold the final IRB? If so, what minimal controls should we require (e.g., data access restrictions, confirmed anonymization steps, logging)? Elena, if you have a short SOP or the K-anonymity summary you mentioned, please attach it here.

Thanks,

Ava Chen
Customer Success Manager, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwoodinference.com | +1 415-555-0192
Redwood Inference

From: Michael Grant <michael.grant@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 02 Oct 2026 09:34:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: provisional compute subsidy while IRB completes - ethics board note

Ava / Priya — quick internal notes and recommendation.

- Recommendation: Approve a provisional grant of 6,000 inference credits, valid 90 days from provisioning, tied to the account "University of Bologna - Dept. Comp Bio" (deal EDU-2026-047).
- Conditions: (1) We require the provisional IRB notice (they supplied it) and a short signed attestation that only synthetic/anonymized data will be used for the pilot; (2) Redwood logs must be retained for 90 days and accessible to our security team for spot checks; (3) No PHI/PII as defined in our Acceptable Use policy.
- Next steps: Priya, can you confirm there are no model / dataset restrictions from a safety standpoint? If ok, I can approve credit provisioning and create a HubSpot deal task to mark the credits as "provisional grant".

Thanks — Michael
Michael Grant
Enterprise Accounts, Redwood Inference

> On Thu, Oct 1, 2026 at 17:05 Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> > Hi Elena — thanks for the clear note and for attaching the provisional IRB acknowledgement. We're excited about the use case and wanting to help you hit your Oct 26 workshop.

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Michael Grant <michael.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 02 Oct 2026 10:12:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: provisional compute subsidy while IRB completes - ethics board note

Thanks Michael — quick technical check: the pilot as described (synthetic symptom synthesis) is compatible with our hosted models. No model weights banned. Controls I'd add before provisioning:

- Create a read-only API key scoped to the project that limits storage of raw inputs (prefix hashing + token redaction enabled).
- Enable prefix / KV caching so repeated test sets don't consume unnecessary tokens.
- Set a hard token cap per project run and alerting to avoid runaway usage.

Evelyn — if security signs off on log access and the attestation approach, I'm comfortable provisioning the 6k credits.

Ava — I can draft the simple attestation template and a provisioning checklist (2–3 bullet items) to include in the approval email to the PI.

— Priya
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference

> On Fri, Oct 2, 2026 at 09:34 Michael Grant <michael.grant@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> > Ava / Priya — quick internal notes and recommendation.

From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
To: Michael Grant <michael.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 02 Oct 2026 11:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: provisional compute subsidy while IRB completes - ethics board note

I reviewed the provisional IRB document. Security is comfortable with the proposed conditions (attestation + log access + token caps). Approve provisional grant from a risk perspective.

Evelyn
Head of Security & Compliance, Redwood Inference

(Forwarded below for context)
> On Fri, Oct 2, 2026 at 10:12 Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> > Thanks Michael — quick technical check: the pilot as described (synthetic symptom synthesis) is compatible with our hosted models.

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Prof. Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@unibo.it>
Cc: Michael Grant <michael.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, 03 Oct 2026 09:20:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: provisional compute subsidy while IRB completes - ethics board note

Hi Elena — good news. We've approved a provisional compute subsidy under the following terms:

1) 6,000 inference credits provisioned to your University of Bologna account, valid for 90 days from the provisioning date.
2) Required attestation: a brief signed statement from the PI confirming only synthetic or fully anonymized data will be used for the pilot (I attach a short attestation template).
3) Controls applied: read-only scoped API key, hard token caps per job, and Redwood audit logs enabled for the project (retained for 90 days).
4) If the formal IRB approval arrives within the 90 day window, credits may be converted to a standard pilot grant under EDU-2026-047; if IRB is not approved, remaining credits will be revoked.

Please see the attestation template here (link) and reply with the signed attestation to accept the provisional grant. Once we receive the signed attestation we'll provision the credits and share the scoped API key and instructions.

HubSpot reference: https://hubspot.company/deal/EDU-2026-047
Drive: https://drive.google.com/open?id=1A2b3C4D5E

Thanks and looking forward to the workshop —

Ava Chen
Customer Success Manager, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwoodinference.com | +1 415-555-0192

Attachment: provisional_attestation_template.docx (docx)
