Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 02 Oct 2026 10:12:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: provisional compute subsidy while IRB completes - ethics board note

Thanks Michael — quick technical check: the pilot as described (synthetic symptom synthesis) is compatible with our hosted models. No model weights banned. Controls I'd add before provisioning:

- Create a read-only API key scoped to the project that limits storage of raw inputs (prefix hashing + token redaction enabled).
- Enable prefix / KV caching so repeated test sets don't consume unnecessary tokens.
- Set a hard token cap per project run and alerting to avoid runaway usage.

Evelyn — if security signs off on log access and the attestation approach, I'm comfortable provisioning the 6k credits.

Ava — I can draft the simple attestation template and a provisioning checklist (2–3 bullet items) to include in the approval email to the PI.

— Priya
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference

> On Fri, Oct 2, 2026 at 09:34 Michael Grant <michael.grant@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> > Ava / Priya — quick internal notes and recommendation.


From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
To: Michael Grant <michael.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 02 Oct 2026 11:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: provisional compute subsidy while IRB completes - ethics board note

I reviewed the provisional IRB document. Security is comfortable with the proposed conditions (attestation + log access + token caps). Approve provisional grant from a risk perspective.

Evelyn
Head of Security & Compliance, Redwood Inference

(Forwarded below for context)
> On Fri, Oct 2, 2026 at 10:12 Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> > Thanks Michael — quick technical check: the pilot as described (synthetic symptom synthesis) is compatible with our hosted models.


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Prof. Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@unibo.it>
Cc: Michael Grant <michael.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, 03 Oct 2026 09:20:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: provisional compute subsidy while IRB completes - ethics board note

Hi Elena — good news. We've approved a provisional compute subsidy under the following terms:

1) 6,000 inference credits provisioned to your University of Bologna account, valid for 90 days from the provisioning date.
2) Required attestation: a brief signed statement from the PI confirming only synthetic or fully anonymized data will be used for the pilot (I attach a short attestation template).
3) Controls applied: read-only scoped API key, hard token caps per job, and Redwood audit logs enabled for the project (retained for 90 days).
4) If the formal IRB approval arrives within the 90 day window, credits may be converted to a standard pilot grant under EDU-2026-047; if IRB is not approved, remaining credits will be revoked.

Please see the attestation template here (link) and reply with the signed attestation to accept the provisional grant. Once we receive the signed attestation we'll provision the credits and share the scoped API key and instructions.

HubSpot reference: https://hubspot.company/deal/EDU-2026-047
Drive: https://drive.google.com/open?id=1A2b3C4D5E

Thanks and looking forward to the workshop —

Ava Chen
Customer Success Manager, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwoodinference.com | +1 415-555-0192

Attachment: provisional_attestation_template.docx (docx)

Date: 2026-09-26T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Executive sponsor routing + EB window orchestration for Q4 close

Hi team,

Following last week's demo and the draft MAP I attached (MAP_acme_v2.xlsx), Acme is within reach but we need a short executive push to lock contract language and finalize deployment timelines. Two asks:

1) Sponsor routing: I sketched a primary/backup sponsor map on tab "Exec Map" — can you both review and confirm owners? Suggested primary is Sarah Patel (CIO) and backup is Tom Rivera (SVP Product). If Priya (CS) needs to be looped as sponsor steward, flag it.

2) EB (executive briefing) windows: Acme asked for a 60-minute EB with execs + procurement for final alignment. Proposed windows (all PT): Oct 6 AM (9–10), Oct 7 PM (2–3), Oct 12 AM (8–9). Please mark availability or propose alternatives.

I've included a short evaluation timeline on tab "EvalPlan" — we need owners for the final technical eval (app perf checklist) and the compliance questionnaire.

Attachments: MAP_acme_v2.xlsx (skeleton MAP & roles), EB_candidate_list.pdf (exec attendee suggestions).

Next step: once we confirm sponsor owners and an EB window I'll draft the invite and a 1-page agenda for Sarah.

Thanks,
Alex
Alex Martinez
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
(alex.martinez@redwood.com)

---

To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: Sarah Patel <sarah.patel@acme-corp.com>
Date: 2026-09-26T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Executive sponsor routing + EB window orchestration for Q4 close

Alex — thanks for pulling this together. Quick notes after reviewing MAP_acme_v2:

- Sponsor map: good start. I agree Sarah primary, Tom backup. Recommend we designate Priya Natarajan as "Sponsor Steward" to handle logistics and follow-ups; she can own the MAP checklist items 1.2 (security evidence) and 2.1 (PO execution).
- EB windows: Oct 6 AM works best for the execs I run with (internal calendar permitting). Oct 12 AM is tight for several leaders.
- Evaluation owners: I can take the compliance questionnaire and coordinate with Security for the SOC2 artifacts. Karthik, can you take the performance/demo checklist?

If we lock Oct 6 9–10 PT, I suggest the agenda: 10min intros, 20min technical highlights (latency / cost controls), 15min procurement/Q&A, 15min next steps & signatures path.

Marissa

Oct 6 9–10 PT looks doable for the execs; can you send a formal invite with an agenda and a short doc that summarizes final acceptance criteria (one pager)? Also please include the compliance artifacts you mentioned so procurement can pre-scan.

Tom has forwarded a note to procurement and looped Tom R and me on timelines.

Sarah
Sarah Patel
CIO | Acme Corporation

Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Acme Procurement <procurement@acme-corp.com>
Date: 2026-09-29T10:47:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Executive sponsor routing + EB window orchestration for Q4 close

Great — locking Oct 6 09:00–10:00 PT tentatively. I will:

- Send calendar invite (will include Doc: "ACME_FinalAcceptance_1pager.pdf" and attachments referenced earlier).
- Draft one-pager acceptance criteria & a compressed MAP view (will attach MAP_acme_v2 as PDF).
- Assign owners: Karthik = tech demo + perf checklist; Marissa = procurement & pricing concessions; Priya = compliance artifacts & SOC2 packet.

Action items for this thread:
- Karthik: confirm you can lead the demo and whether you want a 30min tech prep with Acme engineers (propose Oct 2).
- Priya: confirm you can own compliance artifacts and whether you need any help pulling logs/audits.
- Procurement@acme-corp.com: if you have any redline deadlines or preferred legal combo (NDA / PO cadence), flag them now so we can align the EB agenda.

Thanks — Alex

Subject: Re: Executive sponsor routing + EB window orchestration for Q4 close

Priya — I may need the SOC2 artifact index and a short list of retention policies to reference in the demo appendix. Marissa — please flag any price concession items we should be ready to discuss live.

source.subject: Coord: Emberlane demo — travel conflict & alternative options
messages:
From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sanjay Menon <sanjay@emberlane.com>, Olivia Park <olivia@emberlane.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 28 Sep 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Coordination: Emberlane demo — Thu Oct 1 or alternatives

Hi Sanjay / Olivia,

Thanks again for the quick sync last week — excited to show Emberlane how Redwood can help reduce inference cost for your lineup. I pulled a couple of slots that fit the availability you shared:

- Thu Oct 1, 10:00–11:00 PT (recommended: full walkthrough + Q&A)
- Mon Oct 4, 11:00–11:45 PT (shorter, focused demo)

If you prefer, we can also record the walkthrough and schedule a live 30-minute Q&A during your travel window — that’s worked well for other customers on short site visits.

Attached is a short pre-demo questionnaire (helps us tailor infra/batcher examples to your dataset). Can you confirm which option works and who from Emberlane will attend?

Thanks,
Hannah Schmitt
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
[Attachment: pre-demo-questionnaire.pdf (PDF, 42KB)]
From: Sanjay Menon <sanjay@emberlane.com>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 28 Sep 2026 12:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Coordination: Emberlane demo — Thu Oct 1 or alternatives

Hi Hannah,

Thanks — heads up that Im being pulled onto a customer site visit that afternoon. Im out of town Oct 1-2 and likely in meetings most of Oct 3 as well.

Options that would work for us:
1) Move the live demo to Mon Oct 4 (anytime 10:00-13:00 PT)
2) Send a recorded walkthrough we can watch asynchronously + a 30-min live Q&A while Im on the road (early morning PT would be best)

Olivia will join the session if we go live on Oct 4. If you can do a short recorded walkthrough Ill make sure the relevant stakeholders watch it.

—Sanjay
Head of Platform, Emberlane
From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sanjay Menon <sanjay@emberlane.com>
Cc: Olivia Park <olivia@emberlane.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 28 Sep 2026 13:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Coordination: Emberlane demo — Thu Oct 1 or alternatives

Hi Sanjay — perfect, thanks for the note and the windows. We can do both. Two proposals:

A) Move the live demo to Mon Oct 4, 11:00–11:45 PT (well run a concise product + cost case walkthrough)
B) We record a 22–25 minute walkthrough (slides + live demo) and then schedule a 30-minute live Q&A while youre on the road — suggested slot: Sat Oct 2, 08:00 PT (I know early, but thats been easiest to catch people between site meetings).

Marcus (cc) will lead the technical part of the demo if we go live — Marcus, are you available Mon Oct 4 at 11:00 PT?

source.subject: Decision worksheet: deployment lane & regional lock for MAP
messages:
From: Natalie Brooks <natalie@clarityhealth.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene@redwood.com>
Cc: Evan Cho <evan.cho@clarityhealth.com>, contracts@clarityhealth.com
Date: Wed, 22 Sep 2026 10:15:00 -0700
Subject: MAP kickoff — deployment lane & geo tradeoffs

Irene — thanks for the call this morning. Per our notes, the executive team wants a short mutual action plan (MAP) that locks deployment lane and region choices so we can start procurement and security signoffs. High level from our side:

- Primary requirement: PHI processing in scope for pilot (so we need clarity on data residency and SOC2 evidence).
- Preference: we prefer a private deployment for Prod, but open to a hosted or dedicated pilot if it shortens time-to-value.
- Timeline constraint: board wants a vendor commitment by end of month (30 Sep) for budgeting.

Can you confirm what minimal artifacts Redwood needs to sign off on a final recommendation (hosted vs dedicated vs private) and an accelerated evaluation plan we can share internally? I attached a one‑page internal checklist to help us.

Thanks — I can loop in Legal once we have the MAP draft.

—Natalie
Natalie Brooks
Head of Platform Integrations
ClarityHealth

Attachment: Clarity_MAP_request_v1.xlsx (spreadsheet outline)
From: Irene Choi <irene@redwood.com>
To: Natalie Brooks <natalie@clarityhealth.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 23 Sep 2026 09:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MAP kickoff — deployment lane & geo tradeoffs

Hi Natalie — appreciate the clear priorities. Below is a concise MAP we can use as a working doc; I'll drop a formatted version to the HubSpot deal and DocuSign for signature-ready next steps. Quick summary:

1) Pilot (4–6 weeks): Hosted API in US region (fastest onboarding) — Redwood provides SOC2 summary, proposed data retention defaults, and a short security questionnaire response.
2) Production (90+ days): Dedicated capacity or Private (VPC) — choice depends on acceptance gates (latency, throughput, and legal). If ClarityHealth must process PHI on-prem, choose Private; otherwise Dedicated for isolation and SLA.

Minimal artifacts we need from you to finalize recommendation:
- Confirmation of data residency requirements (e.g., PHI classification and whether on-prem is mandatory).
- List of acceptance tests (latency percentiles, throughput targets, sample prompt sets).
- Point of contact for procurement and Legal redlines (email for contracts@).

Next steps I propose:
- I will share a MAP draft (one pager + milestone table) by EOD today.
- Karthik will add an evaluation checklist for the pilot (latency budgets, sample sequences).
- Marcus will provide a cost delta summary for Hosted vs Dedicated vs Private for expected volumes.

Can we schedule a 30-minute decision review on Tue 29 Sep 11:00 PT to lock the lane? If that works, I will circulate a short meeting invite and a slide with the three options and acceptance gates.

Best,
Irene Choi
Senior Solutions Architect | Redwood Inference

On Wed, 22 Sep 2026 at 10:15 AM Natalie Brooks <natalie@clarityhealth.com> wrote:
> Irene — thanks for the call this morning. Per our notes, the executive team wants a short mutual action plan...
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene@redwood.com>, Natalie Brooks <natalie@clarityhealth.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 24 Sep 2026 14:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MAP kickoff — deployment lane & geo tradeoffs

Thanks — adding the technical acceptance items we'll need for the pilot evaluation. Short checklist (will append to the MAP draft):

- Testbed workload: 1M tokens/day simulated; 95th percentile latency target for pilot = 250ms token-level; 99th percentile for production target = 400ms at target batch sizes.
- Tokenization/embedding footprint: sample dataset (5k documents) and one canonical prompt set for quality verification.
- KV cache requirements: confirm whether session stickiness required across regions (helps advise caching strategy).
- Integration hooks: streaming callback URL, structured function-call outputs (schema example), and expected concurrency.

Operational notes: for a hosted pilot we can enable prefix caching and SLO-aware batching to meet the latency targets; Dedicated will allow tighter latency variance and stronger isolation for PHI. Private deployments require network prep (VPC peering and KMS integration) — lead time ~4–6 weeks after PO.

I'll attach a short preflight checklist for your infra team.

—Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Principal SRE, Inference Platform | Redwood Inference

Attachment: pilot_preflight_checklist.pdf
From: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
To: Natalie Brooks <natalie@clarityhealth.com>, Irene Choi <irene@redwood.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 29 Sep 2026 16:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MAP kickoff — deployment lane & geo tradeoffs

Natalie — thanks. Attached is a short cost delta table and a proposed MAP milestone timeline that aligns with the procurement window you mentioned. Highlights:

- Hosted pilot (4 weeks): low up-front, $0 commit; expected 25% lower initial TCO versus Dedicated for low-volume pilot.
- Dedicated (Prod baseline): monthly reserved capacity — reduces per-token cost ~30% at steady-state 50M tokens/mo.
- Private (on-prem/VPC): highest engineering surge and setup fees (one‑time infra onboarding); recommended only if Legal mandates PHI never leave customer-managed network.

Milestone summary (proposed):
1) 2026-09-29: Decision review (lock lane for pilot).
2) 2026-10-04: Signed MAP + PO issued (if Hosted pilot).
3) 2026-10-11: Pilot onboarding & sample dataset ingestion.
4) 2026-11-20: Pilot acceptance and production cutover planning.

Action items for ClarityHealth: share acceptance prompt set (by 2026-10-01) and confirm legal redline owner so we can route DocuSign. I've also uploaded SOC2 evidence and a diagram showing Hosted vs Dedicated vs Private network flows.

Looking forward to the call tomorrow.

Best,
Marcus Lin
Director, Commercial Operations | Redwood Inference

Attachments: Clarity_MAP_v3.xlsx, deployment-diff-diagram.png, SOC2-evidence.pdf

On Fri, 24 Sep 2026 at 14:05:00 -0700 Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com> wrote:
> Thanks — adding the technical acceptance items we'll need for the pilot evaluation...

- Tokenization/embedding footprint: sample dataset (5k documents) and one canonical prompt set for quality verification.
- KV cache requirements: confirm whether session stickiness required across regions (helps advise caching strategy).
- Integration hooks: streaming callback URL, structured function-call outputs (schema example), and expected concurrency.

Operational notes: for a hosted pilot we can enable prefix caching and SLO-aware batching to meet the latency targets; Dedicated will allow tighter latency variance and stronger isolation for PHI. Private deployments require network prep (VPC peering and KMS integration) — lead time ~4–6 weeks after PO.

I'll attach a short preflight checklist for your infra team.

—Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Principal SRE, Inference Platform | Redwood Inference

Attachment: pilot_preflight_checklist.pdf


From: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
To: Natalie Brooks <natalie@clarityhealth.com>, Irene Choi <irene@redwood.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 29 Sep 2026 16:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MAP kickoff — deployment lane & geo tradeoffs

Natalie — thanks. Attached is a short cost delta table and a proposed MAP milestone timeline that aligns with the procurement window you mentioned. Highlights:

- Hosted pilot (4 weeks): low up-front, $0 commit; expected 25% lower initial TCO versus Dedicated for low-volume pilot.
- Dedicated (Prod baseline): monthly reserved capacity — reduces per-token cost ~30% at steady-state 50M tokens/mo.
- Private (on-prem/VPC): highest engineering surge and setup fees (one‑time infra onboarding); recommended only if Legal mandates PHI never leave customer-managed network.

Milestone summary (proposed):
1) 2026-09-29: Decision review (lock lane for pilot).
2) 2026-10-04: Signed MAP + PO issued (if Hosted pilot).
3) 2026-10-11: Pilot onboarding & sample dataset ingestion.
4) 2026-11-20: Pilot acceptance and production cutover planning.

Action items for ClarityHealth: share acceptance prompt set (by 2026-10-01) and confirm legal redline owner so we can route DocuSign. I've also uploaded SOC2 evidence and a diagram showing Hosted vs Dedicated vs Private network flows.

Looking forward to the call tomorrow.

Best,
Marcus Lin
Director, Commercial Operations | Redwood Inference

Attachments: Clarity_MAP_v3.xlsx, deployment-diff-diagram.png, SOC2-evidence.pdf

On Fri, 24 Sep 2026 at 14:05:00 -0700 Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com> wrote:
> Thanks — adding the technical acceptance items we'll need for the pilot evaluation...

source.subject: Observability add-on: storage metering vs flat bundles (proposal)
messages:
From: Alice Monroe <alice@heliosdata.com>
To: David Kim <david_kim@redwood.ai>
Cc: Procurement Helios <procurement@heliosdata.com>
Date: Thu, 17 Sep 2026 14:12:00 -0700
Subject: Quick question on observability retention / tracing pricing

Hi David,

Thanks for the demo earlier — Helios wants to move ahead with Redwood for inference but we need to firm up the observability add-ons before procurement can approve. A few specific questions:

- Do you price tracing/retention as a flat add-on (30/90/365 days) or do you meter spans/GBs for billing?
- For tracing, do we pay per-span stored, per-indexed-trace, or per-ingest? Our telemetry team estimates ~10M spans/month initially, spiking up to ~25M with pipelines.
- How are advanced dashboards (custom visuals, scheduled exports) licensed — per-seat, per-dashboard, or bundled into retention tiers?
- Is there a standard discount for a 12-month commit (we prefer prepaid ARR for predictable costs)?

Can you send a simple proposal or sample invoice that maps the options to expected charges for our starting usage? Procurement needs line items to compare vs competitors.

Appreciate it —
Alice
Alice Monroe
Director, Platform Engineering
Helios Data
From: David Kim <david_kim@redwood.ai>
To: Alice Monroe <alice@heliosdata.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 17 Sep 2026 15:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Quick question on observability retention / tracing pricing

Hi Alice — thanks for the clear list. Summarizing two common pricing approaches we offer and an initial recommendation for Helios: 

1) Flat-tier bundles (recommended for predictable billing)
   - 30 / 90 / 365 day retention tiers; priced as an add-on SKU to your core plan.
   - Pricing is based on retention bucket + ingested trace volume tiers (e.g., 0–10M spans, 10–50M spans).
   - Advanced dashboards: included in Enterprise tier, or add-on at $150/dashboard/month if purchased ala carte.

2) Metered storage model (recommended if you expect large variance)
   - Billed per GB-month of compressed trace storage and per-1M-span ingest increments.
   - Helpful for short-term projects; can be cheaper if you can aggressively sample/truncate traces.

Recommendation for Helios (based on your 10–25M spans/mo estimate): start with the 90-day retention bundle + 10M–50M span tier. This keeps costs predictable while giving you room to increase sampling.

Discounts: we typically offer 15% off list on add-ons for a 12-month prepay. I attached a short matrix showing line-item prices and a sample invoice that maps the two models to your usage.

Next step: if you can confirm expected average daily ingest and whether you need long-term (365d) retention for compliance, Priya can outline technical optimizations (compression, dedupe, sampling) to reduce your bill.

Attaching: observability_addon_matrix.xlsx; sample-invoice-hel-4821.pdf

Best,
David Kim
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
From: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.ai>
To: Alice Monroe <alice@heliosdata.com>, David Kim <david_kim@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 18 Sep 2026 09:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Quick question on observability retention / tracing pricing

Hi Alice — quick technical notes to complement David's commercial framing: 

- What we store for traces is the compressed span payload plus indexed metadata for search. Effective storage per span varies (0.5–2.0 KB) depending on sampling and enrichment.
- We apply block-level compression and opportunity for dedupe (if you re-ingest the same traces). That typically reduces billed GB by ~25–40%.
- For dashboards: advanced dashboards (custom SQL joins, cross-model widgets, scheduled exports) are licensed as part of the Advanced Observability add-on. If you buy the 365-day retention bundle at Enterprise commitment levels, we include up to 25 advanced dashboards. Otherwise, $150/dashboard/month.

Estimated for your baseline (10M spans/month): assume 1.0 KB/span compressed -> ~10 GB/month raw -> ~7 GB/month after compression/dedupe -> 90-day retention ≈ 21 GB-month.

I'll add a recommended config in the pricing calculator and link it in the proposal. If you want a tighter number, share a 24-hour sample and we can run a quick sizing exercise.

- Priya
Priya Natarajan
Solutions Architect, Redwood Inference
From: Alice Monroe <alice@heliosdata.com>
To: David Kim <david_kim@redwood.ai>
Cc: Procurement Helios <procurement@heliosdata.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 18 Sep 2026 10:10:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Quick question on observability retention / tracing pricing

Thanks David and Priya — that level of detail helps. Procurement is leaning to the 90-day bundle + 10M–50M tier. Two asks before we go to legal: 

1) Concession: can you increase the 12-month prepay discount to 20% for the first year if we commit to annual prepay and a $150k ARR minimum?
2) SLA: for observability ingestion/retention (search/query latency and retention durability), can you confirm an SLO and any credit policy if SLOs are missed?

Procurement, looping you in so you have the requested line items.

Best,
Alice
From: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>
To: Alice Monroe <alice@heliosdata.com>, David Kim <david_kim@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 18 Sep 2026 16:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Quick question on observability retention / tracing pricing

Hi Alice — Marcus here (Head of Commercial). Appreciate the clear ask. We can meet you partway on the concession:

- Commercial concession: 18% off add-on list prices for year one with a 12-month prepaid commitment of $150k ARR to Redwood (applies to the 90-day retention bundle and up to 50M spans/month tier).
- Trial extension: include a 6-month trial of 365-day retention for one team workspace (no extra charge) so you can validate compliance/analytics.
- SLA: Observability add-ons inherit our platform SLA for availability; for ingestion/search latency we offer an SLO of 99.5% within target response bounds. Missed SLOs are eligible for service credits per the standard SLA schedule linked below.

I attached a draft concession letter and a sample invoice. If that sounds acceptable, David will coordinate procurement/legal and send a redline-ready SOW + DocuSign link. Proposed timeline: finalize commercial terms this week, legal sign-off next week, start provisioning after PO and payment clears.

Links: https://redwood-inference.app/sla/observability, https://app.docusign.com/link/XYZ-HEL-4821

Regards,
Marcus Lin
Head of Commercial, Redwood Inference
attachments: concession-letter-hel-4821.pdf, sample-invoice-hel-4821.pdf

To: Alice Monroe <alice@heliosdata.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 17 Sep 2026 15:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Quick question on observability retention / tracing pricing

Hi Alice — thanks for the clear list. Summarizing two common pricing approaches we offer and an initial recommendation for Helios: 

1) Flat-tier bundles (recommended for predictable billing)
   - 30 / 90 / 365 day retention tiers; priced as an add-on SKU to your core plan.
   - Pricing is based on retention bucket + ingested trace volume tiers (e.g., 0–10M spans, 10–50M spans).
   - Advanced dashboards: included in Enterprise tier, or add-on at $150/dashboard/month if purchased ala carte.

2) Metered storage model (recommended if you expect large variance)
   - Billed per GB-month of compressed trace storage and per-1M-span ingest increments.
   - Helpful for short-term projects; can be cheaper if you can aggressively sample/truncate traces.

Recommendation for Helios (based on your 10–25M spans/mo estimate): start with the 90-day retention bundle + 10M–50M span tier. This keeps costs predictable while giving you room to increase sampling.

Discounts: we typically offer 15% off list on add-ons for a 12-month prepay. I attached a short matrix showing line-item prices and a sample invoice that maps the two models to your usage.

Next step: if you can confirm expected average daily ingest and whether you need long-term (365d) retention for compliance, Priya can outline technical optimizations (compression, dedupe, sampling) to reduce your bill.

Attaching: observability_addon_matrix.xlsx; sample-invoice-hel-4821.pdf

Best,
David Kim
Account Executive, Redwood Inference


From: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.ai>
To: Alice Monroe <alice@heliosdata.com>, David Kim <david_kim@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 18 Sep 2026 09:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Quick question on observability retention / tracing pricing

Hi Alice — quick technical notes to complement David's commercial framing: 

- What we store for traces is the compressed span payload plus indexed metadata for search. Effective storage per span varies (0.5–2.0 KB) depending on sampling and enrichment.
- We apply block-level compression and opportunity for dedupe (if you re-ingest the same traces). That typically reduces billed GB by ~25–40%.
- For dashboards: advanced dashboards (custom SQL joins, cross-model widgets, scheduled exports) are licensed as part of the Advanced Observability add-on. If you buy the 365-day retention bundle at Enterprise commitment levels, we include up to 25 advanced dashboards. Otherwise, $150/dashboard/month.

Estimated for your baseline (10M spans/month): assume 1.0 KB/span compressed -> ~10 GB/month raw -> ~7 GB/month after compression/dedupe -> 90-day retention ≈ 21 GB-month.

I'll add a recommended config in the pricing calculator and link it in the proposal. If you want a tighter number, share a 24-hour sample and we can run a quick sizing exercise.

- Priya
Priya Natarajan
Solutions Architect, Redwood Inference


From: Alice Monroe <alice@heliosdata.com>
To: David Kim <david_kim@redwood.ai>
Cc: Procurement Helios <procurement@heliosdata.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 18 Sep 2026 10:10:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Quick question on observability retention / tracing pricing

Thanks David and Priya — that level of detail helps. Procurement is leaning to the 90-day bundle + 10M–50M tier. Two asks before we go to legal: 

1) Concession: can you increase the 12-month prepay discount to 20% for the first year if we commit to annual prepay and a $150k ARR minimum?
2) SLA: for observability ingestion/retention (search/query latency and retention durability), can you confirm an SLO and any credit policy if SLOs are missed?

Procurement, looping you in so you have the requested line items.

Best,
Alice


From: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>
To: Alice Monroe <alice@heliosdata.com>, David Kim <david_kim@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 18 Sep 2026 16:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Quick question on observability retention / tracing pricing

Hi Alice — Marcus here (Head of Commercial). Appreciate the clear ask. We can meet you partway on the concession:

- Commercial concession: 18% off add-on list prices for year one with a 12-month prepaid commitment of $150k ARR to Redwood (applies to the 90-day retention bundle and up to 50M spans/month tier).
- Trial extension: include a 6-month trial of 365-day retention for one team workspace (no extra charge) so you can validate compliance/analytics.
- SLA: Observability add-ons inherit our platform SLA for availability; for ingestion/search latency we offer an SLO of 99.5% within target response bounds. Missed SLOs are eligible for service credits per the standard SLA schedule linked below.

I attached a draft concession letter and a sample invoice. If that sounds acceptable, David will coordinate procurement/legal and send a redline-ready SOW + DocuSign link. Proposed timeline: finalize commercial terms this week, legal sign-off next week, start provisioning after PO and payment clears.

Links: https://redwood-inference.app/sla/observability, https://app.docusign.com/link/XYZ-HEL-4821

Regards,
Marcus Lin
Head of Commercial, Redwood Inference
attachments: concession-letter-hel-4821.pdf, sample-invoice-hel-4821.pdf

Alice

From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Li <jordan.li@acme.ai>, Eliot Markus <eliot@brightworks.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 13 Sep 2026 15:22:00 -0700
Subject: Request: compress walkthrough to match CTO evening window (backup coverage included)

Jordan / Eliot —

Quick note: Jordan flagged that Acme's CTO (Victor Shen) will be available only briefly on Wednesday evening (local PT) due to travel — he can join for ~30–40 minutes between 17:30–18:15 PT. Given that, can we compress the demo so Victor can see the core architecture + TRL and we follow up with a short deep-dive for engineering next week?

Two options I want to propose (both attached in the updated agenda):
- Option A (preferred): Move Wednesday 60-min walkthrough -> compress to a focused 40-min session 17:30–18:10 PT to ensure CTO presence. Follow-up deep-dive next week for technical Qs.
- Option B (fallback): Keep current slot and schedule a separate 30-min CTO briefing later this week; Victor may miss the main session.

If Option A works for you, I can update the calendar and we will:
- Trim intro and business narrative to 5 minutes
- Prioritize architecture + KV cache/latency slides (Marcus)
- Leave 10 mins for Victor's Qs and next steps

Attachments: acme_demo_agenda_v2.pdf (agenda with compressed flow) and acme-demo-invite.ics (provisional).

Can you confirm which option you prefer so I can send the updated invite?

Thanks,
Irene
Irene Choi
Head of Solutions — Redwood Inference
irene.choi@redwood.com | (415) 555-0198

---

From: Jordan Li <jordan.li@acme.ai>
To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Eliot Markus <eliot@brightworks.com>
Date: Mon, 13 Sep 2026 17:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Request: compress walkthrough to match CTO evening window (backup coverage included)

Thanks Irene — Option A is perfect. Victor can do 17:30–18:10 PT. He specifically asked to see:
- the runtime routing & fallback behavior (we care about cross-region fallbacks)
- cost controls and batching behaviors under heavy tail loads

One ask: can we add 10 minutes buffer at the end (so 17:30–18:20 PT) in case Victor wants to continue? If Marcus thinks the demo can be tightened by 10 mins, that would be ideal.

Also adding Eliot from Brightworks into the loop — they'll be observing for integration notes.

Jordan
Jordan Li
Head of Platform, Acme.ai
jordan.li@acme.ai | +1-206-555-0123

- If Victor wants to dive into quantization/latency trade-offs, we should follow up with a separate engineering session (I can run that next Tues/Wed).
- Sean (cc'd on future threads) can handle the product Q&A if Victor has to leave early — happy to hand off.

> Jordan

Subject: Re: Request: compress walkthrough to match CTO evening window (backup coverage included)

Thanks all —

Plan agreed: compress primary walkthrough to 17:30–18:20 PT (50 minutes including 10-minute buffer). To keep motion light, here's the final flow (see attached acme_demo_agenda_v2.pdf):
- 17:30–17:35: quick exec context (Irene)
- 17:35–17:50: runtime architecture + routing/fallback (Marcus)
- 17:50–18:05: demo: end-to-end request + latency profile + batching knobs (Marcus)
- 18:05–18:15: CTO Qs + roadmap fit (Victor)
- 18:15–18:20: next steps & immediate ask (Priya)

Backup coverage: if Victor gets delayed, Sean Gallagher will step in for the first 15 minutes to represent product and confirm next steps; we will still record and schedule the deeper engineering follow-up next week.

I updated the calendar invite and attached the provisional .ics — please accept so the invite picks up Victor's slot. If anyone needs different times, flag immediately and we'll pivot.

Thanks,
Irene
Irene Choi
Head of Solutions — Redwood Inference

Attachments: acme_demo_agenda_v2.pdf, acme-demo-invite.ics

Date: Tue, 14 Sep 2026 11:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: compress walkthrough to match CTO evening window (backup coverage included)

All set — calendar updated and invite sent to Victor's account. I included the compressed agenda and added the follow-up engineering slot options (next Tue/Wed 10:00–11:00 PT).

Jordan: you should now see acme-demo-invite.ics in your calendar; Victor is marked as accepted. If anything changes on your side, let me know and I'll update immediately.

Priya Natarajan
Senior Sales Engineer — Redwood Inference
priya.natarajan@redwood.com

Two options I want to propose (both attached in the updated agenda):
- Option A (preferred): Move Wednesday 60-min walkthrough -> compress to a focused 40-min session 17:30–18:10 PT to ensure CTO presence. Follow-up deep-dive next week for technical Qs.
- Option B (fallback): Keep current slot and schedule a separate 30-min CTO briefing later this week; Victor may miss the main session.

If Option A works for you, I can update the calendar and we will:

source.subject: Phased commit & credit milestones for VPC deployment — pricing structure
messages:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Jillian Park <jillian.park@acme-analytics.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 31 Aug 2026 09:22:00 -0700
Subject: Phased commit & credit milestones for VPC deployment — pricing structure

Hi Jillian —

Thanks again for the call this morning. Per our discussion I put together a concise phased-commit proposal so Acme can proceed with an internal approval path while preserving your cost predictability. Key points in the attached MAP-v3:

- Phase 1 (Pilot, 3 months): 50k tokens / mo baseline; Redwood provides $25k credit (one-time) applied against usage. No platform fee for pilot.
- Phase 2 (Ramp, month 4–9): 250k tokens / mo committed; 20% discount off list hosted rate; platform fee applies at a reduced rate (50% of list) starting month 4, waiver on overage for first two months of ramp.
- Phase 3 (Steady state, month 10+): full committed volume (custom), standard platform fee. Renewal pricing to be tied to performance metrics in MAP.

We believe this structure meets Acme’s need to limit upfront spend while giving Redwood visibility for capacity planning. Attachments: MAP-v3.xlsx (milestones + KPIs) and a high-level term sheet.

Next steps: if procurement is comfortable with the staged credits/fee schedule, can we get procurement to confirm the list of required legal clauses (data residency, audit access) so Priya can prepare a redline? Also looping Karthik for commercial approval on the proposed credit sizing.

Best,
Ava
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.ai | +1 415‑555‑0123

Attachment: MAP-v3.xlsx (milestones, metrics and credit schedule)

From: Jillian Park <jillian.park@acme-analytics.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Procurement Team <procurement@acme-analytics.com>
Date: Tue, 1 Sep 2026 10:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Phased commit & credit milestones for VPC deployment — pricing structure

Thanks Ava — this is helpful and straightforward. A few items procurement will need clarified before routing to our CFO (Tom):

1) Platform fee: we appreciate the pilot waiver. For months 4–9 we need the platform fee capped at $12k/mo (not percentage-based) unless we materially exceed the committed token ramp.
2) Credit timing: the $25k pilot credit — will that be invoiced and then refunded, or issued as an upfront credit on the first invoice? Please confirm accounting treatment.
3) Data residency & audit: we will require explicit language around audit windows and evidence (SOC2) for the on-prem package — please attach your SOC2 scope statement.

Lastly, we have a standard procurement checklist; if you can confirm we meet it, I can put this on Tom’s calendar for a decision next Wednesday.

—Jillian
Jillian Park
Head of Platform Engineering, Acme Analytics
jillian.park@acme-analytics.com

---
On Mon, Aug 31, 2026 at 09:22 AM Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai> wrote:
> (original message and attachments)

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
Cc: Jillian Park <jillian.park@acme-analytics.com>
Date: Tue, 1 Sep 2026 11:12:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Phased commit & credit milestones for VPC deployment — pricing structure

Karthik / Priya — FYI, procurement pushed back on the platform fee and wants a hard cap for months 4–9. Also a question on credit timing (invoice vs. upfront). They indicated Tom wants to decide next Wed (9/8).

My read: we can accept a capped platform fee during ramp as long as the economics still cover the reduced rate + annualized committed volume (we get visibility). Suggested concession: cap at $12k/mo for months 4–9 in exchange for a firm 9‑month commitment (start of phase 2), and move the $25k pilot credit to an upfront credit applied to the first invoice (no refunds). That keeps billing simple.

Priya, can you advise whether the platform-fee cap and the credit-as-offset are acceptable from a legal/accounting perspective? Also we should confirm language around audit access and SOC2 scope — attaching the SOC2 scope statement they asked for.

If we can land legal on the clause set today, I’ll send a term-sheet update and ask Jillian to route to Tom.

Thanks,
Ava

Attachment: SOC2-scope-statement.pdf

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 1 Sep 2026 13:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Phased commit & credit milestones for VPC deployment — pricing structure

Ava — quick thoughts from legal/finance:

- Platform fee cap: OK to agree to a fixed cap for months 4–9 provided we include an explicit usage reconciliation clause. If actual usage in the ramp window exceeds committed tokens by >25% for any month, Redwood reserves the right to reprice that month at a pre-agreed overage rate. Include a 30‑day notice window for any repricing.
- Credit treatment: prefer credit applied to first invoice (accounting simpler). Mark credit as non-refundable, non-transferable.
- Commitment trigger: the cap is tied to Acme signing a firm 9‑month committed volume for Phase 2 by 9/10/2026; if they fail to sign, cap void and standard platform fee applies retroactively (with an invoice for the difference).
- Audit/SOC2: confirm Redwood will provide the SOC2 scope statement (attached). For on‑prem/VPC we will include an agreed list of artifacts/controls and a redaction procedure; do not grant direct sysadmin access — provide evidence via secure share.

I’ll draft a short addendum to the term sheet capturing these points (credit treatment, cap + reconciliation, commitment trigger, audit evidence flow). If commercial agrees, I’ll circulate redlined PDF by EOD.

—Priya
Priya Natarajan
Sr. Counsel, Redwood Inference
priya.natarajan@redwood.ai

Attachment: term-sheet-acme-vpc.pdf (will include addendum)

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Jillian Park <jillian.park@acme-analytics.com>, Tom Redd <tom.redd@acme-analytics.com>, Procurement Team <procurement@acme-analytics.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 3 Sep 2026 16:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Phased commit & credit milestones for VPC deployment — pricing structure

Jillian / Tom — thanks for the quick turn. Summarizing what we can confirm today so you can route to CFO:

1) Pilot credit: $25k applied as an upfront credit against your first invoice (non-refundable).
2) Platform fee: waived for pilot (months 1–3). For months 4–9 Redwood will cap platform fee at $12,000/month provided Acme signs a firm 9‑month committed volume for Phase 2 by 2026‑09‑10.
3) Reconciliation: if any ramp month exceeds committed tokens by >25% we reserve the right to reprice that month at the agreed overage rate after a 30‑day notice and provide a credit offset plan if required.
4) Audit & SOC2: attached is the SOC2 scope statement and Redwood’s proposed evidence delivery process (secure share + redacted artifacts). No direct sysadmin access — evidence-only.

Next steps: I attached the updated term-sheet (term-sheet-acme-vpc.pdf) and a refreshed MAP (MAP-v3.xlsx). If Tom is comfortable, please confirm and I’ll send the final redline to sign. If you want a short call to walk through the reconciliation mechanics we can do a 20‑minute slot early next week.

Thanks — looking forward to getting this across the finish line.

Best,
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.ai | +1 415‑555‑0123

Attachments: term-sheet-acme-vpc.pdf, MAP-v3.xlsx

text:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Jillian Park <jillian.park@acme-analytics.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 31 Aug 2026 09:22:00 -0700
Subject: Phased commit & credit milestones for VPC deployment — pricing structure

Hi Jillian —

Thanks again for the call this morning. Per our discussion I put together a concise phased-commit proposal so Acme can proceed with an internal approval path while preserving your cost predictability. Key points in the attached MAP-v3:

- Phase 1 (Pilot, 3 months): 50k tokens / mo baseline; Redwood provides $25k credit (one-time) applied against usage. No platform fee for pilot.
- Phase 2 (Ramp, month 4–9): 250k tokens / mo committed; 20% discount off list hosted rate; platform fee applies at a reduced rate (50% of list) starting month 4, waiver on overage for first two months of ramp.
- Phase 3 (Steady state, month 10+): full committed volume (custom), standard platform fee. Renewal pricing to be tied to performance metrics in MAP.

We believe this structure meets Acme’s need to limit upfront spend while giving Redwood visibility for capacity planning. Attachments: MAP-v3.xlsx (milestones + KPIs) and a high-level term sheet.

Next steps: if procurement is comfortable with the staged credits/fee schedule, can we get procurement to confirm the list of required legal clauses (data residency, audit access) so Priya can prepare a redline? Also looping Karthik for commercial approval on the proposed credit sizing.

Best,
Ava
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.ai | +1 415‑555‑0123

Attachment: MAP-v3.xlsx (milestones, metrics and credit schedule)

From: Jillian Park <jillian.park@acme-analytics.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Procurement Team <procurement@acme-analytics.com>
Date: Tue, 1 Sep 2026 10:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Phased commit & credit milestones for VPC deployment — pricing structure

Thanks Ava — this is helpful and straightforward. A few items procurement will need clarified before routing to our CFO (Tom):

1) Platform fee: we appreciate the pilot waiver. For months 4–9 we need the platform fee capped at $12k/mo (not percentage-based) unless we materially exceed the committed token ramp.
2) Credit timing: the $25k pilot credit — will that be invoiced and then refunded, or issued as an upfront credit on the first invoice? Please confirm accounting treatment.
3) Data residency & audit: we will require explicit language around audit windows and evidence (SOC2) for the on-prem package — please attach your SOC2 scope statement.

Lastly, we have a standard procurement checklist; if you can confirm we meet it, I can put this on Tom’s calendar for a decision next Wednesday.

—Jillian
Jillian Park
Head of Platform Engineering, Acme Analytics
jillian.park@acme-analytics.com

---
On Mon, Aug 31, 2026 at 09:22 AM Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai> wrote:
> (original message and attachments)


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
Cc: Jillian Park <jillian.park@acme-analytics.com>
Date: Tue, 1 Sep 2026 11:12:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Phased commit & credit milestones for VPC deployment — pricing structure

Karthik / Priya — FYI, procurement pushed back on the platform fee and wants a hard cap for months 4–9. Also a question on credit timing (invoice vs. upfront). They indicated Tom wants to decide next Wed (9/8).

My read: we can accept a capped platform fee during ramp as long as the economics still cover the reduced rate + annualized committed volume (we get visibility). Suggested concession: cap at $12k/mo for months 4–9 in exchange for a firm 9‑month commitment (start of phase 2), and move the $25k pilot credit to an upfront credit applied to the first invoice (no refunds). That keeps billing simple.

Priya, can you advise whether the platform-fee cap and the credit-as-offset are acceptable from a legal/accounting perspective? Also we should confirm language around audit access and SOC2 scope — attaching the SOC2 scope statement they asked for.

If we can land legal on the clause set today, I’ll send a term-sheet update and ask Jillian to route to Tom.

Thanks,
Ava

Attachment: SOC2-scope-statement.pdf

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 1 Sep 2026 13:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Phased commit & credit milestones for VPC deployment — pricing structure

Ava — quick thoughts from legal/finance:

- Platform fee cap: OK to agree to a fixed cap for months 4–9 provided we include an explicit usage reconciliation clause. If actual usage in the ramp window exceeds committed tokens by >25% for any month, Redwood reserves the right to reprice that month at a pre-agreed overage rate. Include a 30‑day notice window for any repricing.
- Credit treatment: prefer credit applied to first invoice (accounting simpler). Mark credit as non-refundable, non-transferable.
- Commitment trigger: the cap is tied to Acme signing a firm 9‑month committed volume for Phase 2 by 9/10/2026; if they fail to sign, cap void and standard platform fee applies retroactively (with an invoice for the difference).
- Audit/SOC2: confirm Redwood will provide the SOC2 scope statement (attached). For on‑prem/VPC we will include an agreed list of artifacts/controls and a redaction procedure; do not grant direct sysadmin access — provide evidence via secure share.

I’ll draft a short addendum to the term sheet capturing these points (credit treatment, cap + reconciliation, commitment trigger, audit evidence flow). If commercial agrees, I’ll circulate redlined PDF by EOD.

—Priya
Priya Natarajan
Sr. Counsel, Redwood Inference
priya.natarajan@redwood.ai

Attachment: term-sheet-acme-vpc.pdf (will include addendum)

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Jillian Park <jillian.park@acme-analytics.com>, Tom Redd <tom.redd@acme-analytics.com>, Procurement Team <procurement@acme-analytics.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 3 Sep 2026 16:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Phased commit & credit milestones for VPC deployment — pricing structure

Jillian / Tom — thanks for the quick turn. Summarizing what we can confirm today so you can route to CFO:

1) Pilot credit: $25k applied as an upfront credit against your first invoice (non-refundable).
2) Platform fee: waived for pilot (months 1–3). For months 4–9 Redwood will cap platform fee at $12,000/month provided Acme signs a firm 9‑month committed volume for Phase 2 by 2026‑09‑10.
3) Reconciliation: if any ramp month exceeds committed tokens by >25% we reserve the right to reprice that month at the agreed overage rate after a 30‑day notice and provide a credit offset plan if required.
4) Audit & SOC2: attached is the SOC2 scope statement and Redwood’s proposed evidence delivery process (secure share + redacted artifacts). No direct sysadmin access — evidence-only.

Next steps: I attached the updated term-sheet (term-sheet-acme-vpc.pdf) and a refreshed MAP (MAP-v3.xlsx). If Tom is comfortable, please confirm and I’ll send the final redline to sign. If you want a short call to walk through the reconciliation mechanics we can do a 20‑minute slot early next week.

Thanks — looking forward to getting this across the finish line.

Best,
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.ai | +1 415‑555‑0123

Attachments: term-sheet-acme-vpc.pdf, MAP-v3.xlsx

source.subject: Temporary BYO/KMS retention exemption — HorizonHealth coordination
messages:
From: Tim Keller <tim.keller@horizonhealth.com>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 30 Aug 2026 14:03:00 -0700
Subject: Request: short-term BYOK retention exemption for pilot

Hi Jasmine,

Thanks again for the demo earlier. One practical item that came up in our legal/security review: for the pilot we need a short-term retention window for KMS-wrapped keys and associated audit evidence that is outside our usual retention policy (30 days instead of 7) to support a clinical QA run next week.

Specifically: can Redwood provide a scoped exception for HorizonHealth's pilot environment to retain key wrapping audit logs and associated request/response traces for 30 days, and export the evidence bundle on-demand? Security wants to see proof of exportability and controls (who/what can request export).

Attached is our formal request (horizon_byo_request.pdf) and an initial list of artifacts they expect (evidence_dump.csv). Please advise what the execution model would look like and timelines — we're hoping to start the QA run on Sep 2.

Appreciate the help,
Tim

--
Tim Keller
VP, Integrations | HorizonHealth

From: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 30 Aug 2026 15:12:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Request: short-term BYOK retention exemption for pilot

Ben / Priya — forwarding Horizon's ask. TL;DR: pilot needs 30-day retention window for BYOK-wrapped audit logs + on-demand export of evidence bundle; start target Sep 2. Attachments included.

Questions for triage:
- Technical feasibility: can Dedicated/Private environments apply a per-tenant retention override for audit logs and KMS wrapping metadata for 30 days? What about Hosted with customer-managed keys?
- Security controls: what access controls and approval flow do we need to satisfy their requirement for "exportability" (audit trail of who requested exports + signed evidence)?
- Product impact: configuration surface to expose (UI/API) and risk of this becoming a broader product exception.

Proposed immediate next steps: quick internal triage today (Ben/Priya), propose a scoped runbook we can share with Horizon by EOD. If we can do a manual export flow for the pilot that meets their ask, we can follow up on automation later.

Thanks,
Jasmine

--
Jasmine Liu
Senior Sales Engineer | Redwood Inference

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 30 Aug 2026 18:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Request: short-term BYOK retention exemption for pilot

Thanks Jasmine — quick notes from infra/compliance perspective after a rapid triage with Priya: 

1) Feasibility: For Dedicated and Private we can implement a tenant-scoped retention override for audit logs and KMS metadata (we already have per-tenant retention buckets in the audit pipeline). For Hosted (customer-managed keys) we'd need a scoped policy change — technically doable but higher review.

2) Controls & export: We can support on-demand evidence export as an authenticated, RBAC-checked operation. Export will generate a signed bundle (sha256 manifest) and a triggered audit record with requestor identity and IP. That satisfies the "who/what requested export" ask. We should require a ticket/approval (internal) and log the approval as part of the bundle metadata.

3) Timeline & manual runbook: We can put a manual fallback in place for the pilot (Dedicated/private):
   - Day 0 (today): create a per-tenant retention policy in the audit ingestion layer scoped to 30 days for Horizon pilot.
   - Day 1: test export flow to produce signed bundle.
   - Day 2: hand off runbook to Horizon for on-demand requests.

4) Risks & productization: This would be treated as a temporary exception (pilot-only). If we see demand, product should plan a small feature to model per-tenant retention exceptions and an approvals UI. For now we keep it scoped and timebound (30 days).

Action items assigned: Priya to draft approval runbook and evidence manifest template; Grace to validate audit pipeline changes and confirm signed bundle format; I will prepare a one-page compliance note for Horizon with temporary SLA and acceptance criteria.

Ben

--
Ben Carter
Senior Engineering Manager, Infra | Redwood Inference

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
To: Tim Keller <tim.keller@horizonhealth.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 31 Aug 2026 10:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: short-term BYOK retention exemption for pilot — plan and next steps

Tim / Horizon security team — thanks for the clear ask and the attachments. We can support the pilot with a scoped, timebound exception as outlined below. Keeping this short and actionable: 

What we will deliver (pilot scope):
- A tenant-scoped retention override of 30 days for audit logs and KMS wrapping metadata in the pilot environment (Dedicated) starting Aug 31 — Sep 30.
- An on-demand evidence export flow that produces a signed bundle (sha256 manifest) containing the requested artifacts. Export will be gated by RBAC and an approval ticket.
- A documented runbook that records who requested the export, the approver, and the export checksum.

Requirements from Horizon:
- A named point-of-contact and the list of personnel authorized to request an evidence export (email addresses).
- Confirmation that pilot will run in the Dedicated pilot cluster (not hosted multi-tenant).
- A short approval email thread that we can attach to the export bundle as proof-of-approval.

Logistics & timeline:
- Today (Aug 31): we will enable the retention override in the pilot cluster and run an internal export test.
- Sep 1: we will share the signed bundle test and the runbook for your security team to validate.
- Sep 2: QA run should be supported; please escalate immediately if any gaps.

Attachments: horizon_byo_request.pdf, evidence_dump.csv (we reviewed both).

If that aligns, please send the POC list and confirm the environment. We will also open a short-lived Jira (INF-1298) to track the work and close it by Sep 30.

Thanks — Priya

--
Priya Natarajan
Product Manager, Security & Compliance | Redwood Inference

source.subject: MAP: Fabrx evaluation assets — one-week sandbox & return
messages:
From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
To: Lina Gomez <lina.gomez@fabrx.com>, Tom Hale <tom.hale@fabrx.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 24 Aug 2026 14:05:00 -0700
Subject: MAP: Fabrx evaluation assets — one-week sandbox & return

Hi Lina / Tom,

Thanks again for the conversation today — good alignment on scope. Attached is a draft mutual action plan (MAP) that lays out the proposed handoff of evaluation assets (prompt pack + testcases + a small sample dataset) into a one-week ephemeral sandbox. Key asks here are:

- 1-week sandbox (start window: first business day after signature)
- Data residency: US-only sandbox (we'll provision in us-west)
- Usage constraints: no re-sharing, no production training; model outputs can be used for internal evaluation only
- Post-sandbox: Fabrx deletes assets and provides a signed confirmation (Redwood will provide a simple destruction confirmation template)

Please review the timeline in the attached PDF and confirm any compliance needs we should account for. I also included an eval_samples_list.csv with the specific files we'd exchange so we can lock access controls.

Attachments: [Redwood-Fabrx-MAP-draft.pdf], [eval_samples_list.csv] 

If that looks reasonable, can you confirm availability for the runway below so we can include dates in the MAP?

- Sign-off by Fri 2026-08-28
- Asset handoff Mon 2026-08-31 (provisioning and access)
- Sandbox closes Mon 2026-09-07 (assets removed)

Quick note on security: we can gate access behind a short-lived token and require IP allowlist; if you need a more formal checklist, Karthik is the best point.

Thanks,

Hannah Schmitt
Senior Solutions Manager | Redwood Inference
[hannah.schmitt@redwood.com]

From: Lina Gomez <lina.gomez@fabrx.com>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
Cc: Tom Hale <tom.hale@fabrx.com>
Date: Tue, 25 Aug 2026 09:22:00 -0500
Subject: Re: MAP: Fabrx evaluation assets — one-week sandbox & return

Hannah — thanks for the doc and the clear timeline. A couple quick clarifications before we sign: 

1) Usage constraints: Fabrx legal wants explicit language that Redwood will not retain a copy beyond the sandbox period and that model vendors used during eval cannot ingest the dataset for training. Is Redwood comfortable adding that clause?

2) Sample set size: the eval_samples_list.csv looks fine but we'll need to redact any PII before handoff. We'll do that on our side and upload a sanitized version. Can you confirm the exact acceptance checklist so our QA knows what to produce?

3) On the security questionnaire: we'll need Azure AD SSO integration details and the encryption-in-transit assurances. Tom and I will loop in our security lead if needed.

Assuming those items are addressed we can target Fri 2026-08-28 for sign-off.

source.subject: Demo: GitHub integration and repo-scanning workflow
messages:
From: Alex Murphy <alex@opensource-lab.io>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 22 Aug 2026 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Demo request — GitHub integration questions

Hi Jordan,

I signed up for Redwood's trial this morning after reading about the GitHub integration on your docs site. We're an open-source security tooling team (OpenSource Lab) and I'm evaluating whether Redwood can power an automated code-scanning assistant tied to our repos.

Quick questions before a demo:
- Does Redwood support webhook-based repo events (push/PR) or only polling?
- Any special permissions required for organization-level installs vs repo-level installs?
- Do you support self-hosted runners or on-prem agents for private repos?

Would like a short walkthrough and to see how embedding into a CI pipeline might look. Attaching our repo README for context.

Attachment: oslab-repo-README.md (text)

Thanks,
Alex Murphy
Engineering Manager — OpenSource Lab
alex@opensource-lab.io
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Murphy <alex@opensource-lab.io>
Cc: Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 22 Aug 2026 10:02:18 -0700
Subject: Re: Demo request — GitHub integration questions

Hi Alex — great to meet you and thanks for signing up. I can run a quick 30-min demo focused on GitHub integration and CI pipeline flow. Maya (cc'ed) is our solutions engineer and will join to cover technical details.

Are you available for one of the following windows next week (I can also send a Calendly link):
- Tue 2026-08-25 10:00-10:30 PT
- Tue 2026-08-25 13:00-13:30 PT
- Wed 2026-08-26 09:00-09:30 PT

A couple quick asks to prepare the demo:
- Could you paste a public/private repo link (we can request read-only access if needed)
- Which cloud or on-prem CI do you use (GitHub Actions, Jenkins, GitLab runners)?

I'll send a calendar invite once you confirm a slot. If you'd rather pick a time, use this link: https://calendly.com/redwood-demo/30min

Best,
Jordan Blake
Sales Development Representative | Redwood Inference
jordan.blake@redwood.ai
From: Alex Murphy <alex@opensource-lab.io>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 22 Aug 2026 12:15:42 -04:00
Subject: Re: Demo request — GitHub integration questions

Thanks Jordan — we can do Tue 2026-08-25 13:00 PT (that's 16:00 ET for me). Our core repo is https://github.com/opensource-lab/oscanner — README attached previously.

Answers to your prep Qs:
- We primarily use GitHub Actions; we do have a couple of Jenkins pipelines for legacy infra.
- We'd prefer org-level install if possible for convenience but concerned about permission scope.
- We do not currently run any on-prem agents; some repos are private and we'd like to discuss options for keeping tokens scoped.

Two quick technical asks for the demo: could Maya cover the permission model for org installs and whether Redwood supports event-driven webhooks vs polling? Also, do you have any docs about embedding the model inference into Actions as a step?

Cheers,
Alex

On Sat, Aug 22, 2026 at 10:02 AM Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Hi Alex — great to meet you and thanks for signing up. I can run a quick 30-min demo focused on GitHub integration and CI pipeline flow. Maya (cc'ed) is our solutions engineer and will join to cover technical details.
>
> Are you available for one of the following windows next week...
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Murphy <alex@opensource-lab.io>
Cc: Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 22 Aug 2026 15:40:05 -0700
Subject: Re: Demo request — GitHub integration questions

Thanks, Alex — locking in Tue 2026-08-25 13:00 PT (16:00 ET). Calendar invite sent separately; please accept when you get it.

Agenda (30 mins):
- 5m: Introductions and goals
- 10m: Maya demos GitHub integration (org vs repo install, permission scopes)
- 10m: Walkthrough of GitHub Actions integration and example workflow
- 5m: Next steps (trial configuration, POC checklist)

Maya, Priya — tagging you both for prep. Priya from Solutions Ops will share a short POC checklist and any required docs (SOC, token scopes) ahead of time.

Attachment: calendar-invite.ics (stub)

Jordan Blake
Sales Development Representative | Redwood Inference
From: Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Murphy <alex@opensource-lab.io>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 25 Aug 2026 08:22:00 -0700
Subject: Prep for today's demo — oscanner repo review

Hi Alex — looking forward to our call later today. Quick prep notes so we make the most of 30 minutes:

1) Access: if the oscanner repo is private, please add maya.srinivasan@redwood.ai (read-only) so I can demo live. If you prefer not to add a user, a short screen-share of the Actions workflow will work.
2) Permissions: I will cover org install scopes vs repo-level permissions and an approach to least-privilege tokens.
3) Webhooks vs polling: Redwood supports both patterns — I'll show event-driven triggers (recommended) and fallback polling options for environments where webhooks are restricted.
4) CI integration: sample GitHub Actions step snippet included below for embedding an inference call; we can tailor it to your pipeline during the call.

Sample step:
- name: Run Redwood scan
  uses: actions/checkout@v3
  run: |
    curl -X POST https://api.redwood.ai/v1/scan \\n      -H 'Authorization: Bearer ${{ secrets.REDWOOD_KEY }}' \\n      -d '{"repo":"https://github.com/opensource-lab/oscanner","ref":"${{ github.sha }}"}'

If you can drop any additional specific repos or workflows you'd like covered, I can prioritize them. See you at 13:00 PT.

— Maya
Maya Srinivasan
Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
maya.srinivasan@redwood.ai

2) Permissions: I will cover org install scopes vs repo-level permissions and an approach to least-privilege tokens.
3) Webhooks vs polling: Redwood supports both patterns — I'll show event-driven triggers (recommended) and fallback polling options for environments where webhooks are restricted.
4) CI integration: sample GitHub Actions step snippet included below for embedding an inference call; we can tailor it to your pipeline during the call.

Sample step:
- name: Run Redwood scan
  uses: actions/checkout@v3
  run: |
    curl -X POST https://api.redwood.ai/v1/scan \
      -H 'Authorization: Bearer ${{ secrets.REDWOOD_KEY }}' \
      -d '{"repo":"https://github.com/opensource-lab/oscanner","ref":"${{ github.sha }}"}'

If you can drop any additional specific repos or workflows you'd like covered, I can prioritize them. See you at 13:00 PT.

— Maya
Maya Srinivasan
Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
maya.srinivasan@redwood.ai

source.subject: Action required: complete supplier activation for Lumen Fabrications
messages:
From: Thomas Rey <onboarding@lumenfab.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 15 Aug 2026 09:37:00 -0700
Subject: Supplier activation failing on mobile

Hi Ava,

We tried to complete the supplier activation link you sent this morning but the button on the activation page doesn't appear on our mobile webview (Android Chrome). Tapping the blank area does nothing. I attached a screenshot.

We can complete from desktop for now, but our AP team mostly works from tablets while on site and this will block our PO acknowledgements later this week. Can you resend a link or suggest a workaround?

Thanks,
Thomas Rey
Head of AP, Lumen Fabrications
onboarding@lumenfab.com
Attachment: screenshot-cta-hidden.png (image/png, 248 KB)
From: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.ai>
To: Thomas Rey <onboarding@lumenfab.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 15 Aug 2026 10:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Supplier activation failing on mobile

Hi Thomas — thanks for the heads up and the screenshot.

Short answer: we saw a small subset of activation links render an empty CTA on certain Android WebViews when the deep-link parameter string exceeded ~1200 characters. That happens when a long return_url + campaign tracking + marketplace params are included. We pushed a mitigation last week but it looks like your link still had a long param set.

Workarounds:
- Open the link in a desktop browser (what you noted)
- Long-term: I'll reissue a trimmed activation link (no campaign params) and paste the direct deep-link token in the email body so mobile clients don't drop the button.

I'll re-send now and loop in Ben for an ops follow-up to ensure our link generator strips non-essential tracking for supplier activation flows.

Ava Chen
Enterprise Onboarding (Redwood Inference)
--
On Sat, Aug 15, 2026 at 09:37 AM Thomas Rey <onboarding@lumenfab.com> wrote:
> Hi Ava,
> 
> We tried to complete the supplier activation link you sent this morning but the button on the activation page doesn't appear on our mobile webview (Android Chrome).
> 
> Thanks,
> Thomas
From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.ai>
Cc: Thomas Rey <onboarding@lumenfab.com>
Date: Sun, 16 Aug 2026 08:21:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Supplier activation failing on mobile

Hi team,

I dug into the generator and confirmed the issue: the link builder appended both hb_campaign_id and ga_client_id for marketing attribution. On small-screen WebViews the layout engine treated the long querystring as part of the inline button rendering, causing the CTA to flow off-canvas.

Planned fixes:
1) Short-term patch to strip non-required query params for activation emails (deploy tomorrow morning).
2) Telemetry: add a hit to capture activation page client type and render errors so we can proactively detect regressions.

Ava — can you resend the trimmed activation link to Thomas now? I'll open a ticket (SUP-8123) to track the patch and include a regression test against Android embedded WebView flows.

Ben
Customer Ops
Attachment: onboarding-instructions.pdf (application/pdf, 112 KB)
From: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.ai>
To: Thomas Rey <onboarding@lumenfab.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 17 Aug 2026 11:12:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Supplier activation failing on mobile

Thomas — new trimmed activation link below. This one removes tracking parameters and should render the CTA correctly on mobile webviews. Please tap the link from your mobile device and confirm success. If it still fails, reply and we'll set up a quick screen-share.

Activation link: https://redwood.app/activate?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9--shorttoken123

Notes:
- Attachment: onboarding-instructions.pdf explains the AP/PO flow and how to add our billing email to your vendor master so POs route correctly.
- We've filed SUP-8123 to make the fix permanent. Priya, can you add the Android WebView regression to the automation backlog?

Thanks — let me know when you're activated and I'll confirm PO visibility from our side.

Best,
Ava Chen
Enterprise Onboarding (Redwood Inference)
Phone: +1 415-555-0192
--
On Sun, Aug 16, 2026 at 08:21 AM Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Hi team,
> 
> I dug into the generator and confirmed the issue...
> 
> Ben
