source.subject: Request: Hawthorne SIG addendum for extended debug-log retention
messages:
From: Elena Rivera <elena.rivera@hawthornehealth.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 4 Aug 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Request: Hawthorne SIG addendum for extended debug-log retention

Hi Jordan / Priya,

Thanks for the quick onboarding call last week — our SOC2 team asked us to follow up with a formal request. Hawthorne needs an exception to Redwood's standard debug-log retention for one of our regulated product lines (telemetry pipeline for device telemetry). Specifically, we need debug-level traces retained for 18 months instead of the default 90 days.

Key context:
- Logs in scope: full debug traces (application + request/response headers) and ephemeral diagnostics tied to tenant IDs (no bulk PII, but may include IPs and device identifiers).
- Reason: state regulatory audit and forensic timeline requirements for device incidents.
- Desired effective date: as soon as possible; legal sign-off target is EOD Aug 10.

Can Redwood provide the following:
1) Confirmation whether a customer-specific retention addendum (DPA amendment) is supported and a short statement of controls (encrypt-at-rest, access audit trail) we can attach to our SIG/CAIQ submission.
2) Any draft addendum or standard verbiage we can circulate with our legal team.
3) Technical feasibility notes (how retention is segmented, who can access the retained debug logs, region constraints).

I've attached our formal request form for the compliance folder (Hawthorne_Retention_Request_Form.pdf) and copied Hawthorne Contracts on the thread. Please advise next steps and whether you need a short call before Aug 10.

Thanks,
Elena Rivera
Director, Compliance Operations
Hawthorne Health

Attachment: Hawthorne_Retention_Request_Form.pdf

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
To: Elena Rivera <elena.rivera@hawthornehealth.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 4 Aug 2026 11:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: Hawthorne SIG addendum for extended debug-log retention

Elena — thanks for the clear request and the attached form. Looping Priya (security/compliance) to lead the controls and legal ask. I'll also bring in our solutions lead (Markus) for the tech approach — is it OK if I add him to the thread?

A couple quick clarifying Qs so we can scope properly:
- Can you confirm whether the logs may contain any customer-supplied PII beyond IP/device IDs (e.g., user-submitted notes or free-text fields)?
- Is the retention requirement tenant-scoped (only for Hawthorne tenants) or do you expect this to apply across multiple Hawthorne product lines / sub-accounts?
- Any region requirement? (US-only vs EU data residency).

If it's helpful I can propose a short task list and timeline after we hear from Priya and Markus — from experience this usually needs (a) legal addendum, (b) scoped storage config, and (c) ops runbook for delete/restore and audit demonstration. Happy to set a 30-min slot tomorrow if that accelerates approvals.

Best,
Jordan Blake
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference
[jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com]
Mobile: +1 (615) 555-0192

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>, Elena Rivera <elena.rivera@hawthornehealth.com>
Cc: 
Date: Wed, 5 Aug 2026 08:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: Hawthorne SIG addendum for extended debug-log retention

Hi Elena / Jordan,

Thanks — this is a common ask and we can support a customer-specific retention addendum, but there are compliance and operational guardrails we must observe. High level:

Controls we require for approval:
- Legal: signed DPA amendment / retention addendum signed by both parties (we provide draft language).
- Access controls: retained debug logs must be accessible only to a defined list of roles and authenticated via SSO + privileged access logging.
- Encryption: data must be encrypted at rest using customer-specific KMS keys if the tenant requests key separation; otherwise Redwood-managed KMS with audited key rotation is acceptable.
- Auditability: we must be able to produce an export showing who accessed the retention bucket and when for any forensic request.
- Scope: we prefer tenant-scoped retention (Hawthorne-only) to avoid cross-tenant entanglement.

Artifacts we can supply quickly:
- Redwood SIG matrix export (redwood_SIG_matrix_v2.xlsx) and CAIQ JSON extract.
- Template retention addendum (we can provide a Word doc with placeholders).

Questions for Hawthorne:
1) Please confirm the exact data types in-scope (especially if free-text user content could be present).
2) Confirm desired key management model: Redwood-managed KMS or customer-provided key (BYOK).
3) Confirm region/residency constraints.

Operational note to Jordan / Markus: if Hawthorne wants hosted (Redwood API) support for 18-month retention, we'll need a per-tenant storage segregation plan (dedicated bucket or object prefix + lifecycle policies) and a short maintenance window to apply the config. Markus, can you outline the least-risk implementation pattern and an ETA?

I'll prepare the SIG/CAIQ artifacts and a draft addendum once we confirm the above.

—Priya
Priya Natarajan
Security & Compliance Manager | Redwood Inference
priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com

Attachment placeholder: redwood_SIG_matrix_v2.xlsx (export ready on request)

From: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Elena Rivera <elena.rivera@hawthornehealth.com>
Date: Fri, 6 Aug 2026 16:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: Hawthorne SIG addendum for extended debug-log retention

Elena / Priya / Jordan — technical feasibility and proposed implementation below. TL;DR: feasible for hosted customers with additional contract + per-tenant storage isolation; ~7 business days of engineering work to provision and validate.

Proposed minimal-risk approach (hosted):
1) Per-tenant write path: debug traces for Hawthorne routed to a tenant-specific object prefix in a dedicated S3 bucket (or customer-provided bucket if BYOK is required).
2) Retention policy: lifecycle rule set to 18 months; we will implement a tombstone + delete job with audit logging.
3) Access controls: restrict bucket IAM to a Hawthorne-admin role and a narrow set of Redwood ops roles; all access forwarded to audit logs (immutable access log retention for X months).
4) Encryption: support for customer KMS key (preferred) or Redwood-managed KMS.
5) Data egress for audits: ad-hoc exports via secure transfer; data redaction tooling available if free-text PII surfaces.

Estimated deliverables and timeline:
- Day 0–2: legal addendum draft + compliance artifacts (Priya).
- Day 1–5: engineering tasks — create bucket/prefix, lifecycle rule, access policy, and diagnostics collection.
- Day 5–7: validation, ops runbook, and evidence pack for SIG/CAIQ.

Constraints / risks:
- If Hawthorne requires cross-region residency (e.g., EU), add 2–3 days for infra provisioning and legal review.
- If logs include customer-controlled PII that requires redaction before retention, we will need to scope transformation work (additional ETA).

Attachments: system_log_retention_diagram.png (high-level flow), retention_addendum_draft.docx (initial legal language).

Next steps I recommend:
1) Jordan: confirm scope (types and tenant coverage) and whether Hawthorne will use BYOK.

title: Request: Hawthorne SIG addendum for extended debug-log retention
preview:
From: Elena Rivera <elena.rivera@hawthornehealth.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 4 Aug 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Request: Hawthorne SIG addendum for extended debug-log retention

Hi Jordan / Priya,

Thanks for the quick onboarding call last week — our SOC2 team asked us to follow up with a formal request. Hawthorne needs an exception to Redwood's standard debug-log retention for one of our regulated product lines (telemetry pipeline for device telemetry). Specifically, we need debug-level traces retained for 18 months instead of the default 90 days.

Key context:
- Logs in scope: full debug traces (application + request/response headers) and ephemeral diagnostics tied to tenant IDs (no bulk PII, but may include IPs and device identifiers).
- Reason: state regulatory audit and forensic timeline requirements for device incidents.
- Desired effective date: as soon as possible; legal sign-off target is EOD Aug 10.

Can Redwood provide the following:
1) Confirmation whether a customer-specific retention addendum (DPA amendment) is supported and a short statement of controls (encrypt-at-rest, access audit trail) we can attach to our SIG/CAIQ submission.
2) Any draft addendum or standard verbiage we can circulate with our legal team.
3) Technical feasibility notes (how retention is segmented, who can access the retained debug logs, region constraints).

I've attached our formal request form for the compliance folder (Hawthorne_Retention_Request_Form.pdf) and copied Hawthorne Contracts on the thread. Please advise next steps and whether you need a short call before Aug 10.

Thanks,
Elena Rivera
Director, Compliance Operations
Hawthorne Health

Attachment: Hawthorne_Retention_Request_Form.pdf

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
To: Elena Rivera <elena.rivera@hawthornehealth.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 4 Aug 2026 11:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: Hawthorne SIG addendum for extended debug-log retention

Elena — thanks for the clear request and the attached form. Looping Priya (security/compliance) to lead the controls and legal ask. I'll also bring in our solutions lead (Markus) for the tech approach — is it OK if I add him to the thread?

A couple quick clarifying Qs so we can scope properly:
- Can you confirm whether the logs may contain any customer-supplied PII beyond IP/device IDs (e.g., user-submitted notes or free-text fields)?
- Is the retention requirement tenant-scoped (only for Hawthorne tenants) or do you expect this to apply across multiple Hawthorne product lines / sub-accounts?
- Any region requirement? (US-only vs EU data residency).

If it's helpful I can propose a short task list and timeline after we hear from Priya and Markus — from experience this usually needs (a) legal addendum, (b) scoped storage config, and (c) ops runbook for delete/restore and audit demonstration. Happy to set a 30-min slot tomorrow if that accelerates approvals.

Best,
Jordan Blake
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference
[jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com]
Mobile: +1 (615) 555-0192

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>, Elena Rivera <elena.rivera@hawthornehealth.com>
Cc: 
Date: Wed, 5 Aug 2026 08:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: Hawthorne SIG addendum for extended debug-log retention

Hi Elena / Jordan,

Thanks — this is a common ask and we can support a customer-specific retention addendum, but there are compliance and operational guardrails we must observe. High level:

Controls we require for approval:
- Legal: signed DPA amendment / retention addendum signed by both parties (we provide draft language).
- Access controls: retained debug logs must be accessible only to a defined list of roles and authenticated via SSO + privileged access logging.
- Encryption: data must be encrypted at rest using customer-specific KMS keys if the tenant requests key separation; otherwise Redwood-managed KMS with audited key rotation is acceptable.
- Auditability: we must be able to produce an export showing who accessed the retention bucket and when for any forensic request.
- Scope: we prefer tenant-scoped retention (Hawthorne-only) to avoid cross-tenant entanglement.

Artifacts we can supply quickly:
- Redwood SIG matrix export (redwood_SIG_matrix_v2.xlsx) and CAIQ JSON extract.
- Template retention addendum (we can provide a Word doc with placeholders).

Questions for Hawthorne:
1) Please confirm the exact data types in-scope (especially if free-text user content could be present).
2) Confirm desired key management model: Redwood-managed KMS or customer-provided key (BYOK).
3) Confirm region/residency constraints.

Operational note to Jordan / Markus: if Hawthorne wants hosted (Redwood API) support for 18-month retention, we'll need a per-tenant storage segregation plan (dedicated bucket or object prefix + lifecycle policies) and a short maintenance window to apply the config. Markus, can you outline the least-risk implementation pattern and an ETA?

I'll prepare the SIG/CAIQ artifacts and a draft addendum once we confirm the above.

—Priya
Priya Natarajan
Security & Compliance Manager | Redwood Inference
priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com

Attachment placeholder: redwood_SIG_matrix_v2.xlsx (export ready on request)

From: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Elena Rivera <elena.rivera@hawthornehealth.com>
Date: Fri, 6 Aug 2026 16:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: Hawthorne SIG addendum for extended debug-log retention

Elena / Priya / Jordan — technical feasibility and proposed implementation below. TL;DR: feasible for hosted customers with additional contract + per-tenant storage isolation; ~7 business days of engineering work to provision and validate.

Proposed minimal-risk approach (hosted):
1) Per-tenant write path: debug traces for Hawthorne routed to a tenant-specific object prefix in a dedicated S3 bucket (or customer-provided bucket if BYOK is required).
2) Retention policy: lifecycle rule set to 18 months; we will implement a tombstone + delete job with audit logging.
3) Access controls: restrict bucket IAM to a Hawthorne-admin role and a narrow set of Redwood ops roles; all access forwarded to audit logs (immutable access log retention for X months).
4) Encryption: support for customer KMS key (preferred) or Redwood-managed KMS.
5) Data egress for audits: ad-hoc exports via secure transfer; data redaction tooling available if free-text PII surfaces.

Estimated deliverables and timeline:
- Day 0–2: legal addendum draft + compliance artifacts (Priya).
- Day 1–5: engineering tasks — create bucket/prefix, lifecycle rule, access policy, and diagnostics collection.
- Day 5–7: validation, ops runbook, and evidence pack for SIG/CAIQ.

Constraints / risks:
- If Hawthorne requires cross-region residency (e.g., EU), add 2–3 days for infra provisioning and legal review.
- If logs include customer-controlled PII that requires redaction before retention, we will need to scope transformation work (additional ETA).

Attachments: system_log_retention_diagram.png (high-level flow), retention_addendum_draft.docx (initial legal language).

Next steps I recommend:
1) Jordan: confirm scope (types and tenant coverage) and whether Hawthorne will use BYOK.
2) Elena: if timeline Aug 10 is firm for legal sign-off, we'll prioritize the addendum draft and aim to have the engineering runbook ready within 7 business days after sign-off.

Happy to jump on a short call to walk through the diagram.

Regards,
Markus Klein
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
markus.klein@redwoodinference.com

Attachments (stubs): system_log_retention_diagram.png, retention_addendum_draft.docx

source.subject: Re: DPA redlines — conditional onshore processing & backup wipe language
messages:
From: Jane Sullivan <jane.sullivan@aurumhealth.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>; Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Cc: legal@aurumhealth.com
Date: 2026-08-02T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: DPA redlines — conditional onshore processing & backup wipe language

Marcus / Priya —

Thanks for taking a look at our requested redlines. We attached Aurum's annotated DPA (Aurum_DPA_Redline_v2.docx). Our primary asks are narrow but important for our compliance team:

- A clear onshore processing carve-out when data must be processed in the US for regulatory reasons; this needs to be explicit rather than implied.
- A firm retention limit for primary data (30 days after deletion request) and a defined maximum for backups (we propose 90 days) with a requirement for certificate-of-destruction upon final purge.
- Escrow/forensic access to immutable logs for 12 months but only under subpoena or audit with a documented process.
- Assurance that backups are encrypted with customer-controlled keys (BYOK) when requested.

Can you confirm whether Redwood is able to accept these or propose a narrow compromise? We are trying to get this wrapped before our procurement committee meeting on 8/9.

Thanks,
Jane
Jane Sullivan
Sr. Counsel, Aurum Health
legal@aurumhealth.com

Attachment: Aurum_DPA_Redline_v2.docx (annotated DPA)
From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Jane Sullivan <jane.sullivan@aurumhealth.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-02T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: DPA redlines — conditional onshore processing & backup wipe language

Jane —

Appreciate the clear asks and the annotated DPA. Looping in Neha and Karthik from our legal/privacy team to respond to the specifics. High level: we can support an onshore processing clause with conditions, and we have BYOK options for Private deployments — we just need to align on the operational details for backups and timelines.

Next steps we suggest: 
1) Neha will propose language that preserves your 30-day primary deletion but gives Redwood a limited backup retention carve-out for disaster recovery.
2) We can agree to produce a certificate of destruction for backups upon final purge where applicable.
3) For logs/escrow access, we can define an audit-request flow (SOC2 evidence first, then preserved logs provided under NDA and legal process).

We aim to have concrete proposed text by EOD 8/3 so your procurement team has time to review.

—Marcus
Marcus Lin
Head of Sales, Redwood Inference
marcus.lin@redwood.com

Attachments: Redwood_Sec_Overview.pdf, SOC2-TypeII-2025.pdf
From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
To: Jane Sullivan <jane.sullivan@aurumhealth.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-03T09:26:00-07:00
Subject: Re: DPA redlines — proposed compromise language

Jane —

Thanks for the detail. Below is proposed compromise language (extracts) that balances Aurum's operational needs and Redwood's multi-tenant and disaster-recovery constraints. If this looks acceptable we can fold it into the DPA and circulate for signature.

1) Onshore processing: 
"Customer may require that, for regulatory reasons, certain processing of Customer Data occur on compute located in the United States. Redwood will use commercially reasonable efforts to route such processing to onshore capacity upon Customer's written request; where onshore routing is not available, Redwood will notify Customer and propose a timeline and mitigation plan."

2) Primary deletion: "Redwood will delete Customer's primary copies of Customer Data within 30 days of Customer's deletion request. Deletion confirmation will be provided in writing."

3) Backups and disaster recovery: "Backups are retained for disaster recovery purposes and will be purged no later than 120 days after deletion request. If Customer requests BYOK for backups, Redwood will enable encryption with Customer-managed keys and will coordinate key rotation and access controls. Redwood will provide a certificate of destruction within 30 days of final backup purge upon Customer's written request."

4) Logs and escrow access: "Redwood will retain immutable operational logs for 12 months. Access to logs or escrowed forensic artifacts will be provided only pursuant to a court order, audit notice, or mutually agreed process; Redwood will make available SOC2 artifacts and cooperate with audit requests under NDA."

Rationale: we moved the backup maximum to 120 days (from your 90 days) to allow for restore windows and cross-region recoverability; we commit to certificate-of-destruction and BYOK where requested. If 120 days is a blocker we can discuss a shorter backup window with additional operational constraints (e.g., region-limited backups, expedited purge for high-risk datasets).

Please advise whether Aurum can accept 120 days or if they require 90 days hard limit. If 90 days is non-negotiable, we will need to agree precise onshore-only backup scoping (e.g., backups limited to customer-dedicated pools).

—Neha
Neha Kapoor
Senior Counsel, Privacy & Compliance
neha.kapoor@redwood.com
From: Jane Sullivan <jane.sullivan@aurumhealth.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-04T08:41:00-07:00
Subject: Re: DPA redlines — Aurum response to compromise

Neha —

Thanks for the clear explanation and proposed text. Aurum's compliance team is sympathetic to DR needs, but 120 days for backups is a hard stretch for us. Two counter-proposals from our side (pick one):

A) Accept Redwood's 120-day backup window provided that (i) backups for Aurum are limited to US-only onshore storage and to customer-dedicated pools; and (ii) Redwood provides a certificate of destruction within 7 business days of purge.

B) Keep backups at 90 days but allow Redwood to keep a single, encrypted, cross-customer disaster snapshot for up to 180 days strictly for platform-level restore (not customer-data specific), with documented segmentation and an audit right.

We prefer A. If Redwood can agree to (A) we will drop the 90-day requirement and move forward to signatures.

Appreciate the quick turn — procurement deadline still 8/9.

Jane
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Jane Sullivan <jane.sullivan@aurumhealth.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-05T17:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: DPA redlines — final confirmation and next steps

Jane —

Thanks, all. We can accept option A with the following operational commitments to be added to the DPA appendix: 
- Backups for Aurum will be stored only within US regions and within customer-dedicated pools (Private/Dedicated customers) when Aurum elects Dedicated or Private deployment. For Hosted customers, Redwood will encrypt and regionally isolate backups to US regions and provide documented segmentation procedures.
- Certificate of destruction for backups will be issued within 7 business days after the final purge request.
- BYOK will be available for backups for Private/Dedicated deployments; for Hosted we will offer customer-managed keys via supported KMS integrations and document key-rotation policies.
- We will include the exact onshore routing request process in the appendix and add an audit/cooperation clause referencing SOC2 artifacts and an NDA-driven forensic access flow.

If this is acceptable, Priya will circulate the redlined appendix and a DocuSign link for an amendment. We will also attach SOC2 reports and the operational runbook.

Priya — please send the HubSpot deal link and amendment.

—Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Director, Privacy Engineering
karthik.iyer@redwood.com

To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-04T08:41:00-07:00
Subject: Re: DPA redlines — Aurum response to compromise

Neha —

Thanks for the clear explanation and proposed text. Aurum's compliance team is sympathetic to DR needs, but 120 days for backups is a hard stretch for us. Two counter-proposals from our side (pick one):

A) Accept Redwood's 120-day backup window provided that (i) backups for Aurum are limited to US-only onshore storage and to customer-dedicated pools; and (ii) Redwood provides a certificate of destruction within 7 business days of purge.

B) Keep backups at 90 days but allow Redwood to keep a single, encrypted, cross-customer disaster snapshot for up to 180 days strictly for platform-level restore (not customer-data specific), with documented segmentation and an audit right.

We prefer A. If Redwood can agree to (A) we will drop the 90-day requirement and move forward to signatures.

Appreciate the quick turn — procurement deadline still 8/9.

Jane


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Jane Sullivan <jane.sullivan@aurumhealth.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-05T17:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: DPA redlines — final confirmation and next steps

Jane —

Thanks, all. We can accept option A with the following operational commitments to be added to the DPA appendix: 
- Backups for Aurum will be stored only within US regions and within customer-dedicated pools (Private/Dedicated customers) when Aurum elects Dedicated or Private deployment. For Hosted customers, Redwood will encrypt and regionally isolate backups to US regions and provide documented segmentation procedures.
- Certificate of destruction for backups will be issued within 7 business days after the final purge request.
- BYOK will be available for backups for Private/Dedicated deployments; for Hosted we will offer customer-managed keys via supported KMS integrations and document key-rotation policies.
- We will include the exact onshore routing request process in the appendix and add an audit/cooperation clause referencing SOC2 artifacts and an NDA-driven forensic access flow.

If this is acceptable, Priya will circulate the redlined appendix and a DocuSign link for an amendment. We will also attach SOC2 reports and the operational runbook.

Priya — please send the HubSpot deal link and amendment.

—Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Director, Privacy Engineering
karthik.iyer@redwood.com
text:
From: Jane Sullivan <jane.sullivan@aurumhealth.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>; Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Cc: legal@aurumhealth.com
Date: 2026-08-02T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: DPA redlines — conditional onshore processing & backup wipe language

Marcus / Priya —

Thanks for taking a look at our requested redlines. We attached Aurum's annotated DPA (Aurum_DPA_Redline_v2.docx). Our primary asks are narrow but important for our compliance team:

- A clear onshore processing carve-out when data must be processed in the US for regulatory reasons; this needs to be explicit rather than implied.
- A firm retention limit for primary data (30 days after deletion request) and a defined maximum for backups (we propose 90 days) with a requirement for certificate-of-destruction upon final purge.
- Escrow/forensic access to immutable logs for 12 months but only under subpoena or audit with a documented process.
- Assurance that backups are encrypted with customer-controlled keys (BYOK) when requested.

Can you confirm whether Redwood is able to accept these or propose a narrow compromise? We are trying to get this wrapped before our procurement committee meeting on 8/9.

Thanks,
Jane
Jane Sullivan
Sr. Counsel, Aurum Health
legal@aurumhealth.com

Attachment: Aurum_DPA_Redline_v2.docx (annotated DPA)


From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Jane Sullivan <jane.sullivan@aurumhealth.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-02T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: DPA redlines — conditional onshore processing & backup wipe language

Jane —

Appreciate the clear asks and the annotated DPA. Looping in Neha and Karthik from our legal/privacy team to respond to the specifics. High level: we can support an onshore processing clause with conditions, and we have BYOK options for Private deployments — we just need to align on the operational details for backups and timelines.

Next steps we suggest: 
1) Neha will propose language that preserves your 30-day primary deletion but gives Redwood a limited backup retention carve-out for disaster recovery.
2) We can agree to produce a certificate of destruction for backups upon final purge where applicable.
3) For logs/escrow access, we can define an audit-request flow (SOC2 evidence first, then preserved logs provided under NDA and legal process).

We aim to have concrete proposed text by EOD 8/3 so your procurement team has time to review.

—Marcus
Marcus Lin
Head of Sales, Redwood Inference
marcus.lin@redwood.com

Attachments: Redwood_Sec_Overview.pdf, SOC2-TypeII-2025.pdf


From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
To: Jane Sullivan <jane.sullivan@aurumhealth.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-03T09:26:00-07:00
Subject: Re: DPA redlines — proposed compromise language

Jane —

Thanks for the detail. Below is proposed compromise language (extracts) that balances Aurum's operational needs and Redwood's multi-tenant and disaster-recovery constraints. If this looks acceptable we can fold it into the DPA and circulate for signature.

1) Onshore processing: 
"Customer may require that, for regulatory reasons, certain processing of Customer Data occur on compute located in the United States. Redwood will use commercially reasonable efforts to route such processing to onshore capacity upon Customer's written request; where onshore routing is not available, Redwood will notify Customer and propose a timeline and mitigation plan."

2) Primary deletion: "Redwood will delete Customer's primary copies of Customer Data within 30 days of Customer's deletion request. Deletion confirmation will be provided in writing."

3) Backups and disaster recovery: "Backups are retained for disaster recovery purposes and will be purged no later than 120 days after deletion request. If Customer requests BYOK for backups, Redwood will enable encryption with Customer-managed keys and will coordinate key rotation and access controls. Redwood will provide a certificate of destruction within 30 days of final backup purge upon Customer's written request."

4) Logs and escrow access: "Redwood will retain immutable operational logs for 12 months. Access to logs or escrowed forensic artifacts will be provided only pursuant to a court order, audit notice, or mutually agreed process; Redwood will make available SOC2 artifacts and cooperate with audit requests under NDA."

Rationale: we moved the backup maximum to 120 days (from your 90 days) to allow for restore windows and cross-region recoverability; we commit to certificate-of-destruction and BYOK where requested. If 120 days is a blocker we can discuss a shorter backup window with additional operational constraints (e.g., region-limited backups, expedited purge for high-risk datasets).

Please advise whether Aurum can accept 120 days or if they require 90 days hard limit. If 90 days is non-negotiable, we will need to agree precise onshore-only backup scoping (e.g., backups limited to customer-dedicated pools).

—Neha
Neha Kapoor
Senior Counsel, Privacy & Compliance
neha.kapoor@redwood.com

source.subject: NorthGrove: portal owner + escrow bank rotation (W-9 & PO implications)
messages:
From: Maya Patel <maya.patel@northgrove.co>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Cc: ap@northgrove.co
Date: Mon, 3 Aug 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Bank rotation + new AP contact — onboarding docs attached

Hi Jordan,

Following our onboarding last month, NorthGrove is switching payment clearing to a new treasury provider and that requires a routing/account update on your vendor record. We've also consolidated AP responsibilities — please update our portal owner to "Maya Patel" (same email).

I've attached the completed W-9 and a signed bank-letter from our treasury provider confirming the new clearing account. We've completed the vendor portal admin transfer in your portal (screenshot attached) and signed the DocuSign we were sent.

A couple of asks so we don't miss upcoming POs:
- Can Redwood confirm the vendor remittance address and the vendor ID you will use after the change?
- Will open POs issued to the old remit account be rerouted automatically or do we need new POs?

Attachments: NorthGrove_W-9_2026.pdf (PDF), clearing-bank-letter_2026-08-03.pdf (PDF), portal-screenshot.png (PNG)

Thanks — happy to hop on a quick call if helpful.

Best,
Maya Patel
Head of Finance Operations
NorthGrove Technologies
maya.patel@northgrove.co
+1 (415) 555-0182

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
To: Maya Patel <maya.patel@northgrove.co>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 3 Aug 2026 09:40:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Bank rotation + new AP contact — onboarding docs attached

Thanks Maya — received, and appreciate the attachments & DocuSign notification. Looping in our AP (Laura) and vendor onboarding (Hannah) to confirm next steps.

Laura / Hannah — NorthGrove (vendor ID supplied in portal: NG-2917) submitted a W-9 and a bank-letter indicating a new clearing account. They also changed the portal owner to Maya. Please advise what additional docs you need and whether existing POs will be affected.

Maya — I'll get back to you with the vendor record update timeline once AP confirms.

Jordan Blake
Senior Procurement Manager
Redwood Inference
jordan.blake@redwood.ai

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Maya Patel <maya.patel@northgrove.co>
Cc: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, finance-team@redwood.ai
Date: Mon, 3 Aug 2026 10:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Bank rotation + new AP contact — onboarding docs attached

Hi all,

Thanks for the heads up, Maya. A few operational items from AP:

1) We need a bank-validated letter with the bank's letterhead and an ACH/Wire verification stamp if this account will accept wires. The clearing-bank-letter you attached looks on point but can you confirm the type (ACH vs wire) and whether the account accepts both?

2) Please confirm the taxpayer name on the W-9 exactly as you want it to appear on our vendor record (company legal name vs DBA).

3) If NorthGrove wants already-issued POs (issued to old remit) paid to the new account, we require a signed PO reissue authorization form. We can process reissued POs within 5 business days of receiving that authorization. If you prefer Redwood to issue a new PO, let us know the PO numbers and we'll close + reissue.

4) For the portal owner change: we need a Vendor Portal Admin Transfer Acknowledgement (template attached in our vendor portal). If Maya has already completed it within the portal, please confirm the portal transaction ID.

I can expedite the vendor record update after receiving the missing confirmation items (bank transfer type, legal name exact string, and PO handling preference).

Laura Bennett
Accounts Payable Manager
Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwood.ai

From: Maya Patel <maya.patel@northgrove.co>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Cc: ap@northgrove.co
Date: Tue, 4 Aug 2026 08:09:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Bank rotation + new AP contact — onboarding docs attached

Hi Laura / Jordan,

Thanks — answers below and uploaded docs/IDs to your portal (folder: NorthGrove > Banking Change Aug 2026).

1) Clearing account supports both ACH and wire. The attached bank-letter is on issuing bank letterhead and includes the wire routing confirmation. (See clearing-bank-letter_2026-08-03.pdf)

2) Legal name for the W-9: NorthGrove Technologies, Inc. (please use this exact string).

3) PO handling: We request a reissue for PO# 334211 and PO# 334342 to the new clearing account. I've attached a signed PO Reissue Authorization (embedded in the DocuSign completed earlier). DocuSign ref: https://app.docusign.com/embeddedSigning/ABC12345

4) Portal transfer: portal transaction ID NG-PORT-1174 (screenshot included). Maya is now Portal Admin and the portal shows vendor id NG-2917.

Let me know if you need anything else; happy to jump on a 15-min call.

Best,
Maya

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
To: Maya Patel <maya.patel@northgrove.co>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, finance-team@redwood.ai
Date: Wed, 5 Aug 2026 11:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Bank rotation + new AP contact — onboarding docs attached

Maya / team — all set on our side. Summary and next steps: 

- Vendor record updated: vendor_id NG-2917, remit name set to "NorthGrove Technologies, Inc.", new clearing account (ACH & wire) added with verification letter uploaded to the Vendor Documents tab.
- Portal ownership confirmed: portal admin updated to Maya Patel (NG-PORT-1174).
- PO reissues: AP (Laura) will reissue PO# 334211 and PO# 334342 to the new remit within 2 business days; payments will follow our standard payment terms once reissued.

Maya — please verify the vendor alias you want Redwood to display on invoices (legal name vs short name). If you want us to display "NorthGrove" instead of the full legal name on the PO header, confirm here and I will set the display name.

I'll close this thread after I see confirmation that the reissued POs are in the system. Thanks for moving quickly on the DocuSign.

Regards,
Jordan Blake
Senior Procurement Manager | Redwood Inference
jordan.blake@redwood.ai

Date: Wed, 5 Aug 2026 11:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Bank rotation + new AP contact — onboarding docs attached

Maya / team — all set on our side. Summary and next steps: 

- Vendor record updated: vendor_id NG-2917, remit name set to "NorthGrove Technologies, Inc.", new clearing account (ACH & wire) added with verification letter uploaded to the Vendor Documents tab.
- Portal ownership confirmed: portal admin updated to Maya Patel (NG-PORT-1174).
- PO reissues: AP (Laura) will reissue PO# 334211 and PO# 334342 to the new remit within 2 business days; payments will follow our standard payment terms once reissued.

Maya — please verify the vendor alias you want Redwood to display on invoices (legal name vs short name). If you want us to display "NorthGrove" instead of the full legal name on the PO header, confirm here and I will set the display name.

I'll close this thread after I see confirmation that the reissued POs are in the system. Thanks for moving quickly on the DocuSign.

Regards,
Jordan Blake

source.subject: Post-close phased deployment milestones / MAP for OrchardCare
messages:
From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
To: Lydia Wells <lydia.wells@orchardcare.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 28 Jul 2026 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Draft MAP: phased deployment milestones / kickoff windows

Hi Lydia —

Thanks again for the signature and trust — excited to get OrchardCare onto Redwood Dedicated. Attached is a first-pass Mutual Action Plan (MAP) that embeds the post-signature rollout, canary migration approach, and our training cadence. High-level items included:

- Week 0: Contract sign + SOW upload to DocuSign (we'll notify when countersigned)
- Week 1: Kickoff + environment security checklist + access handover
- Weeks 2–3: Canary deployment to 5% traffic, NV baseline + smoke tests
- Weeks 4–6: Full migration window, training sessions for product and ops teams (two 90-min workshops)
- Week 8: Go-live retrospective and hypercare runbook handoff

I've attached OrchardCare_MAP_v1.xlsx and the draft SOW (SOW_RW_OrchardCare.pdf). Please review the milestones and capacity windows and let me know constraints we should bake into the canary (e.g., blackout dates or maintenance windows).

Proposed kickoff: Thursday Aug 6, 10am PT — is that workable for your ops and security leads?

Thanks,
Irene

--
Irene Choi
Senior Solutions Lead | Redwood Inference
irene.choi@redwoodinference.com
+1 (415) 555-0129
From: Lydia Wells <lydia.wells@orchardcare.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Tom Alvarez <tom.alvarez@orchardcare.com>, OrchardCare IT <it@orchardcare.com>
Date: Wed, 29 Jul 2026 11:02:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Draft MAP: phased deployment milestones / kickoff windows

Irene —

Thanks for the clear plan. Quick notes after a fast scan:

- Aug 6 kickoff looks fine for leadership, but our clinical training team is out Aug 9–13 (annual conference). Can we avoid peak training in that week?
- For the canary: we need the canary to run across two facilities (NYC & Atlanta) to validate network egress under both carriers.
- Security would like the network-checklist.csv completed before Week 1; I attached the initial filled items (small edits).

I'll circulate to Tom and our security lead for a deeper review; we aim to get formal sign-off on the SOW by Fri Aug 6 so your proposed kickoff can remain intact. Please confirm you can extend the training window into Weeks 5–7 to avoid the Aug 9–13 blackout.

Also: are there prerequisites we need to complete for KMS integration? Our security team wants a short list in the MAP.

Best,
Lydia

--
Lydia Wells
Dir. Partnerships & Integrations | OrchardCare Health
lydia.wells@orchardcare.com
From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 29 Jul 2026 13:40:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Draft MAP: phased deployment milestones / kickoff windows (OrchardCare)

Team — forwarding Lydia's note. Key asks we need to address:

1) Shift training out of Aug 9–13 blackout (move into Weeks 5–7).
2) Canary must cover two facilities (NYC & ATL) and include network egress profiling for each.
3) Provide KMS integration prerequisites in the MAP.
4) Complete and return network-checklist.csv (Lydia attached a filled-first-pass).

Marcus: can you confirm whether our canary traffic split supports multi-region facility targeting and what additional latency impact we should expect?
Priya: please draft a short KMS prerequisites list (3–5 items) and add to the MAP.
Sean: if we need any SRE support or an extended hypercare window, flag now.

We'll aim to produce OrchardCare_MAP_v1a with these updates and resend to Lydia by EOD Thu Jul 30.

Thanks —
Irene
From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 30 Jul 2026 09:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Draft MAP: phased deployment milestones / kickoff windows (OrchardCare)

Quick answers:

- Canary capability: yes — our traffic router can target traffic by origin facility (we tag by facility in the header). For two-facility canary we recommend split: 3% NYC, 2% ATL for an aggregate 5% and to limit blast radius.
- Expected delta: baseline latency increase of ~8–12ms p95 due to additional routing checks, but tokens-per-second unaffected. We'll include a short monitoring dashboard definition (p50/p95 token latency, error rate, dropped connections) in the MAP.
- Hypercare: recommend we offer a 2-week elevated SRE window post-go-live (Weeks 8–9) for quick rollback support; no charge for first week, second week at standard rate (per SOW terms).

I'll drop the monitoring bullet and the router notes into the MAP update.

Marcus
Senior Infra Engineer | Redwood Inference
From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
To: Lydia Wells <lydia.wells@orchardcare.com>
Cc: Tom Alvarez <tom.alvarez@orchardcare.com>, OrchardCare IT <it@orchardcare.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 4 Aug 2026 16:20:00 -07:00
Subject: Final MAP v1a and confirmed kickoff (kicking off Aug 6)

Lydia / team —

Attached: OrchardCare_MAP_v1a.xlsx (updated), SOW_RW_OrchardCare.pdf (refreshed), network-checklist.csv (completed). Changes since the draft:

- Training moved to Weeks 5–7 to avoid your Aug 9–13 conference blackout. New session dates are listed in the MAP (two product workshops: Aug 24 & Aug 26; two ops sessions: Aug 31 & Sept 2).
- Canary configuration updated to target NYC (3%) + ATL (2%) as Marcus recommended. Monitoring KPIs added (p50/p95 latency, error rate, token throughput).
- KMS prerequisites added (Priya): 1) KMS key ARN and IAM role with decrypt/encrypt permissions; 2) TLS mutual auth cert exchange process; 3) a test key and rotation window for validation — details in MAP.
- Hypercare: Redwood will provide 1 week complimentary elevated SRE coverage and an optional second week as paid support (details in SOW).

Confirming kickoff remains Thursday Aug 6, 10am PT — we'll send the calendar invite and a short agenda (kickoff objectives, roles/owners, immediate next steps). Please reply with any last-minute blockers; otherwise we will proceed with the kickoff and start Week 0 activities on Aug 7 (DocuSign countersign expected by then per your note).

Thanks and looking forward,
Irene

Attachments: OrchardCare_MAP_v1a.xlsx (Excel, 120KB), SOW_RW_OrchardCare.pdf (PDF, 58KB), network-checklist.csv (CSV, 3KB)

--
Irene Choi
Senior Solutions Lead | Redwood Inference
irene.choi@redwoodinference.com
+1 (415) 555-0129

source.subject: Vendor onboarding: integration pilot — request for temporary PO deferral
messages:
From: Kelly Brown <kelly@integrations.io>
To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: procure@integrations.io
Date: Thu, 30 Jul 2026 14:03:00 -0700
Subject: Pilot onboarding request — temporary PO deferral

Hi Irene,

Thanks for taking this on. We’re kicking off a 6-week integration pilot with Redwood to validate the new connector. Because this is a timeboxed pilot and our finance team requires a signed SOW before issuing a PO (their standard workflow), we wanted to ask whether Redwood can accept invoices for the first two invoices without an active PO in place while the SOW/contract completes.

We can provide the signed SOW, a completed vendor intake form, and a W-9 today — attached. If a temporary deferral is possible, please let us know any additional evidence you need and the duration Redwood would permit. Our billing lead (procure@integrations.io) is on copy.

Files attached: SOW-IntegrationPilot.pdf; Integrations_W9.pdf; Vendor-Intake-IntegrationsIO.xlsx

Appreciate a quick turnaround — pilot begins Monday.

Best,
Kelly Brown
Head of Partnerships | Integrations.io
kelly@integrations.io
From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
To: Kelly Brown <kelly@integrations.io>
Cc: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 30 Jul 2026 16:12:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Pilot onboarding request — temporary PO deferral

Hi Kelly — thanks, and welcome.

I reviewed the attachments you uploaded. Quick summary of next steps from Redwood: 
- Procurement will do a short risk intake and confirm the minimal evidence required for a temporary invoice path.
- We need an explicit manager approval at Redwood to allow invoice payment without a PO for a fixed period (we typically do this for trials but need to document controls).
- Please confirm bank wiring instructions on the vendor intake form and provide a point of contact for invoice escalation.

Markus (procurement) and Priya (partner ops) — can you advise whether a 60-day temporary deferral is acceptable for a 6-week pilot, and what guardrails we should attach (i.e., invoice caps, number of invoices, PO creation deadline)?

Kelly — I’ll mirror any required template or short approval form once Markus confirms.

Thanks,
Irene Choi
Sr. Partner Ops Manager | Redwood Inference
irene.choi@redwoodinference.com
From: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Kelly Brown <kelly@integrations.io>
Date: Fri, 31 Jul 2026 09:34:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Pilot onboarding request — temporary PO deferral

Irene / Priya / Kelly —

Thanks for the clear packet. I ran the intake against our low-risk criteria for short-term trials. Recommendation: approve a temporary no-PO invoice path under the following conditions: 

1) Limit: up to 2 invoices, total not to exceed $25,000.
2) Window: 60 days from first invoice date; PO must be created or invoice escalation occurs after 60 days.
3) Controls: vendor bank details verified (we’ve got the W-9), invoice header must reference Redwood deal ID INT-2384-PILOT and include a Redwood POC.
4) Evidence: final SOW on letterhead + vendor intake form retained in procurement record.

I can sign off subject to an approval email from Irene (manager-level signoff) into the procurement playbook. If Priya wants a different cap or shorter window, flag it — otherwise I’ll log this as an approved exception and author a short memo for payment ops.

Attachments (for Redwood record): Vendor-Intake-IntegrationsIO.xlsx (stamped), Integrations_W9.pdf

-Markus
Markus Klein
Procurement Lead | Redwood Inference
markus.klein@redwoodinference.com
From: Kelly Brown <kelly@integrations.io>
To: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 02 Aug 2026 11:26:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Pilot onboarding request — temporary PO deferral

Thanks Markus — that sounds reasonable and actionable. We’ve uploaded the signed SOW to the DocuSign link (https://redwood-docusign.example/doc/abcd1234) and confirmed bank wiring on the intake form. We can commit to two invoices only and will ensure the first invoice is dated the week the pilot starts.

One request from our side: can Redwood include a line in the approval memo stating the invoice cap so our finance team has written confirmation? If you can send the short memo or approval note, we’ll attach it on our end for our accounts payable.

Also: if you prefer, we can create a pull request in HubSpot to attach the SOW and evidence to the INT-2384 record (I’ll do that after I get the memo).

Appreciate the quick coordination — excited to get started.

Best,
Kelly
On Mon, Aug 02, 2026 at 09:34 Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Irene / Priya / Kelly —
> 
> Thanks for the clear packet. I ran the intake against our low-risk criteria for short-term trials. Recommendation: approve a temporary no-PO invoice path under the following conditions: 
> 
> 1) Limit: up to 2 invoices, total not to exceed $25,000.
> 2) Window: 60 days from first invoice date; PO must be created or invoice escalation occurs after 60 days.
> 3) Controls: vendor bank details verified (we’ve got the W-9), invoice header must reference Redwood deal ID INT-2384-PILOT and include a Redwood POC.

To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: procure@integrations.io
Date: Thu, 30 Jul 2026 14:03:00 -0700
Subject: Pilot onboarding request — temporary PO deferral

Hi Irene,

Thanks for taking this on. We’re kicking off a 6-week integration pilot with Redwood to validate the new connector. Because this is a timeboxed pilot and our finance team requires a signed SOW before issuing a PO (their standard workflow), we wanted to ask whether Redwood can accept invoices for the first two invoices without an active PO in place while the SOW/contract completes.

We can provide the signed SOW, a completed vendor intake form, and a W-9 today — attached. If a temporary deferral is possible, please let us know any additional evidence you need and the duration Redwood would permit. Our billing lead (procure@integrations.io) is on copy.

Files attached: SOW-IntegrationPilot.pdf; Integrations_W9.pdf; Vendor-Intake-IntegrationsIO.xlsx

Appreciate a quick turnaround — pilot begins Monday.

Best,
Kelly Brown
Head of Partnerships | Integrations.io
kelly@integrations.io

Hi Kelly — thanks, and welcome.

- Procurement will do a short risk intake and confirm the minimal evidence required for a temporary invoice path.
- We need an explicit manager approval at Redwood to allow invoice payment without a PO for a fixed period (we typically do this for trials but need to document controls).
- Please confirm bank wiring instructions on the vendor intake form and provide a point of contact for invoice escalation.

Markus (procurement) and Priya (partner ops) — can you advise whether a 60-day temporary deferral is acceptable for a 6-week pilot, and what guardrails we should attach (i.e., invoice caps, number of invoices, PO creation deadline)?

Kelly — I’ll mirror any required template or short approval form once Markus confirms.

Thanks,
Irene Choi
Sr. Partner Ops Manager | Redwood Inference
irene.choi@redwoodinference.com


From: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>

source.subject: Partnership: integration onboarding & revenue routing
messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: partnerships@edgecraft.io
Cc: marissa.cole@redwood.com
Date: Mon, 20 Jul 2026 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Partnership: integration onboarding & revenue routing

Hi Aisha / Edgecraft team —

Great meeting last week. As discussed, we’re excited about a joint go-to-market where Edgecraft bundles a Redwood connector inside your managed offering. To make the commercial side simple for your sales team, can we align on two quick things so we can scope the technical onboarding work:  

1) Revenue routing model: do you prefer referral (one-time credit) or reseller/rev-share (ongoing split)? We typically support both; rev-share examples below.
2) Lead registration: what intake fields can we require to qualify for partner credit (e.g., account name, HubSpot ID, first contact date)?

I’ve attached a short draft rev-share schedule for discussion (edgecraft-revshare-proposal-v1.pdf) and a technical checklist so our integration and your engineering team can scope a joint demo/POC (partner-technical-checklist.xlsx).

Next step: if you’re good to explore rev-share, can we schedule a 30-minute sync with your alliances lead + our Partner Ops (Marissa) + Solutions (Jonas) this week?

Thanks — I’ll loop in Marissa now.

Best,
Ben Carter
Sr. Partnerships Manager
Redwood Inference
Attachment: edgecraft-revshare-proposal-v1.pdf (1 page)
Attachment: partner-technical-checklist.xlsx (spreadsheet)
From: Aisha Rahman <partnerships@edgecraft.io>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: alliances@devopspro.com
Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 11:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Partnership: integration onboarding & revenue routing

Ben —

Thanks for the note and the materials. We like the rev-share approach if the economics are straightforward and there’s a clear SLA for demos / demo kits. A few clarifying Qs from our side:  

- What granularity do you need for lead registration? Can we register via HubSpot deal ID + email?
- Minimum contract term for a rev-share? Our procurement asks about 12 months minimum for sustained payouts.
- Do you have a standard partner MMP (mutual marketing) crediting window (e.g., 90/180 days)?

We’ll review the attachments. I’ll bring in our alliances director (DevOpsPro is our channel reseller for EMEA) and propose a 30-min slot — Thurs or Fri morning PT.

Best,
Aisha Rahman
Head of Partnerships
Edgecraft Labs
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <partnerships@edgecraft.io>, marissa.cole@redwood.com, jonas.weber@redwood.com
Cc: priya.natarajan@redwood.com
Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 08:43:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Partnership: integration onboarding & revenue routing

Aisha — thanks. Quick answers and suggested defaults from our side so the call can be efficient:  

- Lead registration: HubSpot deal ID + first contact email + account domain. That’s sufficient for us to validate and lock registration.
- Minimum term: Redwood prefers a 6–12 month attribution window for rev-share accounting; 12 months is OK if Edgecraft requires it (we’ll reflect in the SOW).
- MMP crediting window: standard is 90 days for marketing-sourced leads; 180 days for actively engaged MQLs (we can discuss tiers).

Marissa — can you and Jonas prepare: 1) a draft mutual action plan with milestones and owner assignments, and 2) a simple rev-share payout table (baseline, upsell, renewals) for discussion?

Priya — I’m looping you for a quick read on whether the 12-month payout commitment has any legal flags on our side.

Proposed call: Thu 23 Jul 10:00 PT. Aisha, does that work?

Thanks,
Ben

> On Tue, Jul 21, 2026 at 11:02 AM Aisha Rahman <partnerships@edgecraft.io> wrote:
> > Ben —
> >
> > Thanks for the note and the materials. We like the rev-share approach if the economics are straightforward...
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Aisha Rahman <partnerships@edgecraft.io>, jonas.weber@redwood.com
Cc: priya.natarajan@redwood.com
Date: Thu, 23 Jul 2026 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Partnership: integration onboarding & revenue routing

Team —

Thanks for the fast turnaround. Attached is a one-pager mutual action plan (MMP) and a rev-share payout CSV that maps to three buckets: initial sale (year 1), renewal (year 2+), and professional services (integration fees). High-level recommendations:  

- Rev-share split (suggested / starting point): 20% for referral credit (one-time) OR 12% ongoing on-recurring revenue for reseller model; escalator to 15% after $500k ARR through Edgecraft in a rolling 12 months.
- Lead registration: HubSpot deal ID + first contact email + signed SOW; registration locks for 180 days unless the account converts earlier.
- Minimum payout term: we can honor a 12-month payout schedule for booked deals; Redwood will remit monthly with a 30-day payment term after invoice.
- Payouts: Redwood will provide monthly commission reports and tokenized invoice statements; payments via ACH or wire.

Action items for the call:
- Marley (Edgecraft) to confirm whether they need a reseller agreement vs referral.
- Jonas to cover technical onboarding SOW and estimated dev days (see checklist).
- Priya to review any regulatory clauses (data residency, logging).

Attachments: mmp_revshare_table.csv, edgecraft-revshare-proposal-v1.pdf, partner-technical-checklist.xlsx

I’ll also drop the MMP to a shared Drive (link below) and a DocuSign skeleton for signature capture after we agree commercial terms.

Shared Drive: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1aBcDk-edgecraft-partner
DocuSign (skeleton): https://app.docusign.com/Signing/XYZ-EDGE-2026

Marissa Cole
Head, Partner Operations
Redwood Inference
Attachment: mmp_revshare_table.csv
From: Aisha Rahman <partnerships@edgecraft.io>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, jonas.weber@redwood.com
Date: Tue, 28 Jul 2026 16:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Partnership: integration onboarding & revenue routing

Marissa / Ben —

This all looks good. Edgecraft is comfortable moving forward with the reseller/rev-share model (12% recurring, escalator to 15% after $500k ARR) and the 12-month payout term. Marley confirmed she needs reseller paperwork (not just referral).

Next steps we agreed on the call:
- Marissa: finalize MMP and send the DocuSign skeleton for signatures.
- Jonas: share an estimated 6-week technical onboarding plan and kickoff date options (we can start Aug 10 or Aug 24).
- Ben: create HubSpot registration form template and share to Aisha for ops alignment.

Please send the DocuSign and final MMP when ready and I’ll route through procurement.

Thanks — excited to get started.

Best,
Aisha Rahman
Head of Partnerships
Edgecraft Labs

> On Tue, Jul 21, 2026 at 11:02 AM Aisha Rahman <partnerships@edgecraft.io> wrote:
> > Ben —
> >
> > Thanks for the note and the materials. We like the rev-share approach if the economics are straightforward...


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Aisha Rahman <partnerships@edgecraft.io>, jonas.weber@redwood.com
Cc: priya.natarajan@redwood.com
Date: Thu, 23 Jul 2026 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Partnership: integration onboarding & revenue routing

Team —

Thanks for the fast turnaround. Attached is a one-pager mutual action plan (MMP) and a rev-share payout CSV that maps to three buckets: initial sale (year 1), renewal (year 2+), and professional services (integration fees). High-level recommendations:  

- Rev-share split (suggested / starting point): 20% for referral credit (one-time) OR 12% ongoing on-recurring revenue for reseller model; escalator to 15% after $500k ARR through Edgecraft in a rolling 12 months.
- Lead registration: HubSpot deal ID + first contact email + signed SOW; registration locks for 180 days unless the account converts earlier.
- Minimum payout term: we can honor a 12-month payout schedule for booked deals; Redwood will remit monthly with a 30-day payment term after invoice.
- Payouts: Redwood will provide monthly commission reports and tokenized invoice statements; payments via ACH or wire.

Action items for the call:
- Marley (Edgecraft) to confirm whether they need a reseller agreement vs referral.
- Jonas to cover technical onboarding SOW and estimated dev days (see checklist).
- Priya to review any regulatory clauses (data residency, logging).

Attachments: mmp_revshare_table.csv, edgecraft-revshare-proposal-v1.pdf, partner-technical-checklist.xlsx

I’ll also drop the MMP to a shared Drive (link below) and a DocuSign skeleton for signature capture after we agree commercial terms.

Shared Drive: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1aBcDk-edgecraft-partner
DocuSign (skeleton): https://app.docusign.com/Signing/XYZ-EDGE-2026

Marissa Cole
Head, Partner Operations
Redwood Inference
Attachment: mmp_revshare_table.csv


From: Aisha Rahman <partnerships@edgecraft.io>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, jonas.weber@redwood.com
Date: Tue, 28 Jul 2026 16:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Partnership: integration onboarding & revenue routing

Marissa / Ben —

This all looks good. Edgecraft is comfortable moving forward with the reseller/rev-share model (12% recurring, escalator to 15% after $500k ARR) and the 12-month payout term. Marley confirmed she needs reseller paperwork (not just referral).

Next steps we agreed on the call:
- Marissa: finalize MMP and send the DocuSign skeleton for signatures.
- Jonas: share an estimated 6-week technical onboarding plan and kickoff date options (we can start Aug 10 or Aug 24).
- Ben: create HubSpot registration form template and share to Aisha for ops alignment.

Please send the DocuSign and final MMP when ready and I’ll route through procurement.

Thanks — excited to get started.

Best,
Aisha Rahman
Head of Partnerships
Edgecraft Labs

source.subject: Re: Mini POC pricing & staged incentive structure
messages:
From: Hiro Tanaka <hiro@redwood.com>
To: Elaine Murphy <elaine@clearbay.ai>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 23 Jul 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Mini POC pricing & staged incentive structure

Elaine —

Great to meet yesterday. Per our convo, I wanted to outline two practical paths for a small, time-boxed POC that helps ClearBay validate latency/cost tradeoffs and integration effort without a large upfront commitment. Attached: clearbay_mvp_requirements.pdf and a first-pass incentive worksheet (proposed_incentive_schedule.xlsx).

Option 1 — Simple discounted timebox (fastest path)
- 30-day POC, fixed price covering up to 150k inference tokens (Redwood hosted)
- 25% off our published pay-as-you-go token rate for the pilot window
- One included implementation sync (up to 8 eng hours)
- Customer responsible for post-PO engineering time

Option 2 — Staged incentive schedule (risk-share)
- 60-day POC split into two 30-day phases
- Phase A (validation): lower commitment (50k tokens) with 40% off first-tier tokens + 20 hours sandbox support
- Phase B (ramp): if Phase A metrics met (latency < 150ms p95, sample throughput target), Phase B expands to 200k tokens at 15% off; Redwood provides a one-time 40-hour implementation credit to help integrate KV caching and batching configs
- If Phase A fails to meet the agreed metrics, Phase B not billed and Redwood provides a 10-hour post-mortem session free

Why staged? It reduces your initial risk while giving us a path to invest time that actually moves the needle. We're comfortable doing a staged concession provided we can agree on the success metrics (I'll propose a short SLA + metric checklist in the spreadsheet).

A couple of practical notes:
- We can accept a short PO (net 30) for either option and issue an invoice when Phase A starts.
- Finance will need a brief outline of credits/concessions for approval; I can loop in Kimberly if you want a formal quote.

Next steps (suggested):
1) You confirm which option is closer to ClearBay's needs.
2) Agree on 3 success metrics for Phase A (latency, token/profile per call, basic integration milestone).
3) I'll produce a formal addendum and attach DocuSign link.

If you prefer a single fixed-discount approach (no credits), say so — we've done that for customers who need clean procurement.

Thanks — happy to hop on a quick 20-min call if you want to walk the options.

—
Hiro Tanaka
Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
hiro@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0123

Attachments: clearbay_mvp_requirements.pdf (10 pages), proposed_incentive_schedule.xlsx (spreadsheet)

From: Elaine Murphy <elaine@clearbay.ai>
To: Hiro Tanaka <hiro@redwood.com>
Cc: ClearBay POC Team <poc@clearbay.ai>
Date: Thu, 23 Jul 2026 11:28:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Mini POC pricing & staged incentive structure

Hiro —

Thanks for the clear breakdown. A few quick clarifying questions before we take to procurement:

1) For Option 2, what level of telemetry/access do you need from our side to validate the Phase A metrics? We manage data sensitivity carefully and may need to redline any log collection.
2) Can the implementation credit be applied toward a dedicated runbook session with one of your runtime engineers, or is it strictly for onboarding hours?
3) If we go fixed-discount (Option 1), are you able to provide a one-time SLA credit if you miss the agreed p95 latency in the pilot window? Our procurement likes a clear remedy.

Also, procurement asked whether Redwood can produce a single consolidated quote (line items + simple terms) to attach to a PO — if possible, please include both options in the quote so they can compare.

We have an internal decision sync on Friday at 10:00am ET; I'd like to have your recommended option and a suggested concession cap before then.

Best,
Elaine Murphy
Head of ML Ops | ClearBay AI
elaine@clearbay.ai

---
On Thu, Jul 23, 2026 at 09:12 AM Hiro Tanaka <hiro@redwood.com> wrote:
> Elaine —
> 
> Great to meet yesterday. Per our convo, I wanted to outline two practical paths for a small, time-boxed POC...

From: Hiro Tanaka <hiro@redwood.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 23 Jul 2026 13:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Mini POC pricing & staged incentive structure (ClearBay)

Kimberly / Priya —

Looping you in: Elaine from ClearBay had a few follow-up questions and needs a consolidated quote by Friday 10am ET. Quick thoughts below and a recommended concession structure to minimize revenue impact while keeping the deal moving.

Elaine's asks: telemetry limits, use of implementation credit, SLA remedy for missed latency.

Recommendation (lean):
- Offer Option 2 (staged) as primary. It shows willingness to absorb early risk and creates a path to a larger follow-on.
- Concession cap = $12,000 total value (includes the 40-hour implementation credit valued at $8k + an extra $4k one-time invoice credit if Phase A meets validation but there's delay on their side).
- For procurement simplicity, include Option 1 (30d fixed) as an alternate with 20% discount. That avoids lengthy procurement haggling.
- For SLA remedy: suggest a 10% credit on pilot invoice if p95 > target for two consecutive days, capped at the concession cap above. Keeps exposure bounded.

Telemetry: we can offer an allow-list approach — ClearBay runs telemetry export to a temporary S3 bucket (they control retention) and grants read-only access to one named Redwood engineer for the Phase A validation window. This meets most security needs without persistent logs flowing into our systems. Legal will need to sign off on the access arrangement but it's a common pattern.

If you both approve, I'll draft a one-page consolidated quote (two options) and loop in Legal for a minimal addendum. Finance will need the concession cap in writing for approval; Kimberly, can you confirm if $12k is within delegated approval for a field concession?

—
Hiro

> On Thu, Jul 23, 2026 at 11:28 AM Elaine Murphy <elaine@clearbay.ai> wrote:
> > Hiro —
> > 
> > Thanks for the clear breakdown...

From: Hiro Tanaka <hiro@redwood.com>
To: Elaine Murphy <elaine@clearbay.ai>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 24 Jul 2026 16:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Mini POC pricing & staged incentive structure

Elaine —

Thanks for the fast turn. Per your procurement timing, attached is a consolidated quote (two options) and a short summary of the telemetry access pattern. Highlights below and next steps:

Formal proposal summary (attached spreadsheet):
- Option A (30-day fixed): 20% one-time pilot discount on published PAYG rate; covers up to 150k tokens; includes 8 implementation hours; invoice on kick-off.
- Option B (staged): 60-day split pilot (Phase A + Phase B) with the staged incentives described earlier. Phase A metrics gated: p95 latency < 150ms and end-to-end profile match within agreed sample traces. If gates pass, Phase B activates at the discounted Phase B rate. Includes 40 hours implementation credit (applied to engineering support tasks only).

Concession cap and SLA remedy:
- Total concession cap = $12,000 (implementation credit counted at $8k + $4k invoice credit reserve).
- SLA remedy: if p95 latency target missed for >1 day, apply 10% credit to the impacted phase invoice; cap across pilot = concession cap.

Telemetry/access model:
- ClearBay will create a short-lived S3 bucket (or share a signed URL) with sample traces and anonymized logs. Redwood gets read-only access to the S3 path for 30 days. No ingestion into Redwood production logs.

Procurement & timeline:
- I'll attach both options as line items in the quote for PO attachment.
- If you sign Option B and Phase A starts the week of Aug 2, we can target Phase B activation Aug 30 (assuming metrics pass).

DocuSign & quote: https://drive.google.com/open?id=1a2b3cClearBayPOC (proposal spreadsheet + one-page addendum).

Please confirm which option ClearBay prefers and I'll prepare the DocuSign package and send to your procurement contact. If useful, I can draft a 1-paragraph statement for legal summarizing the telemetry protections.

Best,
Hiro Tanaka
Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
hiro@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0123

Attachments: proposed_incentive_schedule.xlsx, clearbay_mvp_requirements.pdf
Related HubSpot: https://app.hubspot.com/deal/1234567890

source.subject: Platform compliance intake: audit windows, BYO-KMS & SSO clarifications
messages:
From: Kamal Iqbal <kamal@medspring.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: MedSpring IT Security <security@medspring.com>
Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Security questions for PoC — audit logs / retention / BYO-KMS

Hi Jordan / Ben,

Thanks again for the demo yesterday. Security team asked me to capture the initial list of things they need clarified for a short PoC (2–4 weeks). The spreadsheet attached has prioritized items; top-line asks are:

- Audit log granularity and retention options (hosted vs dedicated vs private).
- Evidence we can provide for access/datum-level events (who/when/region).
- BYO-KMS: flow for key import, rotation, and escrow — what is supported for the hosted API?
- SSO integration specifics: token expiry, session traceability, and deprovisioning hooks.

source.subject: Post-demo: Hyperscaler parity checklist & next test owners
messages:
From: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
To: Claire Zhang <claire@novaplay.com>, David Liu <david.liu@novaplay.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: Post-demo: Hyperscaler parity checklist & next test owners

Hi Claire / David —

Thanks again for your time earlier. Quick recap from our side and a proposed set of next tests so we can validate parity with the hyperscaler endpoints you’ve been running. High-level differentiation we covered:

- Smart routing & fallback: Redwood can route by cost/latency tier and fall back to smaller variants automatically, preserving uptime.
- Prefix / KV caching: automatic prefix caching to reduce per-token cost on interactive sessions.
- Streaming & structured output: committed work on streaming deltas + stable JSON output options to make tool calling reliable.
- Dedicated/private deployment: ability to run an isolated test in your VPC for KMS/HSM-backed key management and data residency checks.

Attached is a parity checklist (redwood_hyperscaler-parity-checklist.xlsx). Proposed next tests and owners:

1) Streaming latency & head-start behaviour — owner: Ava Chen (streaming-test-spec-v1.pdf attached).
2) Tool / function-calling correctness under interleaved calls — owner: Rishi Malhotra (we'll provide a small harness).
3) Structured output/JSON stability across long responses — owner: Jonas Weber.
4) Security/compliance evidence & isolated Private run for KMS — owner: Priya Natarajan.

Proposed timeline: run initial tests in the week of Jul 27, 2026; internal analysis the week after; quick sync on Aug 6. If that works, can you confirm a 2-hour window for the isolated Private run when David’s procurement team is available?

Next items for us (action owners in parentheses):
- Share test harness and scripts (Rishi)
- Share streaming spec and expected metrics (Ava)
- Provide SOC2 evidence checklist and DocuSign for NDA/PO (Priya)

Claire — would love any additional items you want measured versus the hyperscaler baseline (latency p95/p99, median token cost, function call success rate, structured-output validation pass rate).

Thanks —

Jonas Weber
Sr. Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
[jonas.weber@redwood.com]
[Attachment: redwood_hyperscaler-parity-checklist.xlsx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet)]

From: Claire Zhang <claire@novaplay.com>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 11:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-demo: Hyperscaler parity checklist & next test owners

Thanks Jonas — this is helpful. A couple quick clarifications before we finalize the plan:

1) For tool calling correctness, what latency envelope should we expect for cold vs warm function invocations? Our baseline target is <120ms median for warm calls.
2) For streaming, does your delta streaming introduce additional tokenization overhead that would change the cost model?
3) Procurement: fair to say David (cc'd) will need the scope for an isolated Private test and an estimate for dedicated time/cost.

If we can get those answers, I can confirm internal stakeholders and slot a 2-hour window.

— Claire
Product Lead, NovaPlay
claire@novaplay.com

On Wed, Jul 22, 2026 at 09:18 AM Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com> wrote:
> [quoted message omitted]

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Claire Zhang <claire@novaplay.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 13:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-demo: Hyperscaler parity checklist & next test owners

Hi Claire — quick answers, and a proposed minimal test spec so you can validate against your baseline:

1) Tool-calling latency expectations:
- Cold invocation (no warmed cache / first token): 250-400ms median depending on function weight and token size.
- Warm invocation (KV cache / warmed runtimes): 80-140ms median. We target sub-120ms for typical lightweight function calls; heavier code or large payloads will push beyond.

2) Streaming cost model: Our delta streaming sends only incremental tokens to the client; tokenization overhead at the model level is unchanged. The practical cost delta is primarily due to network framing and smaller per-chunk meta overhead, typically <2% token-equivalent overhead in our tests.

3) Test harness: I uploaded a small harness and sample payloads here: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1aBcdExampleTestHarness. The streaming-test-spec-v1.pdf in the attachments includes test scenarios, request rates (1/10/50 rps), and the pass/fail criteria for structured JSON output.

Attachment: streaming-test-spec-v1.pdf

If that looks good I can run a pre-check with Rishi against our staging infra and then we can schedule the Private run with Priya for the KMS test.

Thanks,
Ava
Solutions Architect | Redwood Inference

From: Claire Zhang <claire@novaplay.com>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: David Liu <david.liu@novaplay.com>
Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 16:10:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-demo: Hyperscaler parity checklist & next test owners

Thanks Ava — that aligns with what we need to validate.

David, can you coordinate procurement availability and confirm whether we can run the Private window Jul 28–30 (flexible two-hour block)? If not, suggest alternatives.

Jonas/Ava — please also include an estimate of any billable hours for the Private run so procurement can assess.

Claire

On Wed, Jul 22, 2026 at 11:02 AM Claire Zhang <claire@novaplay.com> wrote:
