From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Claire Zhang <claire@novaplay.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 22 Jul 2026 13:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-demo: Hyperscaler parity checklist & next test owners

Hi Claire — quick answers, and a proposed minimal test spec so you can validate against your baseline:

1) Tool-calling latency expectations:
- Cold invocation (no warmed cache / first token): 250-400ms median depending on function weight and token size.
- Warm invocation (KV cache / warmed runtimes): 80-140ms median. We target sub-120ms for typical lightweight function calls; heavier code or large payloads will push beyond.

2) Streaming cost model: Our delta streaming sends only incremental tokens to the client; tokenization overhead at the model level is unchanged. The practical cost delta is primarily due to network framing and smaller per-chunk meta overhead, typically <2% token-equivalent overhead in our tests.

3) Test harness: I uploaded a small harness and sample payloads here: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1aBcdExampleTestHarness. The streaming-test-spec-v1.pdf in the attachments includes test scenarios, request rates (1/10/50 rps), and the pass/fail criteria for structured JSON output.

Attachment: streaming-test-spec-v1.pdf

If that looks good I can run a pre-check with Rishi against our staging infra and then we can schedule the Private run with Priya for the KMS test.

Thanks,
Ava
Solutions Architect | Redwood Inference

messages:
From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
To: Jamie Liu <jamie.liu@atlaspay.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 15 Jul 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: MAP kickoff: AtlasPay dedicated GPU pool

Hi Jamie —

Thanks again for the conversation yesterday. As discussed, I wanted to kick off a short Mutual Action Plan (MAP) to align stakeholders, define evaluation criteria, and set a target close window for a dedicated GPU pool for AtlasPay. Goal: get you into a committed dedicated pool with predictable throughput and a validated eval plan.

Proposed high-level MAP (6 weeks):
- Week 0: Kickoff + stakeholder roster (this week)
- Week 1–2: Evaluation runs (prompt sets + SLO verification)
- Week 3: Capacity tuning and performance sign-off
- Week 4: Contract and procurement (PO)
- Week 5–6: Provisioning and rollout plan for staging->prod

Can we schedule a 30-min kickoff tomorrow or Monday to confirm roles and the evaluation checklist? I attached a starter MAP spreadsheet and an initial prompt set for the eval.

Attachments: MAP-AtlasPay_v1.xlsx, AtlasPay_eval_prompts.pdf

Thanks,
Evelyn Hart
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
E: evelyn.hart@redwood.com | M: +1 (415) 555-0123

---
Note: If you prefer a slightly accelerated close (aiming for mid-August), mention any procurement windows or internal approvals we should know about in kickoff so we can prioritize milestones.

From: Jamie Liu <jamie.liu@atlaspay.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Cc: Michael Burns <michael.burns@atlaspay.com>, AtlasPay Legal <legal@atlaspay.com>
Date: Thu, 15 Jul 2026 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MAP kickoff: AtlasPay dedicated GPU pool

Evelyn —

Appreciate the clear outline. We’re interested and can commit the core evaluation resources. A few constraints to flag up front:
- Peak target throughput: ~1.2M tokens/min across concurrent workers (spikes expected during settlement windows)
- Latency target: p95 <= 250ms for 512-token responses
- Data residency: must be hosted in us-east-1; any logs with customer data must be redacted or routed to our secure S3
- Procurement: legal will need a standard SLA addendum; I’ve CC’d them.

I’ll add Michael (CTO) into the kickoff — he’ll want to validate the architecture and the planned GPUs. Monday 10:00am ET works for us for a 30-min slot.

–Jamie
Jamie Liu
Head of Product | AtlasPay Inc.
jamie.liu@atlaspay.com | +1 (212) 555-0987

From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
To: Jamie Liu <jamie.liu@atlaspay.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Michael Burns <michael.burns@atlaspay.com>, AtlasPay Legal <legal@atlaspay.com>
Date: Thu, 15 Jul 2026 15:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MAP kickoff: AtlasPay dedicated GPU pool — roles & ask list

Thanks Jamie — copying the RW team and laying out roles so everyone is clear before kickoff: 
- Sales (map owner / commercial): Evelyn Hart — overall timeline, PO coordination, commercial terms
- Solutions / Runtime: Karthik Iyer — throughput tuning, kernel selection, test harness
- Deploy / Ops: Diego Fernandez — provisioning, region setup (us-east-1), network/VPC checklist
- Customer Success / Onboarding: Monica Patel — rollout plan, staging validation, training docs
- Security & Compliance: Priya Natarajan — data handling, audit logs, SOC 2 evidence

Immediate asks for the kickoff (please bring these items):
1) Canonical eval prompt set + 3 representative request traces (we’ll run initial eval on these)
2) Target concurrency windows (times when settlement spikes occur)
3) Any procurement deadlines (e.g., fiscal cutoffs or PO submission dates)

We attached an SLO doc with suggested latency/availability bands (SLO_latency_targets.docx). Based on your targets, Karthik will propose an initial GPU configuration during the call.

Proposed kickoff: Monday, Jul 19 10:00am ET / 7:00am PT — I’ll send a calendar invite. If that works I’ll also upload the MAP sheet to the shared Drive link below and add a HubSpot MAP record.

Attachments: SLO_latency_targets.docx
Related links: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1AtlasPay-MAP

Cheers,
Evelyn
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
---
On Thu, 15 Jul 2026 at 10:05 AM Jamie Liu <jamie.liu@atlaspay.com> wrote:
> Evelyn —
> 
> Appreciate the clear outline. We’re interested and can commit the core evaluation resources. A few constraints to flag up front: ...

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>, Jamie Liu <jamie.liu@atlaspay.com>
Cc: Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 16 Jul 2026 10:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MAP kickoff: AtlasPay dedicated GPU pool — throughput note

Thanks — quick technical note ahead of Monday’s call so we have alignment on capacity options. Summary based on Jamie’s targets (1.2M tok/min, p95 <= 250ms):
- Recommended starting config: 8x A100-80GB (or 12x A10gn) in a dedicated pool with two scheduling rings for spike isolation
- Kernel/config: enable continuous batching with max-batch 64, dynamic KV cache warm-up for hot prefixes, and 8-16 token prefetch to reduce tail latency
- Concurrency planning: expect ~60 concurrent streams at steady state (512-token median), headroom for 180 streams during spikes with autoscale bursts (policy-based)
- Eval plan: we’ll run 3 passes — (1) baseline perf (no cache), (2) KV-warmed runs with realistic traces, (3) quantized variant to check cost trade-offs
- Metrics to capture: per-token CPU/GPU utilization, p50/p90/p95 latency by sequence length buckets, batch sizes, and cache hit rate

I’ll bring a short slide with expected cost per 1M tokens under each config and the trade-offs for latency vs cost. If legal/data-residency needs anonymization hooks, Diego and Priya and I will align on integration points during the call.

—Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Principal Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference

From: Jamie Liu <jamie.liu@atlaspay.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 17 Jul 2026 16:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MAP kickoff — confirmed

All —

Thanks for the clarity. Monday at 10:00am ET is confirmed on our side. We’ll circulate a short internal note to Legal and Engineering with the datasets for the eval (anonymized traces) prior to the call.

Next actions:
- Evelyn: calendar invite + MAP sheet in Drive (please include HubSpot MAP link)
- Karthik & Diego: bring the perf baseline and provisioning checklist
- Monica: draft a tentative commercial timeline (we aim to submit PO by Aug 6 if eval passes)
- Priya/AtlasPay Legal: confirm any redaction or logging constraints before the eval

We’re aligned to try to target a formal close by Aug 20, aware this depends on procurement cadence. I’ll also add Michael and our infra lead to the invite.

See you Monday — Jamie
Jamie Liu
Head of Product | AtlasPay Inc.

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>, Jamie Liu <jamie.liu@atlaspay.com>
Cc: Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 16 Jul 2026 10:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MAP kickoff: AtlasPay dedicated GPU pool — throughput note

Thanks — quick technical note ahead of Monday’s call so we have alignment on capacity options. Summary based on Jamie’s targets (1.2M tok/min, p95 <= 250ms):
- Recommended starting config: 8x A100-80GB (or 12x A10gn) in a dedicated pool with two scheduling rings for spike isolation
- Kernel/config: enable continuous batching with max-batch 64, dynamic KV cache warm-up for hot prefixes, and 8-16 token prefetch to reduce tail latency
- Concurrency planning: expect ~60 concurrent streams at steady state (512-token median), headroom for 180 streams during spikes with autoscale bursts (policy-based)
- Eval plan: we’ll run 3 passes — (1) baseline perf (no cache), (2) KV-warmed runs with realistic traces, (3) quantized variant to check cost trade-offs
- Metrics to capture: per-token CPU/GPU utilization, p50/p90/p95 latency by sequence length buckets, batch sizes, and cache hit rate

I’ll bring a short slide with expected cost per 1M tokens under each config and the trade-offs for latency vs cost. If legal/data-residency needs anonymization hooks, Diego and Priya and I will align on integration points during the call.

—Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Principal Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference

From: Jamie Liu <jamie.liu@atlaspay.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 17 Jul 2026 16:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MAP kickoff — confirmed

All —

Thanks for the clarity. Monday at 10:00am ET is confirmed on our side. We’ll circulate a short internal note to Legal and Engineering with the datasets for the eval (anonymized traces) prior to the call.

Next actions:
- Evelyn: calendar invite + MAP sheet in Drive (please include HubSpot MAP link)
- Karthik & Diego: bring the perf baseline and provisioning checklist
- Monica: draft a tentative commercial timeline (we aim to submit PO by Aug 6 if eval passes)
- Priya/AtlasPay Legal: confirm any redaction or logging constraints before the eval

We’re aligned to try to target a formal close by Aug 20, aware this depends on procurement cadence. I’ll also add Michael and our infra lead to the invite.

See you Monday — Jamie
Jamie Liu
Head of Product | AtlasPay Inc.
text:
From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
To: Jamie Liu <jamie.liu@atlaspay.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 15 Jul 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: MAP kickoff: AtlasPay dedicated GPU pool

Hi Jamie —

Thanks again for the conversation yesterday. As discussed, I wanted to kick off a short Mutual Action Plan (MAP) to align stakeholders, define evaluation criteria, and set a target close window for a dedicated GPU pool for AtlasPay. Goal: get you into a committed dedicated pool with predictable throughput and a validated eval plan.

Proposed high-level MAP (6 weeks):
- Week 0: Kickoff + stakeholder roster (this week)
- Week 1–2: Evaluation runs (prompt sets + SLO verification)
- Week 3: Capacity tuning and performance sign-off
- Week 4: Contract and procurement (PO)
- Week 5–6: Provisioning and rollout plan for staging->prod

Can we schedule a 30-min kickoff tomorrow or Monday to confirm roles and the evaluation checklist? I attached a starter MAP spreadsheet and an initial prompt set for the eval.

Attachments: MAP-AtlasPay_v1.xlsx, AtlasPay_eval_prompts.pdf

Thanks,
Evelyn Hart
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
E: evelyn.hart@redwood.com | M: +1 (415) 555-0123

---
Note: If you prefer a slightly accelerated close (aiming for mid-August), mention any procurement windows or internal approvals we should know about in kickoff so we can prioritize milestones.

From: Jamie Liu <jamie.liu@atlaspay.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Cc: Michael Burns <michael.burns@atlaspay.com>, AtlasPay Legal <legal@atlaspay.com>
Date: Thu, 15 Jul 2026 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MAP kickoff: AtlasPay dedicated GPU pool

Evelyn —

Appreciate the clear outline. We’re interested and can commit the core evaluation resources. A few constraints to flag up front:
- Peak target throughput: ~1.2M tokens/min across concurrent workers (spikes expected during settlement windows)
- Latency target: p95 <= 250ms for 512-token responses
- Data residency: must be hosted in us-east-1; any logs with customer data must be redacted or routed to our secure S3
- Procurement: legal will need a standard SLA addendum; I’ve CC’d them.

I’ll add Michael (CTO) into the kickoff — he’ll want to validate the architecture and the planned GPUs. Monday 10:00am ET works for us for a 30-min slot.

–Jamie
Jamie Liu
Head of Product | AtlasPay Inc.
jamie.liu@atlaspay.com | +1 (212) 555-0987

From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
To: Jamie Liu <jamie.liu@atlaspay.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Michael Burns <michael.burns@atlaspay.com>, AtlasPay Legal <legal@atlaspay.com>
Date: Thu, 15 Jul 2026 15:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MAP kickoff: AtlasPay dedicated GPU pool — roles & ask list

Thanks Jamie — copying the RW team and laying out roles so everyone is clear before kickoff: 
- Sales (map owner / commercial): Evelyn Hart — overall timeline, PO coordination, commercial terms
- Solutions / Runtime: Karthik Iyer — throughput tuning, kernel selection, test harness
- Deploy / Ops: Diego Fernandez — provisioning, region setup (us-east-1), network/VPC checklist
- Customer Success / Onboarding: Monica Patel — rollout plan, staging validation, training docs
- Security & Compliance: Priya Natarajan — data handling, audit logs, SOC 2 evidence

Immediate asks for the kickoff (please bring these items):
1) Canonical eval prompt set + 3 representative request traces (we’ll run initial eval on these)
2) Target concurrency windows (times when settlement spikes occur)
3) Any procurement deadlines (e.g., fiscal cutoffs or PO submission dates)

We attached an SLO doc with suggested latency/availability bands (SLO_latency_targets.docx). Based on your targets, Karthik will propose an initial GPU configuration during the call.

Proposed kickoff: Monday, Jul 19 10:00am ET / 7:00am PT — I’ll send a calendar invite. If that works I’ll also upload the MAP sheet to the shared Drive link below and add a HubSpot MAP record.

Attachments: SLO_latency_targets.docx
Related links: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1AtlasPay-MAP

Cheers,
Evelyn
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
---

messages:
From: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>
To: Asha Raman <asha.raman@brightbridge.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 15 Jul 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Recap + MAP draft: Pilot environment networking & SSO next steps

Hi Asha —

Thanks again for the time this morning. Quick recap and an initial MAP attachment (see MAP-draft-2026-07-15.xlsx) with proposed milestones, owners, and a suggested target close. I wanted to capture the dependencies we discussed for the pilot environment so we can move to scheduling the hands-on setup window.

Top-line proposed milestones (short form):
- M1: Network connectivity — VPC peering + routing + IP allowlists (owner: BrightBridge NetOps)
- M2: Identity federation — SAML metadata exchange and test user (owner: BrightBridge IdP / Redwood SRE)
- M3: Secrets & encryption — KMS key access policy + secret injection (owner: BrightBridge Security / Redwood Infra)
- M4: Smoke test & validation — test prompts, latency baseline, token accounting (owner: Redwood Solutions)

Target overall pilot readiness date: 2026-08-20 (flexible by 1–2 weeks depending on NetOps bandwidth).

Immediate asks from BrightBridge for the MAP items above:
1) CIDR blocks for the pilot VPC and the preferred peering account ID / AWS account number.
2) IP ranges to add to our allowlist for the initial test window (we recommend a tight window first).
3) SAML metadata (EntityID + ACS URL) or a test IdP SAML file we can import.
4) KMS key ARN and the principal to grant decrypt/encrypt to our service role (we can provide a sample IAM policy).

Next internal steps (Marissa / Rishi / Priya): please review the attached MAP and add any technical notes or constraints by EOD tomorrow so I can send a consolidated version to BrightBridge.

Thanks — I’ll follow up on scheduling the 2-hour setup session once we have the CIDR and SAML items.

Jonas Weber
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
jonas.weber@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0123

Attachments: MAP-draft-2026-07-15.xlsx, network-diagram-vpc-peering.pdf
From: Asha Raman <asha.raman@brightbridge.ai>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>
Cc: BrightBridge Network Team <netops@brightbridge.ai>, Michael Lowe <m.lowe@consultingpartners.com>
Date: Wed, 15 Jul 2026 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Recap + MAP draft: Pilot environment networking & SSO next steps

Thanks Jonas — this is helpful. We reviewed the MAP and have a few clarifying notes. High level: we can meet the 2026-08-20 date if NetOps prioritizes the peering work this month. A couple of specifics from our side: 

- CIDR / account: our pilot VPC is 10.12.0.0/16 and the AWS account is 987654321098.
- IP allowlist: we prefer to start with two IPs for the initial test (72.18.34.12, 72.18.34.13) and will expand after smoke tests.
- SAML: attaching our IdP metadata (see below). We have a dedicated test SP entry you can use.
- KMS: KMS key ARN is arn:aws:kms:us-west-2:987654321098:key/aa11bb22-cc33-44dd-55ee-ffffff666666. We need the sample policy before we open a ticket with Security.

We will loop in NetOps to coordinate peering windows. Michael from Consulting Partners will help on the network diagram and routing.

Asha Raman
Director, Platform Engineering | BrightBridge Analytics
asha.raman@brightbridge.ai

Attachments: brightbridge-idp-metadata.xml
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>, Asha Raman <asha.raman@brightbridge.ai>
Date: Thu, 16 Jul 2026 08:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Recap + MAP draft: Pilot environment networking & SSO next steps

Jonas / team —

Thanks for pulling this together. I reviewed the MAP and added inline comments to the spreadsheet (high level notes below). I’m also adding explicit owners and estimated engineering days per milestone so procurement has more visibility: 

- M1 Network connectivity (Owner: BrightBridge NetOps / Redwood Infra contact: Rishi) — 2–3 days for peering + route propagation; confirm security controls for BGP/route tables.
- M2 Identity federation (Owner: BrightBridge IdP / Redwood SRE contact: Priya) — 1 day to exchange metadata + 1/2 day to validate test SSO account.
- M3 Secrets / KMS (Owner: BrightBridge Security / Redwood Infra) — 1 day to agree on key policy, 1 day to test secret injection.
- M4 Smoke test (Owner: Redwood Solutions / Asha) — 0.5–1 day for functional tests, plus token/cost baseline run.

Rishi: I'll need a short-runbook snippet for the peering steps and the sample IAM policy for KMS so Asha can open the Security ticket. Priya: please prepare a short checklist of the SAML attributes we require and example SP settings (ACS, NameID format).

If BrightBridge confirms the two IPs and KMS ARN (Asha — thanks), I propose we schedule a 2-hour window the week of 2026-07-27 for the initial peering and network validation, then a separate 1-hour session for SAML the following week. If NetOps can't do the 27th window, move to the week of 2026-08-03.

Marissa Cole
Director, Customer Success | Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.ai

> On Jul 15, 2026, at 09:12, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai> wrote:
> 
> > Top-line proposed milestones (short form):
> > - M1: Network connectivity — VPC peering + routing + IP allowlists (owner: BrightBridge NetOps)
> > - M2: Identity federation — SAML metadata exchange and test user (owner: BrightBridge IdP / Redwood SRE)
> > - M3: Secrets & encryption — KMS key access policy + secret injection (owner: BrightBridge Security / Redwood Infra)
> > - M4: Smoke test & validation — test prompts, latency baseline, token accounting (owner: Redwood Solutions)
From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Asha Raman <asha.raman@brightbridge.ai>
Date: Fri, 17 Jul 2026 15:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Recap + MAP draft: Pilot environment networking & SSO next steps

All — quick confirmation after reviewing Asha's details. We can proceed with the proposed plan. Notes: 

- Peering: Redwood peering account will be 123450987654; we’ll initiate the peering request once BrightBridge opens their side. Route propagation typically completes within 40–60 mins, but DNS and firewall rules may take longer depending on change windows.
- IP allowlist: we'll accept the two IPs for the initial window. Please confirm the exact timestamp (UTC) for the 2-hour window so we can provision temporary firewall rules.
- SAML: Priya and I will coordinate the metadata exchange. Priya — I sent the sample SP/ACS URL and test attributes to Asha in a separate note.
- KMS: Jonas — I’ll draft the sample KMS policy (minimal permissions) and post to the MAP spreadsheet by EOD Monday. BrightBridge Security will still need to approve.

Proposed immediate next actions (action owners):
- BrightBridge (Asha / NetOps): confirm peering request open + schedule 2-hour window (owner: Asha).
- Redwood (Rishi): prepare KMS sample policy + create peering request template (owner: Rishi).
- Redwood (Priya): share SAML test SP details and attribute checklist (owner: Priya).
- Redwood (Jonas / Marissa): finalize MAP with updated owners & send final to procurement (owner: Jonas).

Target pilot readiness: keeping 2026-08-20 as the MAP goal but flexible. If the peering window is scheduled in the last week of July we’re on track.

Rishi Malhotra
Infrastructure Engineer | Redwood Inference
rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai

> On Jul 16, 2026, at 08:22, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai> wrote:
> > Rishi: I'll need a short-runbook snippet for the peering steps and the sample IAM policy for KMS so Asha can open the Security ticket. Priya: please prepare a short checklist of the SAML attributes we require and example SP settings (ACS, NameID format).

Next internal steps (Marissa / Rishi / Priya): please review the attached MAP and add any technical notes or constraints by EOD tomorrow so I can send a consolidated version to BrightBridge.

Thanks — I’ll follow up on scheduling the 2-hour setup session once we have the CIDR and SAML items.

Jonas Weber
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
jonas.weber@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0123

Attachments: MAP-draft-2026-07-15.xlsx, network-diagram-vpc-peering.pdf


From: Asha Raman <asha.raman@brightbridge.ai>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>
Cc: BrightBridge Network Team <netops@brightbridge.ai>, Michael Lowe <m.lowe@consultingpartners.com>
Date: Wed, 15 Jul 2026 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Recap + MAP draft: Pilot environment networking & SSO next steps

Thanks Jonas — this is helpful. We reviewed the MAP and have a few clarifying notes. High level: we can meet the 2026-08-20 date if NetOps prioritizes the peering work this month. A couple of specifics from our side: 

- CIDR / account: our pilot VPC is 10.12.0.0/16 and the AWS account is 987654321098.
- IP allowlist: we prefer to start with two IPs for the initial test (72.18.34.12, 72.18.34.13) and will expand after smoke tests.
- SAML: attaching our IdP metadata (see below). We have a dedicated test SP entry you can use.
- KMS: KMS key ARN is arn:aws:kms:us-west-2:987654321098:key/aa11bb22-cc33-44dd-55ee-ffffff666666. We need the sample policy before we open a ticket with Security.

We will loop in NetOps to coordinate peering windows. Michael from Consulting Partners will help on the network diagram and routing.

Asha Raman
Director, Platform Engineering | BrightBridge Analytics
asha.raman@brightbridge.ai

Attachments: brightbridge-idp-metadata.xml


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>, Asha Raman <asha.raman@brightbridge.ai>
Date: Thu, 16 Jul 2026 08:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Recap + MAP draft: Pilot environment networking & SSO next steps

Jonas / team —

Thanks for pulling this together. I reviewed the MAP and added inline comments to the spreadsheet (high level notes below). I’m also adding explicit owners and estimated engineering days per milestone so procurement has more visibility: 

- M1 Network connectivity (Owner: BrightBridge NetOps / Redwood Infra contact: Rishi) — 2–3 days for peering + route propagation; confirm security controls for BGP/route tables.
- M2 Identity federation (Owner: BrightBridge IdP / Redwood SRE contact: Priya) — 1 day to exchange metadata + 1/2 day to validate test SSO account.
- M3 Secrets / KMS (Owner: BrightBridge Security / Redwood Infra) — 1 day to agree on key policy, 1 day to test secret injection.
- M4 Smoke test (Owner: Redwood Solutions / Asha) — 0.5–1 day for functional tests, plus token/cost baseline run.

Rishi: I'll need a short-runbook snippet for the peering steps and the sample IAM policy for KMS so Asha can open the Security ticket. Priya: please prepare a short checklist of the SAML attributes we require and example SP settings (ACS, NameID format).

If BrightBridge confirms the two IPs and KMS ARN (Asha — thanks), I propose we schedule a 2-hour window the week of 2026-07-27 for the initial peering and network validation, then a separate 1-hour session for SAML the following week. If NetOps can't do the 27th window, move to the week of 2026-08-03.

Marissa Cole
Director, Customer Success | Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.ai

> On Jul 15, 2026, at 09:12, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai> wrote:
> 
> > Top-line proposed milestones (short form):
> > - M1: Network connectivity — VPC peering + routing + IP allowlists (owner: BrightBridge NetOps)
> > - M2: Identity federation — SAML metadata exchange and test user (owner: BrightBridge IdP / Redwood SRE)
> > - M3: Secrets & encryption — KMS key access policy + secret injection (owner: BrightBridge Security / Redwood Infra)
> > - M4: Smoke test & validation — test prompts, latency baseline, token accounting (owner: Redwood Solutions)


From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Asha Raman <asha.raman@brightbridge.ai>
Date: Fri, 17 Jul 2026 15:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Recap + MAP draft: Pilot environment networking & SSO next steps

All — quick confirmation after reviewing Asha's details. We can proceed with the proposed plan. Notes: 

- Peering: Redwood peering account will be 123450987654; we’ll initiate the peering request once BrightBridge opens their side. Route propagation typically completes within 40–60 mins, but DNS and firewall rules may take longer depending on change windows.
- IP allowlist: we'll accept the two IPs for the initial window. Please confirm the exact timestamp (UTC) for the 2-hour window so we can provision temporary firewall rules.
- SAML: Priya and I will coordinate the metadata exchange. Priya — I sent the sample SP/ACS URL and test attributes to Asha in a separate note.
- KMS: Jonas — I’ll draft the sample KMS policy (minimal permissions) and post to the MAP spreadsheet by EOD Monday. BrightBridge Security will still need to approve.

Proposed immediate next actions (action owners):
- BrightBridge (Asha / NetOps): confirm peering request open + schedule 2-hour window (owner: Asha).
- Redwood (Rishi): prepare KMS sample policy + create peering request template (owner: Rishi).
- Redwood (Priya): share SAML test SP details and attribute checklist (owner: Priya).
- Redwood (Jonas / Marissa): finalize MAP with updated owners & send final to procurement (owner: Jonas).

Target pilot readiness: keeping 2026-08-20 as the MAP goal but flexible. If the peering window is scheduled in the last week of July we’re on track.

Rishi Malhotra
Infrastructure Engineer | Redwood Inference
rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai

> On Jul 16, 2026, at 08:22, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai> wrote:
> > Rishi: I'll need a short-runbook snippet for the peering steps and the sample IAM policy for KMS so Asha can open the Security ticket. Priya: please prepare a short checklist of the SAML attributes we require and example SP settings (ACS, NameID format).

messages:
From: Amy Carter <amy.carter@brightwell.ai>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Darren Holt <darren.holt@brightwell.ai>
Date: Sun, 12 Jul 2026 15:03:00 -0700
Subject: Launchpad eval — draft owners & timeline

Hi Jonas,

Thanks again for the demo last week — our team left excited about the latency improvements and the rolling fallback story. Darren and I sketched a short evaluation charter so we can move to a scoped pilot quickly. Can you review the owners and timing below and share a MAP template (GDoc) we can use to track open items?

Proposed high level timeline
- Week 1 (Jul 19–23): Technical kick-off, test data exchange, baseline perf capture
- Week 2 (Jul 26–30): Integration + short pilot on 1 model variant
- Week 3 (Aug 2–6): Quality checks, cost validation, security sign-off
- Goal: decision by Aug 13 for production go/no-go

Owners (Brightwell): Darren (SRE), Amy (Product)
Owners (Redwood): ? (tech lead), ? (CS/eng sponsor)

We also have a procurement checkpoint on Aug 6 — would appreciate a pricing anchor and a shortlist of dedicated vs hosted options.

Attaching a short slide with scope. Happy to hop on a 30-min call if that helps.

Thanks,
Amy

--
Amy Carter
Senior Product Manager | Brightwell Analytics
amy.carter@brightwell.ai | +1 (415) 555-0192

Attachment: Scope_Slides_v1.pptx (pptx, 2 slides)
From: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwoodinference.com>
To: Amy Carter <amy.carter@brightwell.ai>
Cc: Darren Holt <darren.holt@brightwell.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 13 Jul 2026 09:31:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Launchpad eval — draft owners & timeline

Hi Amy — great to see a tight timeline. I created a working MAP GDoc and seeded the owners section; link below. I also attached our MAP template so you can copy into your tracker.

Quick notes:
- Proposed Redwood owners: Jonas Weber (Technical lead), Marissa Cole (Customer Success) and Priya Natarajan (Solutions / procurement path).
- We'll aim to provide hosted baseline pricing and a sketch for Dedicated capacity by Aug 4 so you have numbers ahead of the Aug 6 procurement checkpoint.
- For the pilot, recommend a 24–48 hour steady-state run for representative traffic to measure cost/latency.

GDoc (initial): https://docs.google.com/document/d/1A2b3Claunchpad-gdoc
Attachment: Launchpad_MAP_Template_v1.gdoc (Google Doc template)

If you want, Marissa and I can run the technical kick-off on Jul 20 or Jul 21 — which works better for your SRE team?

Best,
Jonas

--
Jonas Weber
Account Executive, Strategic Accounts | Redwood Inference
jonas.weber@redwoodinference.com | +1 (650) 555-0146
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Amy Carter <amy.carter@brightwell.ai>, Darren Holt <darren.holt@brightwell.ai>
Date: Mon, 13 Jul 2026 13:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Launchpad eval — draft owners & timeline

Jonas — quick internal sync before we lock the doc. A few action items I want captured in the MAP:
- Data flow and PII: Brightwell will share a redacted sample set; we need confirmation on retention window and whether on-hosted API or private deployment is required for that dataset.
- Eval criteria: Latency p50/p95 targets, token cost per 1M tokens, correctness QA (sample prompts + expected outputs), and a rollback criteria.
- Security evidence: checklist for SOC2 items they request; Priya to own the questionnaire turnaround and provide our standard evidence pack.

Priya — can you draft the procurement checklist and attach our standard SOC2 summary to the GDoc? I put placeholders in the template and tagged you.

Also Jonas — Jul 21 works for me for a 30-min tech kickoff at 10:00 PT unless Brightwell prefers the 20th.

Marissa

--
Marissa Cole
Director, Customer Success | Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0168
From: Amy Carter <amy.carter@brightwell.ai>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Jul 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Launchpad eval — draft owners & timeline

Thanks — Jul 21 at 10:00 PT is perfect for us. Darren will send a calendar invite.

We reviewed the draft GDoc and left a couple of comments on the evaluation criteria (mostly around sample prompts and the traffic profile). We'll populate the sample data details by Jul 19.

Amy

--
Amy Carter
Senior Product Manager | Brightwell Analytics
amy.carter@brightwell.ai

source.subject: Novus pilot: tempo uplift & throttle handoff — coordination needed
messages:
From: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-07-13T08:14:00Z
Subject: Novus pilot: tempo uplift & throttle handoff — coordination needed

Team —

Novus is going live with their closed beta on Monday (they confirmed nights-of-traffic starting 2026-07-18). They just sent a last-minute ramp schedule and are asking for a temporary ingress uplift during the first 72 hours of the pilot plus a short-term higher concurrency window for warmstarts. Key asks from Novus:
- 2x nominal RPS for first 48 hours (expected peak: 220 RPS, baseline 110)
- Temporary increase to token throughput for streaming requests (peak ~1.6M tokens/min)
- Warmstart KV cache retention for conversation sessions (they expect 10–15s between follow-up calls)

I need us to confirm feasibility and the operational checklist: entitlements from Billing, IP whitelist, route tag + canary rollout, and an emergency throttling rollback plan. Priya — what product toggles do we need to enable for warmstart retention and fallback model variant? Karthik — can infra support a 2x uplift for 48 hours with a safe drain plan? Marcus/Evelyn — any telemetry/alerting we should pre-configure in Console?

I've attached their forecast spreadsheet and the entitlement request they emailed. I'll loop in Novus after we agree on the internal playbook.

Thanks,
Avery
Avery Johnson
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

Attachments: novus_warmstart_forecast.xlsx (spreadsheet), entitlement_request_novus.pdf (PDF)
From: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-07-13T09:02:00Z
Subject: Re: Novus pilot: tempo uplift & throttle handoff — coordination needed

Thanks for the heads-up, Avery. I skimmed the forecast — helpful. A few product/roadmap clarifications before we commit to an entitlement:

1) Warmstart KV semantics: we can increase per-session KV retention to 60s for the pilot but it will impact memory usage on the serving pool. We should scope which routes actually need this — recommend enabling for the /chat/stream route only.

2) Fallback model variant: we can prepare a quantized 8-bit variant (same architecture) that reduces GPU token cost ~40%. We need ~24 hours to validate quality on their smoke prompts and to add the variant to the routing policy.

3) Throttle window & quotas: product can support a timebound ephemeral entitlement (48 hours max) that maps to a temp rate limit and a post-window automatic rollback. We should specify exact start/end UTC times in the entitlement doc.

Questions:
- Expected percent of requests that are streaming vs non-streaming? The forecast lists tokens but not streaming mix.
- Do they plan long-running streaming sessions (>30s)? That changes KV and connection resource planning.

If we get answers by EOD we can ship the fallback model and the entitlement change in time.

—Priya
Priya Natarajan
Product Manager, Inference

On 2026-07-13 08:14, Avery Johnson wrote:
> Team —
> 
> Novus is going live with their closed beta on Monday (they confirmed nights-of-traffic starting 2026-07-18). They just sent a last-minute ramp schedule and are asking for a temporary ingress uplift during the first 72 hours of the pilot plus a short-term higher concurrency window for warmstarts.
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-07-13T10:45:00Z
Subject: Re: Novus pilot: tempo uplift & throttle handoff — coordination needed

We can support a temporary 2x uplift for 48 hours but a few infra caveats: 

- Pool capacity: current dedicated pool for pilot-grade tenants has ~30% spare headroom; pushing to 2x will require borrowing from the shared burst pool. That is feasible but we need an explicit borrow-and-return schedule to avoid impacting other pilots.

- KV cache retention: increasing retention to 60s is ok for the /chat/stream route only. We'll need to set per-route cache limits (max entries per session) — recommend max 256 tokens stored per session as a guardrail.

- Canary & rollback: We'll publish a route tag (novus-pilot-48h) and do a canary rollout at 10% for the first hour. If error rate or 95p latency increases beyond 2x baseline we'll auto-roll back. We need Product to sign off on the rollback SLA thresholds.

- Whitelisting: Novus must provide egress IP ranges for their client traffic (or we can accept a JWT assertion). Marcus — please coordinate with Network for the 2–3 CIDR blocks.

I'll push a runbook for ops and a short playbook for on-call to the shared drive once we confirm times.

-Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Solutions Engineering / Infra

On 2026-07-13 09:02, Priya Natarajan wrote:
> Thanks for the heads-up, Avery. I skimmed the forecast — helpful. A few product/roadmap clarifications before we commit to an entitlement...
From: Alice Park <alice@novus.ai>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>
Cc: CloudOps <cloudops@novus.ai>, Procurement <procurement@novus.ai>
Date: 2026-07-13T13:30:00Z
Subject: Re: Novus pilot: tempo uplift & throttle handoff — coordination needed

Hi Avery —

Thanks for coordinating. Quick answers from our side: 
- Streaming vs non-streaming: ~70% streaming (short bursts), 30% normal request/response. Median session ~12s, 95p ~45s.
- Ramp schedule: 2026-07-18 02:00–08:00 UTC (nights), 2026-07-19 scaled ramp 00:00–10:00 UTC. We'll provide a CSV with our expected IP egress ranges by EOD today.
- Procurement will review the entitlement doc; they asked whether we can get a one-time credit if we exceed the uptick during the pilot window (they want a safety net).

We appreciate the fallback variant offer — would like the 8-bit option on standby.

Best,
Alice Park
Head of Product, Novus AI

On Tue, Jul 13, 2026 at 08:14 AM Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com> wrote:
> Team —
> 
> Novus is going live with their closed beta on Monday...
From: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-07-14T11:20:00Z
Subject: Re: Novus pilot: tempo uplift & throttle handoff — coordination needed

From: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-07-13T08:14:00Z
Subject: Novus pilot: tempo uplift & throttle handoff — coordination needed

Team —

Novus is going live with their closed beta on Monday (they confirmed nights-of-traffic starting 2026-07-18). They just sent a last-minute ramp schedule and are asking for a temporary ingress uplift during the first 72 hours of the pilot plus a short-term higher concurrency window for warmstarts. Key asks from Novus:
- 2x nominal RPS for first 48 hours (expected peak: 220 RPS, baseline 110)
- Temporary increase to token throughput for streaming requests (peak ~1.6M tokens/min)
- Warmstart KV cache retention for conversation sessions (they expect 10–15s between follow-up calls)

I need us to confirm feasibility and the operational checklist: entitlements from Billing, IP whitelist, route tag + canary rollout, and an emergency throttling rollback plan. Priya — what product toggles do we need to enable for warmstart retention and fallback model variant? Karthik — can infra support a 2x uplift for 48 hours with a safe drain plan? Marcus/Evelyn — any telemetry/alerting we should pre-configure in Console?

I've attached their forecast spreadsheet and the entitlement request they emailed. I'll loop in Novus after we agree on the internal playbook.

Thanks,
Avery
Avery Johnson
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

Attachments: novus_warmstart_forecast.xlsx (spreadsheet), entitlement_request_novus.pdf (PDF)


From: Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-07-13T09:02:00Z
Subject: Re: Novus pilot: tempo uplift & throttle handoff — coordination needed

Thanks for the heads-up, Avery. I skimmed the forecast — helpful. A few product/roadmap clarifications before we commit to an entitlement:

1) Warmstart KV semantics: we can increase per-session KV retention to 60s for the pilot but it will impact memory usage on the serving pool. We should scope which routes actually need this — recommend enabling for the /chat/stream route only.

2) Fallback model variant: we can prepare a quantized 8-bit variant (same architecture) that reduces GPU token cost ~40%. We need ~24 hours to validate quality on their smoke prompts and to add the variant to the routing policy.

3) Throttle window & quotas: product can support a timebound ephemeral entitlement (48 hours max) that maps to a temp rate limit and a post-window automatic rollback. We should specify exact start/end UTC times in the entitlement doc.

Questions:
- Expected percent of requests that are streaming vs non-streaming? The forecast lists tokens but not streaming mix.
- Do they plan long-running streaming sessions (>30s)? That changes KV and connection resource planning.

If we get answers by EOD we can ship the fallback model and the entitlement change in time.

—Priya
Priya Natarajan
Product Manager, Inference

> Team —
> 
> Novus is going live with their closed beta on Monday (they confirmed nights-of-traffic starting 2026-07-18). They just sent a last-minute ramp schedule and are asking for a temporary ingress uplift during the first 72 hours of the pilot plus a short-term higher concurrency window for warmstarts.


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-07-13T10:45:00Z
Subject: Re: Novus pilot: tempo uplift & throttle handoff — coordination needed

We can support a temporary 2x uplift for 48 hours but a few infra caveats: 

- Pool capacity: current dedicated pool for pilot-grade tenants has ~30% spare headroom; pushing to 2x will require borrowing from the shared burst pool. That is feasible but we need an explicit borrow-and-return schedule to avoid impacting other pilots.

- KV cache retention: increasing retention to 60s is ok for the /chat/stream route only. We'll need to set per-route cache limits (max entries per session) — recommend max 256 tokens stored per session as a guardrail.

- Canary & rollback: We'll publish a route tag (novus-pilot-48h) and do a canary rollout at 10% for the first hour. If error rate or 95p latency increases beyond 2x baseline we'll auto-roll back. We need Product to sign off on the rollback SLA thresholds.

- Whitelisting: Novus must provide egress IP ranges for their client traffic (or we can accept a JWT assertion). Marcus — please coordinate with Network for the 2–3 CIDR blocks.

I'll push a runbook for ops and a short playbook for on-call to the shared drive once we confirm times.

-Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Solutions Engineering / Infra

Hi Avery —

Thanks for coordinating. Quick answers from our side: 
- Streaming vs non-streaming: ~70% streaming (short bursts), 30% normal request/response. Median session ~12s, 95p ~45s.
- Ramp schedule: 2026-07-18 02:00–08:00 UTC (nights), 2026-07-19 scaled ramp 00:00–10:00 UTC. We'll provide a CSV with our expected IP egress ranges by EOD today.
- Procurement will review the entitlement doc; they asked whether we can get a one-time credit if we exceed the uptick during the pilot window (they want a safety net).

We appreciate the fallback variant offer — would like the 8-bit option on standby.

Best,
Alice Park

source.subject: Invoice recount & token miscount — Novus Health (June billing)
messages:
From: Sara Kim <sara.kim@novushealth.com>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.ai>
Cc: Avery Johnson <avery@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 29 Jun 2026 08:21:00 -0700
Subject: Unexpected June invoice — token double-count?

Hi Hannah,

We received our June invoice this morning (attached) and are seeing a ~2x spike in token counts compared to our dashboard and expected usage pattern. Can you confirm whether this is a billing issue or if something on our side caused duplicate requests? Invoice total: $12,348.02 (invoice_NOVUS_202606.pdf attached).

We use the Redwood hosted API (prod key tied to HV-3245). We noticed the spike around 2026-06-14 00:00 UTC and again on 2026-06-21; our app logs show retries but not at the scale of the billed tokens. We need to understand quickly — can your team review for duplicate counting or replay?

Thanks for the quick look — Sara
Sara Kim
Head of Engineering, Novus Health
Sara.Kim@novushealth.com

---
attachments: invoice_NOVUS_202606.pdf (application/pdf)
From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.ai>
To: Sara Kim <sara.kim@novushealth.com>
Cc: Avery Johnson <avery@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 29 Jun 2026 09:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Unexpected June invoice — token double-count?

Hi Sara — thanks for flagging this and sharing the invoice. I pulled your billing window and am escalating to engineering + billing ops to triage immediately.

Quick notes/actions:
- I've attached the invoice you sent to our case and will reference it in the investigation.
- Looping in Marcus (billing ops) and Avery (Customer Success) to coordinate credits and a temporary hold if we find a platform-side issue.
- Engineering (Ethan) will check request logs, token accounting, and any replay/duplication events for June 14–22.

We aim to have an initial update within 24 hours and a proposed remediation/credit plan within 48 hours. Sorry for the disruption — we'll prioritize.

Best,
Hannah Schmitt
Account Lead, Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference
hannah@redwood.ai

---
attachments: invoice_NOVUS_202606.pdf (application/pdf)
From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.ai>
To: Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>
Cc: Avery Johnson <avery@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 29 Jun 2026 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Unexpected June invoice — urgent engineering assist needed

FYI forwarding Novus' note (below). Sara reports presumed double-counted tokens on HV-3245 around 2026-06-14 and 2026-06-21. Please treat as high priority — Novus is a mid-size healthcare customer with sensitive SLAs.

What I need:
- Ethan: can you surface request-level logs for the API key tied to HV-3245 for 2026-06-13 through 2026-06-22 (include sequence lengths and any retry/replay indications)? Look for duplicate requests, replays from the router, or any cache/keyV mismatches.
- Priya: if this looks platform-side, outline the corrective credit math and a temp credit we can issue while we finish the postmortem.
- Marcus: prepare a draft credit/adjustment notice for Novus that we can send once engineering confirms root cause.

I forwarded the customer's invoice as well. Please reply-all with findings and timing.

On Mon, Jun 29, 2026 at 08:21 Sara Kim <sara.kim@novushealth.com> wrote:
> Hi Hannah,
>
> We received our June invoice this morning (attached) and are seeing a ~2x spike in token counts compared to our dashboard and expected usage pattern. Can you confirm whether this is a billing issue or if something on our side caused duplicate requests? Invoice total: $12,348.02 (invoice_NOVUS_202606.pdf attached).
>
> We use the Redwood hosted API (prod key tied to HV-3245). We noticed the spike around 2026-06-14 00:00 UTC and again on 2026-06-21; our app logs show retries but not at the scale of the billed tokens. We need to understand quickly — can your team review for duplicate counting or replay?
>
> Thanks for the quick look — Sara
> Sara Kim
> Head of Engineering, Novus Health
> Sara.Kim@novushealth.com
From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.ai>
To: Sara Kim <sara.kim@novushealth.com>
Cc: Avery Johnson <avery@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>, Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 02 Jul 2026 16:40:00 -0700
Subject: Resolution: token recount, credit issued, and postmortem link (Novus Health)

Hi Sara — following up with final findings and the customer-facing remediation plan. Thanks for your patience.

Summary (what happened)
- Root cause: We had a transient router replay bug during an autoscaling event on 2026-06-14 that caused certain in-flight requests to be replayed to the inference runtime. The replay path did not deduplicate at the token-accounting layer for a short window, which resulted in duplicate token counts for affected requests.
- Affected window: 2026-06-14 00:00 UTC — 2026-06-14 03:30 UTC (primary), with a smaller replay burst on 2026-06-21 11:45 UTC — 2026-06-21 12:10 UTC.
- Scope: 11% of Novus' billed tokens for June were double-counted; total overbill ≈ $1,362.40.

Immediate remediation we applied
- We issued a customer credit of $1,362.40 against your July billing cycle. Credit notice attached (credit_notice_NOVUS_2026-07-02.pdf).
- Engineering implemented a quick server-side fix in the routing layer to dedupe replayed requests at the accounting boundary. The change was rolled to prod on 2026-06-30 and validated.
- We created a short-term monitoring alert and throttle for autoscaling windows to prevent a recurrence while we complete a full postmortem.

Deliverables and next steps
- Postmortem: public-facing postmortem document is linked here for review: https://drive.redwood.ai/postmortems/2026-06-token-recount (internal full doc attached as postmortem_token_recount_2026-06.docx).
- Compliance: We retained request logs and counters and are ready to share filtered CSVs for your auditors if needed.
- Confirmation: please reply to confirm the credit applied and whether you need the audit extracts or a different remediation timeline.

We're sorry for the disruption to your billing and are treating this as a priority reliability gap. Let me know if you'd like a call to walk through the postmortem — Avery and Marcus are available early next week.

Best,
Hannah Schmitt
Account Lead, Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference
hannah@redwood.ai

title: Invoice recount & token miscount — Novus Health (June billing)
preview:
From: Sara Kim <sara.kim@novushealth.com>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.ai>
Cc: Avery Johnson <avery@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 29 Jun 2026 08:21:00 -0700
Subject: Unexpected June invoice — token double-count?

Hi Hannah,

We received our June invoice this morning (attached) and are seeing a ~2x spike in token counts compared to our dashboard and expected usage pattern. Can you confirm whether this is a billing issue or if something on our side caused duplicate requests? Invoice total: $12,348.02 (invoice_NOVUS_202606.pdf attached).

We use the Redwood hosted API (prod key tied to HV-3245). We noticed the spike around 2026-06-14 00:00 UTC and again on 2026-06-21; our app logs show retries but not at the scale of the billed tokens. We need to understand quickly — can your team review for duplicate counting or replay?

Thanks for the quick look — Sara
Sara Kim
Head of Engineering, Novus Health
Sara.Kim@novushealth.com

From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.ai>
To: Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>
Cc: Avery Johnson <avery@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 29 Jun 2026 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Unexpected June invoice — urgent engineering assist needed

FYI forwarding Novus' note (below). Sara reports presumed double-counted tokens on HV-3245 around 2026-06-14 and 2026-06-21. Please treat as high priority — Novus is a mid-size healthcare customer with sensitive SLAs.

What I need:
- Ethan: can you surface request-level logs for the API key tied to HV-3245 for 2026-06-13 through 2026-06-22 (include sequence lengths and any retry/replay indications)? Look for duplicate requests, replays from the router, or any cache/keyV mismatches.
- Priya: if this looks platform-side, outline the corrective credit math and a temp credit we can issue while we finish the postmortem.
- Marcus: prepare a draft credit/adjustment notice for Novus that we can send once engineering confirms root cause.

I forwarded the customer's invoice as well. Please reply-all with findings and timing.

On Mon, Jun 29, 2026 at 08:21 Sara Kim <sara.kim@novushealth.com> wrote:
> Hi Hannah,
>
> We received our June invoice this morning (attached) and are seeing a ~2x spike in token counts compared to our dashboard and expected usage pattern. Can you confirm whether this is a billing issue or if something on our side caused duplicate requests? Invoice total: $12,348.02 (invoice_NOVUS_202606.pdf attached).
>
> We use the Redwood hosted API (prod key tied to HV-3245). We noticed the spike around 2026-06-14 00:00 UTC and again on 2026-06-21; our app logs show retries but not at the scale of the billed tokens. We need to understand quickly — can your team review for duplicate counting or replay?
>
> Thanks for the quick look — Sara
> Sara Kim
> Head of Engineering, Novus Health
> Sara.Kim@novushealth.com


From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.ai>
To: Sara Kim <sara.kim@novushealth.com>
Cc: Avery Johnson <avery@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>, Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.ai>, Priya Natarajan <priya_natarajan@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 02 Jul 2026 16:40:00 -0700
Subject: Resolution: token recount, credit issued, and postmortem link (Novus Health)

Hi Sara — following up with final findings and the customer-facing remediation plan. Thanks for your patience.

Summary (what happened)
- Root cause: We had a transient router replay bug during an autoscaling event on 2026-06-14 that caused certain in-flight requests to be replayed to the inference runtime. The replay path did not deduplicate at the token-accounting layer for a short window, which resulted in duplicate token counts for affected requests.
- Affected window: 2026-06-14 00:00 UTC — 2026-06-14 03:30 UTC (primary), with a smaller replay burst on 2026-06-21 11:45 UTC — 2026-06-21 12:10 UTC.
- Scope: 11% of Novus' billed tokens for June were double-counted; total overbill ≈ $1,362.40.

Immediate remediation we applied
- We issued a customer credit of $1,362.40 against your July billing cycle. Credit notice attached (credit_notice_NOVUS_2026-07-02.pdf).
- Engineering implemented a quick server-side fix in the routing layer to dedupe replayed requests at the accounting boundary. The change was rolled to prod on 2026-06-30 and validated.
- We created a short-term monitoring alert and throttle for autoscaling windows to prevent a recurrence while we complete a full postmortem.

Deliverables and next steps
- Postmortem: public-facing postmortem document is linked here for review: https://drive.redwood.ai/postmortems/2026-06-token-recount (internal full doc attached as postmortem_token_recount_2026-06.docx).
- Compliance: We retained request logs and counters and are ready to share filtered CSVs for your auditors if needed.
- Confirmation: please reply to confirm the credit applied and whether you need the audit extracts or a different remediation timeline.

We're sorry for the disruption to your billing and are treating this as a priority reliability gap. Let me know if you'd like a call to walk through the postmortem — Avery and Marcus are available early next week.

Best,
Hannah Schmitt
Account Lead, Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference
hannah@redwood.ai

---
attachments: postmortem_token_recount_2026-06.docx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document), credit_notice_NOVUS_2026-07-02.pdf (application/pdf)

source.subject: Re: Evaluation gates & MAP — hosting decision for clinical pilot
messages:
From: Brenda Long <brenda.long@medtech.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Cc: Samir Patel <samir.patel@medtech.com>
Date: Mon, 28 Jun 2026 15:02:00 -0700
Subject: Evaluation gates & MAP — hosting decision for clinical pilot

Hi Alex / Priya,

Thanks again for last week's demo. As we discussed, the steering committee needs a short mutual action plan so we can lock a pilot decision by mid-July. Two things are blocking the contract: (1) clear hosting recommendation (Hosted API vs Dedicated capacity vs Private deployment) for our pilot dataset (PHI subset, US-only), and (2) a validated timeline with security gating (SOC 2 evidence + our questionnaire).

Attached is our draft MAP (MedTech_MAP_v2.xlsx) and our security questionnaire (security_questionnaire.pdf). High level asks from our side:
- Deployment mode recommendation with tradeoffs (latency, cost, data residency, SLA)
- Estimated run costs for a 3-month pilot (token volumes attached in MAP)
- Evaluation gates and minimum success criteria (latency P95, throughput, false-positive rate on redaction)
- Region options — we prefer us-east for latency but need clarification on data residency and encryption in transit/at rest.

Our steering committee wants a firm commit or a clear go/no-go path by 2026-07-15 so procurement can start PO paperwork. Can you send a proposed MAP with milestones + dates and a recommended hosting mode by EOD 2026-06-30?

Thanks —
Brenda
Brenda Long
Head of Platform Partnerships, MedTech Inc.
brenda.long@medtech.com
+1 (415) 555-0129

Attachments: MedTech_MAP_v2.xlsx (spreadsheet), security_questionnaire.pdf (PDF, 12 pages)
From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
To: Brenda Long <brenda.long@medtech.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 29 Jun 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Evaluation gates & MAP — hosting decision for clinical pilot

Brenda — thanks for the clear ask and the attachments. Drafting a concise MAP and recommendation now. High-level response below; I will attach an updated MAP workbook (MedTech_MAP_v2_redwood_comments.xlsx) by EOD Tuesday.

Short recommendation (pilot scope):
- Recommended mode: Hosted API (us-east-1) for fastest time-to-value and to avoid initial procurement friction.
  - Pros: no infra procurement, Redwood manages SLAs and autoscaling, fastest onboarding (1–2 weeks).
  - Cons: shared tenancy (we provide strict data isolation controls), possibly higher unit costs vs Dedicated.
- Dedicated capacity: attractive if you need predictable SLOs + cost controls for sustained high QPS; requires reserved capacity contract and ~4–6 week lead time.
- Private (VPC/on‑prem): recommended only if legal requires customer-controlled keys or air-gap. Longer setup (6–10+ weeks) and heavier Ops.

Security gating: we can provide SOC 2 artifacts and a completed questionnaire. We will also propose an encrypted-in-transit and encrypted-at-rest default for the pilot, and offer customer-managed KMS if you need key separation (this pulls the thread toward Dedicated/Private).

Proposed MAP milestones (short):
1) Security review complete (Redwood provides SOC 2 + questionnaire answers) — target: 2026-07-06
2) Technical eval (integration + smoke tests, 7 days) — target window: 2026-07-07 to 2026-07-13
3) Performance gate (latency P95 < 150ms for typical prompt mix OR documented fallback to smaller model) — by 2026-07-13
4) Contract & PO signoff — target: 2026-07-15

To hit these dates we need:
- Rishi / infra team: quick costed estimate for Dedicated reserved pool (3-month pilot profile) and delta vs hosted (units).
- CS (Priya): success criteria & canary plan for integration and data loading.
- MedTech: confirmation on whether customer-managed KMS or standard Redwood keys are acceptable for the pilot.

Looping in Rishi for the cost numbers. Priya — can you draft the canary plan? I will prepare the updated MAP and call out the decisions that shift us between Hosted/Dedicated/Private.

Best,
Alex Martinez
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwood.com | +1 (650) 555-0190

On Mon, Jun 28, 2026 at 3:02 PM Brenda Long <brenda.long@medtech.com> wrote:
> Hi Alex / Priya,
> 
> Thanks again for last week's demo. As we discussed...
>
> (see attached MedTech_MAP_v2.xlsx, security_questionnaire.pdf)
From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>, Brenda Long <brenda.long@medtech.com>
Date: Tue, 29 Jun 2026 13:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Evaluation gates & MAP — hosting decision for clinical pilot

Thanks Alex — happy to own the evaluation plan from CS side. Quick notes on canary & success criteria:

Canary plan (7 days):
- Day 0–1: integration (API keys, test dataset upload — limited to synthetic/hashed PHI)
- Day 2–3: baseline latency and throughput tests (our harness + Redwood streaming)
- Day 4–5: end-to-end functional checks with redaction metrics and downstream consumer validation
- Day 6–7: stakeholder review and go/no-go decision

Success criteria (minimum):
- Latency: P95 < 200ms for typical prompt mix (prefer <150ms)
- Availability: >= 99% during canary window
- Redaction/PII handling: automated redaction passes 95% of vetted prompts
- Cost threshold: pilot projected cost + 20% buffer <= $12k/month (per MAP numbers)

If Hosted path is chosen we can be live faster; for Dedicated we should start PO conversations immediately (Rishi — can you provide a ballpark reserved capacity estimate for 3 months by 2026-07-01?).

Thanks —
Priya Natarajan
Customer Success Manager, Redwood Inference
priya.natarajan@redwood.com

On Tue, Jun 29, 2026 at 9:12 AM Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com> wrote:
> Brenda — thanks for the clear ask and the attachments. Drafting a concise MAP and recommendation now...
From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
To: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 30 Jun 2026 08:10:00 -0700
Subject: Internal: quick estimate for MedTech pilot (dedicated vs hosted)

Rishi / Marcus —

Following MedTech's MAP request (see thread), we need a short internal runbook and two cost points by EOD 2026-07-01:
- Hosted API estimate (3-month pilot): monthly token profile is in the MAP. Provide expected monthly spend and expected p95 latency in us-east-1.
- Dedicated reservation estimate (3-month pilot): upfront reservation delta, monthly burn, and time-to-provision (weeks). Include any special KMS/HSM integration overhead if customer-managed keys are requested.

Constraints: customer will accept Hosted if we can supply SOC 2 + questionnaire and meet latency P95 <150–200ms. If keys are customer-managed, they prefer Dedicated or Private.

Please paste short bullets here; I will fold into the MAP and send to Brenda.

Thanks,
Alex

On Tue, Jun 29, 2026 at 9:12 AM Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com> wrote:
> Brenda — thanks for the clear ask and the attachments...
From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
To: Brenda Long <brenda.long@medtech.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 30 Jun 2026 11:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Evaluation gates & MAP — hosting decision for clinical pilot  (updated MAP attached)

Brenda — attaching the updated MAP (MedTech_MAP_v2_redwood_comments.xlsx) and the Redwood security questionnaire responses. Quick summary and ask:

Executive summary / recommended path:
- Recommendation for pilot: Hosted API in us-east-1 to meet your mid-July timeline and minimize procurement friction. We will provide SOC 2 artifacts and completed questionnaire.
- If MedTech requires customer-managed KMS or hardware isolation, the alternative is Dedicated capacity in a single-tenant pool (provisioning time: ~3–5 weeks) or Private deployment (6–10+ weeks).

Updated MAP milestones & dates:
- 2026-07-01: Redwood provides completed security questionnaire + SOC 2 artifacts (DONE — attached)
- 2026-07-06: Security review complete (MedTech confirms)
- 2026-07-07 to 2026-07-13: Technical canary (see Priya's plan)
- 2026-07-13: Performance gate & stakeholder review
- 2026-07-15: Contract & PO signoff (target)

Action items for MedTech:
- Confirm whether customer-managed KMS is required for pilot (reply to this thread by 2026-07-01).
- Approve the MAP milestones above or propose adjust by 2026-07-01 so we can align procurement.

Action items for Redwood:
- Rishi: dedicated vs hosted cost delta (see internal note) — deliver by 2026-07-01.
- Priya: run canary plan and engineering handoff — ready to start on 2026-07-07.

If those dates look good I will schedule a 30-minute alignment call on 2026-07-02 to walk the steering committee through the MAP and the hosting tradeoffs. Please confirm availability.

Thanks,
Alex Martinez
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwood.com | +1 (650) 555-0190

Attachments: MedTech_MAP_v2_redwood_comments.xlsx (workbook), redwood_security_responses.pdf (PDF)

On Wed, Jun 30, 2026 at 8:10 AM Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com> wrote:
> Rishi / Marcus — Following MedTech's MAP request (see thread)...

Hi Alex / Priya,

Thanks again for last week's demo. As we discussed, the steering committee needs a short mutual action plan so we can lock a pilot decision by mid-July. Two things are blocking the contract: (1) clear hosting recommendation (Hosted API vs Dedicated capacity vs Private deployment) for our pilot dataset (PHI subset, US-only), and (2) a validated timeline with security gating (SOC 2 evidence + our questionnaire).

Attached is our draft MAP (MedTech_MAP_v2.xlsx) and our security questionnaire (security_questionnaire.pdf). High level asks from our side:
- Deployment mode recommendation with tradeoffs (latency, cost, data residency, SLA)
- Estimated run costs for a 3-month pilot (token volumes attached in MAP)

From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>

Date: Tue, 29 Jun 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Evaluation gates & MAP — hosting decision for clinical pilot

Brenda — thanks for the clear ask and the attachments. Drafting a concise MAP and recommendation now. High-level response below; I will attach an updated MAP workbook (MedTech_MAP_v2_redwood_comments.xlsx) by EOD Tuesday.

Short recommendation (pilot scope):
- Recommended mode: Hosted API (us-east-1) for fastest time-to-value and to avoid initial procurement friction.
  - Pros: no infra procurement, Redwood manages SLAs and autoscaling, fastest onboarding (1–2 weeks).
  - Cons: shared tenancy (we provide strict data isolation controls), possibly higher unit costs vs Dedicated.
- Dedicated capacity: attractive if you need predictable SLOs + cost controls for sustained high QPS; requires reserved capacity contract and ~4–6 week lead time.
- Private (VPC/on‑prem): recommended only if legal requires customer-controlled keys or air-gap. Longer setup (6–10+ weeks) and heavier Ops.

Security gating: we can provide SOC 2 artifacts and a completed questionnaire. We will also propose an encrypted-in-transit and encrypted-at-rest default for the pilot, and offer customer-managed KMS if you need key separation (this pulls the thread toward Dedicated/Private).

Proposed MAP milestones (short):
1) Security review complete (Redwood provides SOC 2 + questionnaire answers) — target: 2026-07-06
2) Technical eval (integration + smoke tests, 7 days) — target window: 2026-07-07 to 2026-07-13
3) Performance gate (latency P95 < 150ms for typical prompt mix OR documented fallback to smaller model) — by 2026-07-13
4) Contract & PO signoff — target: 2026-07-15

To hit these dates we need:
- Rishi / infra team: quick costed estimate for Dedicated reserved pool (3-month pilot profile) and delta vs hosted (units).
- CS (Priya): success criteria & canary plan for integration and data loading.
- MedTech: confirmation on whether customer-managed KMS or standard Redwood keys are acceptable for the pilot.

Looping in Rishi for the cost numbers. Priya — can you draft the canary plan? I will prepare the updated MAP and call out the decisions that shift us between Hosted/Dedicated/Private.

Best,
Alex Martinez
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwood.com | +1 (650) 555-0190

messages:
From: Oliver Grant <oliver@atlas.ai>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-06-24T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Performance evaluation & MAP for Atlas search use case

Jasmine —

Thanks for the quick intro call earlier. Atlas.ai is working on an enterprise search feature that will run vector+retrieval generation at 8–12 qps per shard, 1k avg tokens per request, and we need an objective performance and quality comparison across the Redwood catalog before signing a Dedicated commitment.

Key asks:
- Baseline perf metrics (TTFT, p95 latency, sustained throughput) for candidate variants under a 10-concurrency steady-state and 50-concurrency burst profile
- Quality comparison on our eval set (relevance + hallucination rate) for the same variants
- A mutual action plan with milestones to close by July 16 — procurement will need a finalized SLA and a runbook.

Can your team run a quick MAP-style benchmark and share a draft plan this week? We can provide the eval dataset and sample traffic.

—Oliver
Oliver Grant
Head of Product, Atlas.ai

From: Jasmine Liu <jasmine@redwood.com>
To: Oliver Grant <oliver@atlas.ai>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>, Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-06-24T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Performance evaluation & MAP for Atlas search use case

Oliver —

Appreciate the clarity. Attaching a first-draft Mutual Action Plan and benchmark plan (MAP_PerfPlan_v1.xlsx). High-level proposal below, we kept milestones tight to hit your July 16 close target: 

1) Today — ingest eval set and confirm traffic shape (Jasmine)
2) 6/25–6/27 — run controlled perf runs (Priya) with 3 model variants (rw-7b, rw-13b, rw-13b-quant) under 10c steady + 50c burst; measure TTFT (time-to-first-token), p50/p95, and token/s throughput
3) 6/28 — run quality eval against your labeled set (Ethan) and generate precision/hallucination metrics
4) 6/29 — deliver perf + quality matrix, recommended variant for pilot, and optional Dedicated sizing guidance
5) 7/1 — finalize MAP, contract redlines start, SOW/Procurement kickoff

Notes on metrics: we define TTFT as time from request accept to first token emitted at the application edge. We’ll also include cost-per-1k-requests estimates for each variant so you can compare unit economics.

If that timeline works I’ll kick off infra and tagging. Priya — can you confirm the test config (batching, seq lengths, quantization flags) and which model builds to run?

Thanks,

Jasmine Liu
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
Attachments: MAP_PerfPlan_v1.xlsx

From: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine@redwood.com>, Oliver Grant <oliver@atlas.ai>
Cc: Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-06-24T12:26:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Performance evaluation & MAP for Atlas search use case

Oliver / team —

Confirming we'll run the following controlled config set (attached summary as bench-configs.csv):
- Models: redwood-rw-7b, redwood-rw-13b, redwood-rw-13b-4bit (quantized)
- Concurrency profiles: steady 10, sustained 25, burst 50 (30s bursts every 5m)
- Request pattern: avg prompt 1k tokens; 95th percentile prompt 1.8k tokens
- Batching policy: continuous batching with max batch size 32, target latency SLO 250ms to first token for steady state
- Measurement: TTFT (edge->first token), p50/p95/p99 latency to first token, tokens/sec sustained, GPU utilization, failure/retry rate

We’ll run 3 repeats per config and provide median values. For the quantized build we’ll also include a short quality spot check.

Priya Natarajan
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
Attachments: bench-configs.csv

From: Oliver Grant <oliver@atlas.ai>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>, Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.com>, Maya Rios <maya.rios@atlas.ai>
Date: 2026-06-25T08:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Performance evaluation & MAP for Atlas search use case

Thanks — that config looks reasonable. A couple clarifications before you run: 
- Our eval set has ~12k queries; please sample ~2k for the perf runs to save cycles but run quality on the full set.
- For quality we care about top-1 relevance and hallucination flags (binary). We can provide the label schema by EOD.
- Can you include a short variant-comparison summary in the MAP that explicitly calls out quality/perf tradeoffs and a recommended pilot variant with estimated Dedicated GPU footprint?

If you can deliver the perf matrix by 6/29 we’ll prioritize procurement and legal on our side.

—Oliver

From: Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.com>
To: Oliver Grant <oliver@atlas.ai>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-06-28T14:41:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Performance evaluation & MAP for Atlas search use case

Oliver / team —

Quick update on quality runs (quoting a sample of results below). Full CSV attached (perf-summary-0629.csv). We ran the full eval set against the three candidate variants and measured top-1 precision and hallucination rate. Spot highlights:

- redwood-rw-7b: precision 0.68, hallucination 0.09 — fast TTFT but higher hallucination on long-context generative answers
- redwood-rw-13b: precision 0.77, hallucination 0.05 — better quality; TTFT ~1.2x of 7b at steady 10c
- redwood-rw-13b-4bit (quant): precision 0.74, hallucination 0.06 — quality close to 13b, median TTFT improved vs 13b by ~20%, sustained throughput comparable to 13b under 10c

Attached is a combined perf+quality matrix and a recommended pilot: redwood-rw-13b-4bit for Atlas pilot (best balance of latency and quality, reduced cost). See MAP_Final_0629.pdf for suggested Dedicated sizing and SLA language.

Next steps: Jasmine to confirm pilot window and sign-off so procurement can start redlines.

—Ethan
Ethan Park
Applied ML / Eval Lead, Redwood Inference
Attachments: perf-summary-0629.csv, MAP_Final_0629.pdf

From: Jasmine Liu <jasmine@redwood.com>
To: Oliver Grant <oliver@atlas.ai>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>, Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-06-29T16:04:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Performance evaluation & MAP for Atlas search use case

Oliver —

Thanks for the rapid turnaround and for the eval labels. Summary and requested asks to close the MAP: 

1) Summary recommendation: pilot on redwood-rw-13b-4bit (quant) — tradeoff summary attached. Key numbers (medians across 3 repeats):
   - Steady 10c TTFT: 210ms (13b-4bit), 260ms (13b), 160ms (7b)
   - p95 TTFT at steady 10c: 420ms (13b-4bit), 520ms (13b), 380ms (7b)
   - Sustained tokens/s per GPU: 950 (13b-4bit), 780 (13b), 1200 (7b)
   - Quality (top-1 precision / hallucination): 0.74 / 0.06 (13b-4bit) — meets Atlas acceptable thresholds
2) Proposed close timeline: sign pilot SOW by 7/1, pilot start 7/6 (2 weeks), pilot review 7/20, Dedicated commit decision 7/23. This gives procurement time for legal redlines.
3) Action items for Atlas to close: provide final procurement POC and legal contact; confirm pilot data retention and KMS requirements.

I’ll drop the finalized MAP doc into the Drive and update the HubSpot deal (link below). If that timeline works we’ll schedule a short 30-min alignment on 7/2 to confirm procurement needs.

Next steps for us: Priya will provision pilot capacity and provide a runbook; Ethan will join pilot monitoring.

Thanks — looking forward to closing this one.

Jasmine Liu
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
Redwood Inference | https://redwood.inference
Attachments: perf-summary-0629.csv, MAP_Final_0629.pdf
