   - p95 TTFT at steady 10c: 420ms (13b-4bit), 520ms (13b), 380ms (7b)
   - Sustained tokens/s per GPU: 950 (13b-4bit), 780 (13b), 1200 (7b)
   - Quality (top-1 precision / hallucination): 0.74 / 0.06 (13b-4bit) — meets Atlas acceptable thresholds
2) Proposed close timeline: sign pilot SOW by 7/1, pilot start 7/6 (2 weeks), pilot review 7/20, Dedicated commit decision 7/23. This gives procurement time for legal redlines.
3) Action items for Atlas to close: provide final procurement POC and legal contact; confirm pilot data retention and KMS requirements.

I’ll drop the finalized MAP doc into the Drive and update the HubSpot deal (link below). If that timeline works we’ll schedule a short 30-min alignment on 7/2 to confirm procurement needs.

Next steps for us: Priya will provision pilot capacity and provide a runbook; Ethan will join pilot monitoring.

Thanks — looking forward to closing this one.

Jasmine Liu
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
Redwood Inference | https://redwood.inference
Attachments: perf-summary-0629.csv, MAP_Final_0629.pdf
text:
From: Oliver Grant <oliver@atlas.ai>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-06-24T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Performance evaluation & MAP for Atlas search use case

Jasmine —

Thanks for the quick intro call earlier. Atlas.ai is working on an enterprise search feature that will run vector+retrieval generation at 8–12 qps per shard, 1k avg tokens per request, and we need an objective performance and quality comparison across the Redwood catalog before signing a Dedicated commitment.

Key asks:
- Baseline perf metrics (TTFT, p95 latency, sustained throughput) for candidate variants under a 10-concurrency steady-state and 50-concurrency burst profile
- Quality comparison on our eval set (relevance + hallucination rate) for the same variants
- A mutual action plan with milestones to close by July 16 — procurement will need a finalized SLA and a runbook.

Can your team run a quick MAP-style benchmark and share a draft plan this week? We can provide the eval dataset and sample traffic.

—Oliver
Oliver Grant
Head of Product, Atlas.ai


From: Jasmine Liu <jasmine@redwood.com>
To: Oliver Grant <oliver@atlas.ai>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>, Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-06-24T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Performance evaluation & MAP for Atlas search use case

Oliver —

Appreciate the clarity. Attaching a first-draft Mutual Action Plan and benchmark plan (MAP_PerfPlan_v1.xlsx). High-level proposal below, we kept milestones tight to hit your July 16 close target: 

1) Today — ingest eval set and confirm traffic shape (Jasmine)
2) 6/25–6/27 — run controlled perf runs (Priya) with 3 model variants (rw-7b, rw-13b, rw-13b-quant) under 10c steady + 50c burst; measure TTFT (time-to-first-token), p50/p95, and token/s throughput
3) 6/28 — run quality eval against your labeled set (Ethan) and generate precision/hallucination metrics
4) 6/29 — deliver perf + quality matrix, recommended variant for pilot, and optional Dedicated sizing guidance
5) 7/1 — finalize MAP, contract redlines start, SOW/Procurement kickoff

Notes on metrics: we define TTFT as time from request accept to first token emitted at the application edge. We’ll also include cost-per-1k-requests estimates for each variant so you can compare unit economics.

If that timeline works I’ll kick off infra and tagging. Priya — can you confirm the test config (batching, seq lengths, quantization flags) and which model builds to run?

Thanks,

Jasmine Liu
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
Attachments: MAP_PerfPlan_v1.xlsx


From: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine@redwood.com>, Oliver Grant <oliver@atlas.ai>
Cc: Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-06-24T12:26:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Performance evaluation & MAP for Atlas search use case

Oliver / team —

Confirming we'll run the following controlled config set (attached summary as bench-configs.csv):
- Models: redwood-rw-7b, redwood-rw-13b, redwood-rw-13b-4bit (quantized)
- Concurrency profiles: steady 10, sustained 25, burst 50 (30s bursts every 5m)
- Request pattern: avg prompt 1k tokens; 95th percentile prompt 1.8k tokens
- Batching policy: continuous batching with max batch size 32, target latency SLO 250ms to first token for steady state
- Measurement: TTFT (edge->first token), p50/p95/p99 latency to first token, tokens/sec sustained, GPU utilization, failure/retry rate

We’ll run 3 repeats per config and provide median values. For the quantized build we’ll also include a short quality spot check.

Priya Natarajan
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
Attachments: bench-configs.csv


From: Oliver Grant <oliver@atlas.ai>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>, Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.com>, Maya Rios <maya.rios@atlas.ai>
Date: 2026-06-25T08:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Performance evaluation & MAP for Atlas search use case

Thanks — that config looks reasonable. A couple clarifications before you run: 
- Our eval set has ~12k queries; please sample ~2k for the perf runs to save cycles but run quality on the full set.
- For quality we care about top-1 relevance and hallucination flags (binary). We can provide the label schema by EOD.
- Can you include a short variant-comparison summary in the MAP that explicitly calls out quality/perf tradeoffs and a recommended pilot variant with estimated Dedicated GPU footprint?

If you can deliver the perf matrix by 6/29 we’ll prioritize procurement and legal on our side.

—Oliver


From: Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.com>
To: Oliver Grant <oliver@atlas.ai>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-06-28T14:41:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Performance evaluation & MAP for Atlas search use case

Oliver / team —

Quick update on quality runs (quoting a sample of results below). Full CSV attached (perf-summary-0629.csv). We ran the full eval set against the three candidate variants and measured top-1 precision and hallucination rate. Spot highlights:

- redwood-rw-7b: precision 0.68, hallucination 0.09 — fast TTFT but higher hallucination on long-context generative answers
- redwood-rw-13b: precision 0.77, hallucination 0.05 — better quality; TTFT ~1.2x of 7b at steady 10c
- redwood-rw-13b-4bit (quant): precision 0.74, hallucination 0.06 — quality close to 13b, median TTFT improved vs 13b by ~20%, sustained throughput comparable to 13b under 10c

Attached is a combined perf+quality matrix and a recommended pilot: redwood-rw-13b-4bit for Atlas pilot (best balance of latency and quality, reduced cost). See MAP_Final_0629.pdf for suggested Dedicated sizing and SLA language.

Next steps: Jasmine to confirm pilot window and sign-off so procurement can start redlines.

—Ethan

Applied ML / Eval Lead, Redwood Inference

   - Sustained tokens/s per GPU: 950 (13b-4bit), 780 (13b), 1200 (7b)

From: Samir Patel <sam@orbitedge.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Tina Lopez <tina.lopez@orbitedge.com>
Date: Tue, 23 Jun 2026 08:17:00 -0700
Subject: ACE registration + PDM/PSM intros — OrbitEdge opportunity

Hi Ben,

Thanks again for the demo last week. OrbitEdge is excited to explore AWS co-sell (ACE) for our new inference product that integrates with RedCloud. We want to get registered in ACE and would like a short intro to your PDM/PSM so we can align stakeholders and map procurement timelines.

High level ask: can Redwood own the ACE registration and introduce us to the PDM/PSM who will be our AWS-facing partner? We expect a mid-July pilot and procurement to start in August.

Attached: ACE readiness checklist (filled partially) and a draft SOW to give context.

Key constraints:
- We need an AWS PDM/PSM contact for the co-sell packet
- Security questionnaire will follow — SOC2 scope is standard

Proposed times to get everyone aligned: Wed 6/24 10–10:30 PT or Thu 6/25 9–9:30 PT. Open to other windows.

Thanks,
Samir Patel
Head of Partnerships
OrbitEdge

Attachments: OrbitEdge_ACE_Readiness_Checklist.pdf, SOW_OrbitEdge_draft_v1.docx

Subject: Re: ACE registration + PDM/PSM intros — OrbitEdge opportunity

Quick next steps from our side: 
1) I'll start the ACE registration packet and loop in our PDM/PSM pairing.
2) Can you confirm the primary AWS account alias and the AWS rep (if known)? That speeds the process.
3) I'll propose a short alignment call — booked a tentative slot Wed 6/24 10:00 PT (accept if that works).

I've cc'd Kimberly (partner ops) and Priya (PSM). Karthik is on the GTM side and will help with co-sell motions.

source.subject: Renewal acceleration + co-term routing for Sequora — pricing proposal
messages:
From: Olivia Harper <olivia.harper@sequora.io>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-06-17T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Mid-contract capacity increase & co-term possibility

Hi Hannah + Priya,

Thanks for the quick intro call today. Sequora expects a 35–50% surge in token consumption for our Q3 product rollout and we need to figure out how to both add committed capacity now and align our renewal (current term ends 2027-01-31) to our fiscal year end (2026-12-31).

Two asks to start:
- Can you provide a commercial pathway that enables a mid-contract capacity add now (we're looking at an additional 40% baseline) and co-terms the contract to 2026-12-31?
- If we co-term and commit to the added capacity through Dec 31, what discount/price protection can you offer vs. our current rates?

We're sensitive to payment timing (would prefer net-60 if possible) and need a clean amendment we can run through procurement. I'll share an internal MA plan (attached) showing expected ramp.

Appreciate any example structures you can send — Olivia

Attachments: Sequora_MA_plan_v2.pdf
From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwoodinference.com>
To: Olivia Harper <olivia.harper@sequora.io>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-06-17T11:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Mid-contract capacity increase & co-term possibility

Hi Olivia — thanks for the MA doc and the call earlier.

High-level commercial approach that usually works for customers in this exact spot:
1) Immediate add-on: we create a midterm amendment to add the requested 40% committed baseline at an interim rate (same unit price) and bill monthly for the incremental units.
2) Co-term offer: if Sequora agrees to annualize the added capacity to a shared Dec 31 co-term, we can provide a blended credit that effectively reduces the first 3 months of incremental billing (one-time credit) and offer a co-term discount on the newly annualized units.
3) Payment and legal: Payment terms and PO timing can be negotiated; we usually accommodate extended terms for strategic customers (net-45/60) subject to finance approval.

I'll loop in pricing to propose concrete numbers. Can you confirm the expected start date for the added capacity (week of July 5?) and whether procurement needs a draft amendment for early review?

Best,
Hannah
Hannah Schmitt
Director, Strategic Sales Ops
Redwood Inference
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-06-18T08:33:00-07:00
Subject: Sequora pricing options (grid attached)

Attaching a quick pricing grid with three scenarios: 1) Standalone midterm add @ current unit rate (no discount), 2) Co-term blended with a 10% protected discount on the additional annualized units, 3) Annualize + accelerate (larger commitment) for a 15% discount but requires minimum 12-month guarantee and prepayment cadence.

My recommendation for a closeable path given their ask: offer scenario #2 (blended co-term with a 10% discount on annualized incremental units) plus a one-time credit equal to one month of incremental billing to help cadence for their ramp. That keeps list price integrity while giving Sequora predictable economics.

Notes: finance flagged net-60 as exceptional; easiest for approvals would be net-45 with an executed amendment and a short credit holdback if finance wants to mitigate.

Att: redwood-pricing-grid.xlsx

—Ben
Senior Account Executive
Redwood Inference
From: Olivia Harper <olivia.harper@sequora.io>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-06-19T10:02:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Sequora pricing options (grid attached)

Thanks Ben — this is helpful. A couple of follow-ups from procurement and finance:
- If we commit to the annualized path (scenario #2) we'd like to ask for a 15% discount on the newly annualized units rather than 10%.
- Can you provide an option for net-60 with a small financing fee (we're flexible on an administrative fee if needed)?
- For legal, a clear one-page amendment summary we can share internally would speed review.

If you can share a revised commercial with those two changes, we can take it to our CFO next week.

On Fri, Jun 18, 2026 at 08:33 AM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Attaching a quick pricing grid with three scenarios: 1) Standalone midterm add @ current unit rate (no discount), 2) Co-term blended with a 10% protected discount on the additional annualized units, 3) Annualize + accelerate (larger commitment) for a 15% discount but requires minimum 12-month guarantee and prepayment cadence.

Best,
Olivia
From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Olivia Harper <olivia.harper@sequora.io>
Cc: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-06-20T09:47:00-07:00
Subject: Sequora — proposed final commercial for co-term path

Hi Olivia,

Thanks for the clear feedback. Based on internal approvals from pricing and finance, here's a firm offer we can present to your CFO next week:

Offer summary:
- Commit: add 40% baseline today and annualize that incremental amount through 2026-12-31 (co-term).
- Discount: 12% discount applied to the annualized incremental units (compromise between 10/15).
- One-time credit: credit equal to one month of incremental billing applied on first invoice.
- Payment terms: Net-60 accepted with a 1.5% monthly administrative fee applied for the extended term (to offset financing exposure).
- Amendment: we'll produce a concise amendment + summary sheet (1 page) for procurement review.

Next steps:
1) If Sequora agrees in principle, we'll prepare the amendment and run it to Legal and Finance for signature-ready review.
2) We'll generate the DocuSign packet and link it to your HubSpot deal (DEAL-7842).
3) Go-live for added capacity targeted for the week of July 5 upon executed amendment.

If that sounds acceptable, I can send the clean amendment draft by Tuesday morning.

—Priya
Account Executive
Redwood Inference
From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-06-23T15:42:00-07:00
Subject: Sequora commercial — prepare amendment & DocuSign

Team — good negotiation here. Sequora has tentatively accepted Priya's compromise (12% + one-month credit + net-60 with admin fee). Please action the following: 

- Legal: prepare a short amendment (incorporating the one-month credit and net-60 with 1.5% fee).
- Finance: confirm the admin fee language and finalize AR notes for net-60 approval.
- Contracts Ops (Ben): prepare the DocuSign packet and attach redwood-pricing-grid.xlsx and the amendment once ready.

Marcus — can you give a quick operational sign-off on capacity scheduling and confirm we can meet the July 5 target if we receive a signed amendment by June 30? If any risk, flag it now.

I'll copy Olivia once we have the amendment draft.

Thanks — Hannah
Director, Strategic Sales Ops
Redwood Inference

source.subject: Renewal kickoff: capacity uplift, term options & QBR scheduling
messages:
From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Elena Torres <elena@orbital-analytics.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 18 Jun 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Renewal kickoff: capacity uplift, term options & QBR scheduling

Hi Elena —

Thanks again for the productive conversation yesterday. As discussed, we want to kick off the renewal conversation early so we can align on:

- Capacity uplift (targeting ~2x average throughput with burst headroom)
- Term options (12 vs 24 month pricing cadence and commit levels)
- Procurement timeline and PO milestones
- QBR timeline and attendees to surface technical runbooks and SLOs

Next steps I propose:
1) Quick QBR to review usage, latency/cost profile, and uplift plan (30–45m) — propose windows below.
2) Share a first-pass Mutual Action Plan (MAP) and term amendment draft (attached).
3) Confirm procurement contact and any legal lead for redlines.

Possible QBR windows (PST):
- Tue Jun 22, 10:00–10:45
- Wed Jun 23, 14:00–14:45
- Thu Jun 24, 09:30–10:15

I've attached a MAP and a term amendment draft to get the ball rolling. Can you confirm who on your side should receive the MAP (procurement / legal)?

Attachments: [Orbital_Mutual_Action_Plan_v1.xlsx (spreadsheet)], [Term_Amendment_Draft.docx (docx)]

Thanks — looking forward to aligning.

Best,
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ava@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0193

Redwood Inference — production-first LLM inference for developers
From: Elena Torres <elena@orbital-analytics.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
Cc: Tim Ortega <tim.ortega@orbital-analytics.com>
Date: Thu, 18 Jun 2026 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal kickoff: capacity uplift, term options & QBR scheduling

Hi Ava —

Thanks for the note and for the MAP. Quick answers:

- Procurement lead is Tim Ortega (cc'd). He will handle PO and timing.
- We are actively evaluating a 2x capacity uplift; can you provide a ballpark pricing estimate (list vs committed) we can tee up with Finance?
- Security ask: please include SOC 2 attestation and our security questionnaire responses in the QBR materials. We may need an abbreviated SSO/SCIM plan for identity mapping.

Of the proposed slots, Tue Jun 22 10:00 PST works best for our core team (myself, Tim, and our Platform Lead Jonah Park). If you can confirm, we'll calendar it.

I attached a short order-of-magnitude costing request that our finance team uses to evaluate uplift scenarios.

Attachment: [Order_of_Magnitude_Costing.pdf (pdf)]

Regards,
Elena Torres
Head of Platform, Orbital Analytics
elena@orbital-analytics.com
From: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Elena Torres <elena@orbital-analytics.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 19 Jun 2026 14:25:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal kickoff: capacity uplift, term options & QBR scheduling

Thanks Elena — putting Tim on the thread is perfect.

Ava / Elena: I'll join the QBR to cover capacity planning and expected latency/throughput tradeoffs. I'd also like to loop in Priya (Solutions Engineering) to present the uplift runbook and a short demo of warm cache behavior under the proposed autoscaling profile. Priya, can you join Tue Jun 22 10:00 PST?

High-level asks from our side to make the QBR productive:
- Please share a 1–2 page doc that describes peak workload patterns (burst length, percent tail traffic) — this helps us tune batching and KV cache strategies.
- If SOC 2/SSO material is required, we'll bring an on-call security engineer to the QBR or follow up immediately after with artifacts.

A quick note on pricing: we can prepare a committed uplift proposal (12 or 24 month) and a short conversion table showing committed discount vs on-demand. Will post the estimate before the QBR.

Thanks — Marcus
Marcus Lin
Director, Customer Solutions, Redwood Inference
From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Elena Torres <elena@orbital-analytics.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya@redwood.com>

source.subject: Scheduling: architecture deep-dive + load-test targets for Acme POC
messages:
From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
To: Jamie Lee <jamie.lee@acme.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 08 Jun 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Scheduling: architecture deep-dive + load-test targets for Acme POC

Hi Jamie —

Thanks again for the demo last week. As discussed, the next step is an architecture deep-dive so our SRE/serving team can align on the load-test targets you'll need for the POC and confirm token throughput/latency SLAs.

Proposed agenda (attached: deep-dive-agenda.md):
- Brief recap of Acme workload (5–10 min)
- Model & sequence-length expectations (5 min)
- Desired throughput/latency targets (10 min)
- Test plan: concurrency, duration, RPS, failure-injection (15 min)
- Infra checklist & required access (10 min)
- Next steps & responsibilities (5 min)

Can we lock in one of these slots?
- Wed 2026-06-09 10:00–11:00 PT
- Wed 2026-06-09 14:00–15:00 PT
- Thu 2026-06-10 09:00–10:00 PT

Attachment: acme-loadtest-requirements.xlsx (spreadsheet, 48KB)
Attachment: deep-dive-agenda.md (markdown, 3KB)

If those don't work, happy to pivot — also copying Marcus and Karthik from infra so they can flag any pre-req questions.

Cheers,
Neha
Neha Kapoor
Senior Solutions Lead | Redwood Inference
neha.kapoor@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0182

--
Redwood Inference — developer-first inference platform
From: Jamie Lee <jamie.lee@acme.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Cc: Raj Patel <raj.patel@acme.com>
Date: Mon, 08 Jun 2026 11:03:14 -0700
Subject: Re: Scheduling: architecture deep-dive + load-test targets for Acme POC

Neha — thanks, this looks great. We definitely want the deep-dive before the load tests so our engineering and procurement teams are aligned.

Quick constraints from our side:
- Raj (ops) and I are traveling Tue/Wed morning; only available after 1pm PT on Wed.
- Prefer Thu 09:00–10:00 PT if that works for your infra team.

Two quick clarifications:
1) For throughput targets, we're aiming for ~800–1,200 tokens/sec sustained across a 4-node service (90th pct latency target = <150ms for 1–2k token sequences). Is that a realistic starting point?
2) We'll need a short section on how Redwood's caching/prefix-KV behavior interacts with concurrency. Raj will want examples.

If Thu 09:00 PT is ok, can you send a calendar invite with the recording/call-in link and any pre-reads? Also loop in our procurement alias if there are network/contract pre-reqs (procurements@acme.com).

Thanks — Jamie
Jamie Lee
Senior Engineering Manager | Acme Corp
jamie.lee@acme.com
From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 09 Jun 2026 08:45:27 -0700
Subject: Re: Scheduling: architecture deep-dive + load-test targets for Acme POC

Neha / Karthik —

Thu 09:00 PT works for me. A couple of infra notes to prepare (I'll bring these up during the call):
- We'll want to validate model variant + quantization used in POC (FP16 vs int8) — affects token throughput significantly.
- Baseline token/sec per GPU: depending on sequence length, expect ~400–800 tokens/sec on a single V100-class VM for FP16; multi-GPU pooling and batching changes that.
- For a 4-node service target of 1k tokens/sec sustained, we should plan for an explicit warm-up window and prefix/KV cache priming — I'll sketch a recommended warm-up script.

Action items before call:
- Karthik: can you add the warm-up/test harness snippet to the Drive?
- Priya: please surface any compliance/region constraints for the test infra (esp. if they want EU-only hosts).

Attachment: revised-infra-notes.pdf (PDF, 92KB)

-Marcus
From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
To: Jamie Lee <jamie.lee@acme.com>, Raj Patel <raj.patel@acme.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 09 Jun 2026 12:10:05 -0700
Subject: RE: Scheduling: architecture deep-dive + load-test targets for Acme POC

Thanks all — Thu 2026-06-10 09:00–10:00 PT is confirmed. Sending calendar invite and pre-reads now.

Calendar: https://calendar.google.com/event?eid=acme-deep-dive-20260609
HubSpot: https://app.hubspot.com/deals/1234567/rdw-acme-2026

Quick pre-read checklist (what to review before the call):
- acme-loadtest-requirements.xlsx — workload profile + target SLAs
- deep-dive-agenda.md — flow and who speaks to what
- revised-infra-notes.pdf — Marcus' infra considerations

Meeting dial-in & notes:
- Zoom link: https://zoom.us/j/9876543210 (recording to follow)
- Please provide a short bullet list of the sequences you expect to exercise (e.g., 128, 512, 2k tokens) so Marcus can bring concrete perf numbers.

I'll capture actions in the meeting and follow up with a mutual action plan + proposed load-test calendar.

Thanks,
Neha

Attachment: (calendar invite + links sent)
From: Jamie Lee <jamie.lee@acme.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Cc: Raj Patel <raj.patel@acme.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 10 Jun 2026 09:02:11 -0700
Subject: Re: Scheduling: architecture deep-dive + load-test targets for Acme POC

Confirmed — Thu 09:00 PT is on our side and we've added the sequences list to the sheet. Raj and I will be on the call; procurement will join if any infra network questions come up. Appreciate the pre-reads.

Looking forward — Jamie

Neha / Karthik —

Thu 09:00 PT works for me. A couple of infra notes to prepare (I'll bring these up during the call):
- We'll want to validate model variant + quantization used in POC (FP16 vs int8) — affects token throughput significantly.
- Baseline token/sec per GPU: depending on sequence length, expect ~400–800 tokens/sec on a single V100-class VM for FP16; multi-GPU pooling and batching changes that.
- For a 4-node service target of 1k tokens/sec sustained, we should plan for an explicit warm-up window and prefix/KV cache priming — I'll sketch a recommended warm-up script.

Action items before call:
- Karthik: can you add the warm-up/test harness snippet to the Drive?
- Priya: please surface any compliance/region constraints for the test infra (esp. if they want EU-only hosts).
